- Prefer simultaneous input, spotlight rotation, escalating round multipliers, and no elimination; dead air and single-actor phases are the top pacing killers.
- LLMs are provably unfunny as joke-tellers: 90% of ChatGPT's jokes were 25 memorized templates (arxiv.org/abs/2306.04563), and pun recognition drops below chance on novel material (EMNLP 2025).
- Death by AI's HN postmortem: prompt-injectable, arbitrary, repetitive AI judging killed novelty fast; fun survived only via group dynamics — so use AI to set up humans to be funny about each other, never as the joke-teller or final judge (news.ycombinator.com/item?id=38318889).

content.content:
1	# research:browser-vs-app
2	
3	RECOMMENDATION: Joiners connect via mobile BROWSER (QR + room code). Keep the native Kotlin app for the HOST only. Do not build a joiner app. Use Cloudflare Workers + Durable Objects (free tier) as the room relay for v1; treat host-phone-as-local-server as an optional offline mode later, not the front door.
4	
5	1) Mobile browser realities (2026) — GOOD ENOUGH, with known traps
6	- WebSockets work reliably in-foreground on iOS Safari and Chrome Android. The trap is backgrounding: iOS kills WS/timers within ~30-60s of the tab backgrounding or the screen locking; you must build auto-reconnect + state resync on visibilitychange (https://developer.apple.com/forums/thread/66157, https://gist.github.com/mloughran/2052006).
7	- Screen Wake Lock API is now universal: Safari 16.4+, and the bug that broke it in installed PWAs was fixed in iOS/iPadOS 18.4; global support >94% (https://web.dev/blog/screen-wake-lock-supported-in-all-browsers, https://caniuse.com/wake-lock, https://webkit.org/blog/16574/webkit-features-in-safari-18-4/). Wake lock + reconnect logic together neutralize most mid-game drop problems. NOTE: wake lock requires a secure context (HTTPS) — this matters for the local-server option below.
8	- Vibration API: still NOT supported on iOS Safari (Chrome Android yes) — design haptics as optional garnish (https://caniuse.com/mdn-api_navigator_vibrate). Audio needs a user gesture first (standard unlock-on-tap pattern).
9	- QR join: native camera QR scanning on both platforms makes qr→URL-with-room-code the lowest-friction flow; Jackbox itself uses 4-letter codes at a fixed URL and added QR join (https://support.jackboxgames.com/hc/en-us/articles/15794759479959-How-do-I-join-a-game).
10	- New risk to track: Chrome 142+ (Oct 2025) ships a Local Network Access permission prompt, and Google plans to extend it to WebSockets/WebRTC — this punishes "public page talks to local IP" architectures, not cloud relays (https://developer.chrome.com/blog/local-network-access).
11	
12	2) $0 backend — Cloudflare DO wins decisively
13	- Cloudflare Durable Objects are on the Workers FREE plan since April 2025 (https://developers.cloudflare.com/changelog/post/2025-04-07-durable-objects-free-tier/). Free tier: 100k requests/day, 13k GB-s duration/day, 5 GB SQLite storage; incoming WS messages billed 20:1 (so ~2M incoming messages/day free); outgoing messages free; WebSocket Hibernation zeroes duration billing for idle rooms (https://developers.cloudflare.com/durable-objects/platform/pricing/). One DO per room is exactly the Jackbox-room pattern. Cliff: hard daily cap — operations fail (not bill) past it; at 8-player-room scale you'd need thousands of concurrent rooms to hit it.
14	- Firebase RTDB Spark: 100 simultaneous connections = ~12 rooms total concurrently — a real cliff (https://firebase.google.com/docs/database/usage/limits). Firestore realtime is poll-ish and write-metered. Weaker fit.
15	- Supabase Realtime free: 200 peak concurrent connections, 2M messages/month — the monthly message cap is the cliff for chatty game traffic (https://supabase.com/docs/guides/realtime/limits).
16	- PartyKit: acquired by Cloudflare (Apr 2024); now free platform fee, deploys to YOUR Cloudflare account — i.e., it's a nice DX layer over the same DO free tier (https://blog.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-acquires-partykit/, https://docs.partykit.io/guides/deploy-to-cloudflare/). Liveblocks free tier is MAU-capped and aimed at collab docs, overkill/wrong shape.
17	
18	3) Local-only (host phone serves the game) — viable on Android-to-Android, hostile with iPhones
19	- Technically easy: Ktor/NanoHTTPD embedded HTTP+WS server in the Kotlin app, joiners hit http://192.168.x.x:8080 on the host's hotspot (https://blog.azzahid.com/posts/android-ktor-http-server/, https://github.com/lopspower/AndroidWebServer). Zero cloud cost pairs perfectly with on-device llama.cpp.
20	- But: (a) an internet-less hotspot triggers "no internet" heuristics — Android clients may auto-fall-back to mobile data and drop the socket; iPhones hit captive-portal purgatory (https://discussions.apple.com/thread/250330973, https://www.spotipo.com/post/troubleshooting-captive-portals-common-issues); (b) plain-HTTP page = no secure context = no Wake Lock API (NoSleep.js video hack needed); (c) Chrome's LNA restrictions are moving toward WS enforcement; (d) Google Nearby Connections on iOS supports only Wi-Fi LAN and there's no browser story at all (https://developers.google.com/nearby/overview). Verdict: ship as "offline mode" v2 for Android-heavy groups; don't make it the default join path.
21	
22	4) What Jackbox-likes chose: Jackbox, AirConsole, Kahoot all chose browser controllers — host runs the paid app/console, players join free at a URL with a room code, over cloud WebSockets (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Jackbox_Party_Pack). Friction data says they're right: 78% of users won't install an app for a one-off; App Clips-style instant experiences convert 35-50% better; one chain saw 72% vs 34% completion instant-web vs forced-install (https://www.mobiloud.com/blog/what-are-app-clips). Forcing 7 friends to install an app kills the party before it starts.
23	
24	5) Store policy: No issue. Google Play explicitly allows consumption-only access and content "paid for somewhere else"; only the HOST's purchase (in the Android app) must use Play Billing — free web joiners are outside Play's jurisdiction entirely (https://support.google.com/googleplay/android-developer/answer/10281818). Apple is irrelevant until/unless an iOS host app exists; web joiners never touch the App Store.
25	
26	MATRIX (build order): v1 = Kotlin host app + HTTPS web controller (plain JS, QR + 4-letter code) + one Durable Object per room on CF free tier. v1.1 = PWA install prompt for repeat players (optional). v2 = offline hotspot mode (Ktor in-app server) gated to "everyone on Android / tech-savvy" groups. Never = joiner native app.
27	
28	## KEY FACTS
29	- Recommendation: joiners via mobile browser (QR + room code), native app stays host-only; never build a joiner app.
30	- Cloudflare Durable Objects are on the Workers free plan (since Apr 2025): 100k requests/day, 13k GB-s/day, WS messages counted 20:1 (~2M incoming msgs/day free), outgoing free, and WebSocket Hibernation stops duration billing for idle rooms — easily hosts thousands of 8-player rooms at $0.
31	- PartyKit is now Cloudflare-owned with zero platform fee when deployed to your own CF account, making it a free DX layer over the same Durable Objects tier.
32	- Free-tier cliffs elsewhere: Firebase RTDB Spark = 100 concurrent connections (~12 rooms total); Supabase Realtime free = 200 peak connections but only 2M messages/month.
33	- Screen Wake Lock API now works everywhere including iOS Safari 16.4+ (PWA bug fixed in iOS 18.4), but it requires HTTPS/secure context — which a plain-HTTP local hotspot server cannot provide.
34	- iOS Safari kills WebSockets ~30-60s after backgrounding/screen-lock, so the web controller needs wake lock + auto-reconnect/resync on visibilitychange; vibration API is still unsupported on iOS Safari.
35	- Local-only host-phone serving (Ktor/NanoHTTPD over Android hotspot) is technically easy and pairs with on-device llama.cpp, but internet-less hotspots trigger Android clients' auto-switch to mobile data and iPhone captive-portal purgatory — ship it only as a v2 offline mode.
36	- Google Nearby Connections supports only Wi-Fi LAN on iOS and has no browser story, ruling it out for cross-platform joiners.
37	- Chrome 142+ (Oct 2025) adds a Local Network Access permission prompt, with WS/WebRTC enforcement planned — a headwind for local-IP architectures, irrelevant to cloud relays.
38	- Join-friction data: 78% of users won't install an app for a one-off; instant web experiences convert 35-50% better (72% vs 34% completion in one instant-vs-forced-install case) — Jackbox, AirConsole, and Kahoot all chose browser controllers.
39	- Google Play policy explicitly permits 'host pays in app (via Play Billing), friends join free on the web' — consumption-only/companion-web access is allowed; Apple is irrelevant unless an iOS host app ships.

content.content:
1	# research:pricing-model
2	
3	PRICING RESEARCH: "one host pays, everyone plays" mobile party game (2026)
4	
5	1) PRECEDENTS & PRICE POINTS
6	- Jackbox: Party Packs are $24.99–$29.99 on Apple TV/iPad (Pack 8 = $29.99; Pack 1 = $24.99) (https://apps.apple.com/us/app/the-jackbox-party-pack/id971879034, https://www.jackboxgames.com/blog/the-jackbox-party-pack-8-is-now-available-for-apple-tv-ipad-and-amazon-fire-tv). Standalones (Quiplash, Drawful 2) run under $10 (https://www.howtogeek.com/free-party-games-you-can-play-with-just-your-phone/). Naughty Pack — Jackbox's first M-rated pack and the closest taste-comp to HELLDECK — is $19.99 (https://gg.deals/game/the-jackbox-naughty-pack/). The model: one purchase, up to 8 phone players + 10,000 audience, all joiners free.
7	- Heads Up! (Ellen): $0.99–$1.99 paid app + $0.99 deck IAPs; 25.5M downloads by 2016 and top-paid for years. Crucially, Forbes documented burstiness: ~300k–1M games/day baseline vs 3M Thanksgiving, 5.5M Christmas Day (https://www.forbes.com/sites/natalierobehmed/2016/07/14/heads-up-up-and-away-how-ellen-degeneres-game-became-a-hit/). Cheap paid + content packs monetized bursty usage without churn mechanics.
8	- Evil Apples: free + ads + dual currency ($0.99–$99.99 packs); friend games free, stranger games cost currency; $9.99 unlocks all decks (https://wezift.com/parent-portal/apps/evil-apples-a-dirty-card-game/). Earns on whales but reads as grubby — off-brand for a taste-enforced product.
9	- Psych! (WB): free + ads + paid decks; reviews complain "now you have to pay for everything" — freemium deck-nickel-diming churns goodwill (https://screenwiseapp.com/media/psych-outwit-your-friends-app).
10	- AirConsole Hero: the purest "one host pays" precedent — subscription (~$59.99/yr tier shown) where ONE Hero in a session unlocks all games and removes ads for EVERYONE connected (https://www.airconsole.com/hero, https://developers.airconsole.com/airconsole-hero-and-revenue). Party Up uses weekly/monthly/yearly subs (https://apps.apple.com/us/app/party-up-friends-group-game/id1620838428).
11	- Pattern: session-based party games earn via one-time host purchases and content packs; subscriptions suffer from subscription fatigue and dormancy-driven cancellation between parties (https://segwise.ai/blog/comparing-different-monetization-models-freemium-premium-and-subscription-for-mobile-games-clxvdqq6z00074gdktfh3ust5). Freemium converts 2–5% but churns; premium fits episodic use.
12	
13	2) TRIAL/DEMO PSYCHOLOGY & VIRAL LOOP
14	Jackbox runs a free demo pack (1–100 players) on consoles (https://store.playstation.com/en-us/product/UP2139-CUSA01229_00-JBGPARTYTRIALPS4) and repeatedly gives Drawful 2 away free (2020 COVID, 2024 Megapicker launch) explicitly "so new players have something to get them started" (https://www.thegamer.com/drawful-2-free-steam-megapicker-release-jackbox-sale/). The structural viral loop: every joiner experiences the full product free at someone else's party; the only conversion needed is "I want to host this myself." A "first game night free for new hosts" replicates this in-app — the demo IS the party they already attended, so the pitch at conversion is hosting power, not gameplay.
15	
16	3) STORE RULES (2026 state)
17	- Digital unlocks/passes must normally use IAP: 30% standard, 15% via Apple Small Business Program (enroll required, first $1M) and Google's automatic 15% first-$1M tier (https://www.revenuecat.com/blog/engineering/small-business-program, https://appbot.co/blog/app-developers-apple-google-small-business-programs/).
18	- Post-Epic loopholes: Apple US apps can include external payment links, currently at 0% commission, though the Ninth Circuit (Dec 2025) said Apple may seek a "reasonable" fee on remand — Supreme Court review pending, so don't architect solely around 0% (https://www.macrumors.com/2026/04/29/epic-games-wins-reversal-app-store-fee-battle/, https://www.revenuecat.com/blog/growth/apple-anti-steering-ruling-monetization-strategy/). Google Play (since Oct 29, 2025) must allow external purchase links/alternative billing in the US; the March 2026 Epic settlement caps fees at ≤20%, with proposals of 9–20% (https://support.google.com/googleplay/android-developer/answer/15582165, https://www.neonpay.com/blog/google-plays-new-u.s.-billing-linking-policies-what-game-developers-need-to-know). Practical: sell host unlocks on web checkout (Stripe ~3%) with link-outs, keep IAP as the convenience path.
19	
20	RECOMMENDED CANDIDATE STRUCTURES
21	A. Free app + one-time "Host" unlock, $9.99 (primary). Everyone downloads/joins free; new host gets one full game night free (the Jackbox-demo loop), then pays once to host forever. Add $2.99–$4.99 themed content packs later (Heads Up!/Jackbox pack economics). Fits bursty usage — no dormancy churn, priced at the proven sub-$10 standalone point, and $9.99 is an easy "I'm the host" identity purchase. Caveat: if AI generation cost per game night is material, cap included AI-heavy modes.
22	B. Consumable Game Night Pass $3.99 + Lifetime Host $19.99 anchor. Pass = one night, unlimited players — priced under "a beer for the table," bought at the moment of party. Lifetime (Naughty Pack price point) is upsold after 2–3 passes. Best fit if per-night AI/server cost is real, since revenue scales with usage; risk is checkout friction mid-party, so passes must never expire and purchase must be <30 seconds.
23	C. Avoid pure subscription. AirConsole Hero proves one-sub-unlocks-session works mechanically, but a solo-dev party game can't feed a sub's content treadmill, and bursty players cancel between parties. Only consider a cheap annual "Season" (~$14.99/yr) later as a bundle on top of A/B, never as the sole door.
24	
25	## KEY FACTS
26	- Jackbox Party Packs sell for $24.99–$29.99 on Apple TV/iPad, standalones under $10, and the M-rated Naughty Pack (closest comp to HELLDECK's adult tone) is $19.99 — all one-purchase, joiners-free.
27	- Heads Up! proves cheap-paid + $0.99 deck IAPs monetizes bursty party usage: Forbes documented ~300k–1M games/day baseline spiking to 5.5M on Christmas Day, with no subscription.
28	- AirConsole Hero is the direct 'one host pays' precedent: a single subscriber (~$59.99/yr) unlocks all games and removes ads for everyone in the session.
29	- Freemium deck-nickel-diming (Psych!, Evil Apples $0.99–$99.99 currency) earns but churns goodwill — Psych! reviews complain about paying for everything; off-brand for a taste-enforced product.
30	- Jackbox's conversion engine is structural: free demo pack + repeated Drawful 2 giveaways turn joiners into hosts, because every joiner already played free at someone's party — replicate with 'first game night free' for new hosts.
31	- Store take rates 2026: 15% on first $1M via Apple Small Business Program (enrollment required) and Google's automatic tier; 30% standard above that.
32	- Post-Epic 2026: Apple US apps can use external payment links at currently 0% commission (fee on remand pending, SCOTUS review sought); Google Play must allow external links since Oct 29 2025, with the March 2026 Epic settlement capping fees at ≤20%.
33	- Recommendation A (primary): free app, free joiners, first game night free, then one-time $9.99 Host unlock + later $2.99–$4.99 content packs — matches proven price points and bursty usage with zero churn surface.
34	- Recommendation B (if AI cost per game night is material): $3.99 consumable Game Night Pass with $19.99 lifetime Host anchor, upsold after 2–3 passes; passes must never expire and buy in under 30 seconds.
35	- Recommendation C: avoid pure subscription — solo dev can't feed the content treadmill and bursty players cancel between parties; at most a ~$14.99/yr season bundle layered on later.
36	- Sell unlocks/passes via web checkout (~3% Stripe) using the post-Epic link-out rights, keeping IAP as the convenience path — but don't architect around 0% Apple commission since a 'reasonable fee' is being set on remand.

Freemium converts 2–5% but churns; premium fits episodic use.

2) TRIAL/DEMO PSYCHOLOGY & VIRAL LOOP
Jackbox runs a free demo pack (1–100 players) on consoles (https://store.playstation.com/en-us/product/UP2139-CUSA01229_00-JBGPARTYTRIALPS4) and repeatedly gives Drawful 2 away free (2020 COVID, 2024 Megapicker launch) explicitly "so new players have something to get them started" (https://www.thegamer.com/drawful-2-free-steam-megapicker-release-jackbox-sale/). The structural viral loop: every joiner experiences the full product free at someone else's party; the only conversion needed is "I want to host this myself." A "first game night free for new hosts" replicates this in-app — the demo IS the party they already attended, so the pitch at conversion is hosting power, not gameplay.

3) STORE RULES (2026 state)
- Digital unlocks/passes must normally use IAP: 30% standard, 15% via Apple Small Business Program (enroll required, first $1M) and Google's automatic 15% first-$1M tier (https://www.revenuecat.com/blog/engineering/small-business-program, https://appbot.co/blog/app-developers-apple-google-small-business-programs/).
- Post-Epic loopholes: Apple US apps can include external payment links, currently at 0% commission, though the Ninth Circuit (Dec 2025) said Apple may seek a "reasonable" fee on remand — Supreme Court review pending, so don't architect solely around 0% (https://www.macrumors.com/2026/04/29/epic-games-wins-reversal-app-store-fee-battle/, https://www.revenuecat.com/blog/growth/apple-anti-steering-ruling-monetization-strategy/). Google Play (since Oct 29, 2025) must allow external purchase links/alternative billing in the US; the March 2026 Epic settlement caps fees at ≤20%, with proposals of 9–20% (https://support.google.com/googleplay/android-developer/answer/15582165, https://www.neonpay.com/blog/google-plays-new-u.s.-billing-linking-policies-what-game-developers-need-to-know). Practical: sell host unlocks on web checkout (Stripe ~3%) with link-outs, keep IAP as the convenience path.

RECOMMENDED CANDIDATE STRUCTURES
A. Free app + one-time "Host" unlock, $9.99 (primary). Everyone downloads/joins free; new host gets one full game night free (the Jackbox-demo loop), then pays once to host forever. Add $2.99–$4.99 themed content packs later (Heads Up!/Jackbox pack economics). Fits bursty usage — no dormancy churn, priced at the proven sub-$10 standalone point, and $9.99 is an easy "I'm the host" identity purchase. Caveat: if AI generation cost per game night is material, cap included AI-heavy modes.
B. Consumable Game Night Pass $3.99 + Lifetime Host $19.99 anchor. Pass = one night, unlimited players — priced under "a beer for the table," bought at the moment of party. Lifetime (Naughty Pack price point) is upsold after 2–3 passes. Best fit if per-night AI/server cost is real, since revenue scales with usage; risk is checkout friction mid-party, so passes must never expire and purchase must be <30 seconds.
C. Avoid pure subscription. AirConsole Hero proves one-sub-unlocks-session works mechanically, but a solo-dev party game can't feed a sub's content treadmill, and bursty players cancel between parties. Only consider a cheap annual "Season" (~$14.99/yr) later as a bundle on top of A/B, never as the sole door.

## KEY FACTS
- Jackbox Party Packs sell for $24.99–$29.99 on Apple TV/iPad, standalones under $10, and the M-rated Naughty Pack (closest comp to HELLDECK's adult tone) is $19.99 — all one-purchase, joiners-free.
- Heads Up! proves cheap-paid + $0.99 deck IAPs monetizes bursty party usage: Forbes documented ~300k–1M games/day baseline spiking to 5.5M on Christmas Day, with no subscription.
- AirConsole Hero is the direct 'one host pays' precedent: a single subscriber (~$59.99/yr) unlocks all games and removes ads for everyone in the session.
- Freemium deck-nickel-diming (Psych!, Evil Apples $0.99–$99.99 currency) earns but churns goodwill — Psych! reviews complain about paying for everything; off-brand for a taste-enforced product.
- Jackbox's conversion engine is structural: free demo pack + repeated Drawful 2 giveaways turn joiners into hosts, because every joiner already played free at someone's party — replicate with 'first game night free' for new hosts.
- Store take rates 2026: 15% on first $1M via Apple Small Business Program (enrollment required) and Google's automatic tier; 30% standard above that.
- Post-Epic 2026: Apple US apps can use external payment links at currently 0% commission (fee on remand pending, SCOTUS review sought); Google Play must allow external links since Oct 29 2025, with the March 2026 Epic settlement capping fees at ≤20%.
- Recommendation A (primary): free app, free joiners, first game night free, then one-time $9.99 Host unlock + later $2.99–$4.99 content packs — matches proven price points and bursty usage with zero churn surface.
- Recommendation B (if AI cost per game night is material): $3.99 consumable Game Night Pass with $19.99 lifetime Host anchor, upsold after 2–3 passes; passes must never expire and buy in under 30 seconds.
- Recommendation C: avoid pure subscription — solo dev can't feed the content treadmill and bursty players cancel between parties; at most a ~$14.99/yr season bundle layered on later.
- Sell unlocks/passes via web checkout (~3% Stripe) using the post-Epic link-out rights, keeping IAP as the convenience path — but don't architect around 0% Apple commission since a 'reasonable fee' is being set on remand.

1	# research:llm-humor
2	
3	## 1. LLM humor quality by model size
4	
5	Evidence says humor is hard even for frontier models, and generic 1B-class models are far below the usable floor for creative text of any kind — but a *fine-tuned* ~7B is the smallest class with demonstrated competitive humor generation.
6	
7	- Jentzsch & Kersting, "ChatGPT is fun, but it is not funny!" (ACL WASSA 2023, https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.04563): >90% of 1,008 ChatGPT-generated jokes were the same 25 memorized jokes; joke "understanding" was shallow surface-pattern matching. Baseline: even large chat models default to recycled, safe humor.
8	- "Cards Against LLMs" (CHum 2026, https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08757, https://aclanthology.org/2026.chum-1.4/): five frontier models judged 9,894 real CAH rounds; alignment with human funniness picks was only modestly above random, models agreed with each other more than with humans, and results were partly position-bias artifacts. Even frontier models are unreliable *judges* of party-game humor.
9	- HumorRank tournament leaderboard (https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19786, live: https://humorrank-leaderboard.pages.dev/): 10,800 pairwise matches show clear stratification; a specialized fine-tuned **HumorGen-7B ranked 4th, beating open models an order of magnitude larger**. Humor quality tracks mastery of comedic mechanisms (incongruity, escalation, conciseness), not raw scale — but no 1B-class model appears anywhere near usable.
10	- Community/local-model consensus (https://www.promptquorum.com/local-llms/best-local-llms-for-creative-writing, https://localaimaster.com/blog/ai-model-size-comparison): 1B–3B models aren't even discussed as viable for creative writing; **8B is treated as the practical floor** (and only for ~500-word pieces). TinyLlama 1.1B and Qwen2.5-1.5B class models produce low-coherence output; wit requires the incongruity-resolution the class lacks.
11	- Related: small-scale human alignment can make LLMs decent humor *rankers* (https://arxiv.org/pdf/2502.20356) — useful as a filter, not an author.
12	
13	**Verdict:** generic 1B on-device = fundamentally below the bar for comedy. Smallest usable: fine-tuned ~7B for generation; frontier cloud models for anything unfiltered-quality.
14	
15	## 2. What shipped games actually do
16	
17	- **Cards Against Humanity**: 100% hand-written by a collaborative writers' room — cards live on a shared spreadsheet, nobody owns a card ("We all wrote this card!" chant). White cards are written by picking a black card and riffing fills; every card is stress-tested for "how could this be played hurtfully in the worst hands." Best jokes survive a playtesting + editorial funnel. (https://www.elitedaily.com/p/the-head-writers-for-cards-against-humanity-just-want-to-make-you-laugh-13027874, https://submissions.cardsagainsthumanity.com/). Their 2019 Black Friday AI Challenge (https://www.cardsagainsthumanityaichallenge.com/) fine-tuned GPT-2 on ~44,000 CAH cards, then human-filtered the output — and the human-written pack still outsold the AI pack (by 2%). Exact per-card kill rates aren't published, but heavy filtering is explicit in both processes.
18	- **Jackbox**: hired comedy writers steeped in Chicago improv; lead writer + whole editorial department per game; prompts are designed to "set players up to be funny" (players are the comedians, not the game); several-times-a-week internal playtests over Discord plus large-group tests (8 players + 10–50 audience) checking that prompts "consistently generate laughter" (https://www.jackboxgames.com/game-type/writing, https://www.jackboxgames.com/blog/behind-the-scenes-of-roomerang-dev-diary-2, https://www.cgmagonline.com/interviews/the-jackbox-party-pack-11/). No published Jackbox use of generative AI for shipped card content.
19	- **Cloud-LLM-native**: Little Umbrella's **Death by AI** — 20M+ players in 3 months, built in 10 weeks, cloud orchestration layer over OpenAI/Inworld/ElevenLabs custom models; the LLM *reacts to player-written input* (judging death-escape plans) rather than authoring joke cards (https://techcrunch.com/2025/01/30/little-umbrellas-next-jackbox-style-game-pits-you-and-your-friends-against-an-ai-game-show-host/). This is the one proven AI-content pattern at scale: AI as judge/host of player-generated material.
20	
21	## 3. Hybrid: LLM-as-personalizer vs LLM-as-author
22	
23	Direct A/B evidence is thin, but every success case points the same way: **humans (players or writers) author the joke structure; AI fills, adapts, or judges.** CAH's own experiment (AI-as-author, human-filtered, lost to writers), Jackbox's design philosophy (prompts set *players* up to be funny), and Death by AI (players author, AI reacts) all support personalizer/judge roles over author roles. Mad-Libs-style slot-filling into hand-written templates is a well-established pattern (e.g. https://github.com/shlomoswidler/ai-mad-libs) and matches HELLDECK's identity ("AI as your wittiest friend"): hand-written, playtested card skeletons + LLM inserting friends' names/facts is the evidence-backed hybrid. A frontier LLM works acceptably as a *ranker/filter* of candidate fills (arXiv 2502.20356), poorly as a from-scratch comedian, and 1B on-device works for neither.
24	
25	## 4. Cloud economics (verified July 2026)
26	
27	Claude API pricing (Anthropic docs, cached 2026-06): Opus 4.8 $5/$25 per MTok in/out; Sonnet 5 $3/$15 (intro $2/$10 through 2026-08-31); Haiku 4.5 $1/$5; Batch API −50%. OpenAI July 2026 (https://www.aipricing.guru/openai-pricing/, https://benchlm.ai/openai/api-pricing): GPT-5.6 Sol $5/$30, Terra $2.50/$15, Luna $1/$6. For ~100 cards/session (~5K input + ~5K output tokens, generously ~20K/10K): **Opus 4.8 ≈ $0.15–0.35/session; Sonnet 5 ≈ $0.05–0.15; Haiku 4.5 ≈ $0.03; batch pre-generation halves it.** Per-session top-model generation is economically trivial — cents, not dollars. The binding constraints are latency/connectivity and content-quality filtering, not cost.
28	
29	## KEY FACTS
30	- Generic 1B-class models (TinyLlama 1.1B, Qwen 1.5B) are below the community-accepted floor for creative writing — local-LLM guides treat 8B as the practical minimum, and 1B-3B models aren't even discussed as viable (promptquorum.com, localaimaster.com).
31	- HumorRank (arXiv 2604.19786) shows humor generation quality stratifies by comedic-mechanism mastery, not scale alone: fine-tuned HumorGen-7B ranked 4th, beating open models 10x larger — a fine-tuned ~7B is the smallest demonstrated-competitive humor generator.
32	- Even frontier LLMs are weak at CAH-style humor judgment: 'Cards Against LLMs' (arXiv 2604.08757) found only modest above-random alignment with human funniness picks across 9,894 rounds, with position-bias artifacts.
33	- ChatGPT humor is memorized, not generated: >90% of 1,008 jokes were the same 25 jokes (Jentzsch & Kersting, arXiv 2306.04563).
34	- CAH's 2019 Black Friday AI Challenge (GPT-2 fine-tuned on ~44,000 CAH cards, human-filtered) lost to the human writers' pack — writers sold 2% more (cardsagainsthumanityaichallenge.com).
35	- CAH and Jackbox both use collaborative human writers' rooms with heavy playtesting funnels: CAH stress-tests every card for worst-case hurtful play; Jackbox playtests several times weekly plus large-group sessions (8 players + 10-50 audience) checking prompts 'consistently generate laughter'.
36	- Jackbox's core design principle: prompts set PLAYERS up to be funny — the players are the comedians, not the game content.
37	- The one proven AI-native party-game pattern at scale is AI-as-judge/host of player-written input, not AI-as-author: Little Umbrella's Death by AI hit 20M+ players in 3 months using cloud OpenAI/Inworld models (TechCrunch, Jan 2025).
38	- Evidence favors the hybrid 'LLM as personalizer/filler/judge over hand-written structures' pattern; no success case exists for small-model from-scratch joke authorship.
39	- Cloud generation of ~100 cards/session is economically trivial in 2026: ~$0.03/session on Claude Haiku 4.5 ($1/$5 per MTok), ~$0.15-0.35 on Opus 4.8 ($5/$25), ~$0.05-0.15 on Sonnet 5 ($3/$15); OpenAI GPT-5.6 tiers are comparable ($1/$6 to $5/$30); batch APIs halve costs for pre-generated decks.

- Related: small-scale human alignment can make LLMs decent humor *rankers* (https://arxiv.org/pdf/2502.20356) — useful as a filter, not an author.

**Verdict:** generic 1B on-device = fundamentally below the bar for comedy. Smallest usable: fine-tuned ~7B for generation; frontier cloud models for anything unfiltered-quality.

No published Jackbox use of generative AI for shipped card content.
- **Cloud-LLM-native**: Little Umbrella's **Death by AI** — 20M+ players in 3 months, built in 10 weeks, cloud orchestration layer over OpenAI/Inworld/ElevenLabs custom models; the LLM *reacts to player-written input* (judging death-escape plans) rather than authoring joke cards (https://techcrunch.com/2025/01/30/little-umbrellas-next-jackbox-style-game-pits-you-and-your-friends-against-an-ai-game-show-host/). This is the one proven AI-content pattern at scale: AI as judge/host of player-generated material.

## 3. Hybrid: LLM-as-personalizer vs LLM-as-author

content.content:
1	# read:infra-monetization
2	
3	## HELLDECK Infrastructure Readiness Report
4	
5	### 1. Monetization — YES, real Play Billing exists
6	Dependency: `com.android.billingclient:billing-ktx:6.0.1` (app/build.gradle:186). Implementation in `app/src/main/java/com/helldeck/billing/PurchaseManager.kt` (469 lines): singleton with two INAPP products — `helldeck_premium` ($4.99 one-time, unlocks 9 premium games) and `helldeck_tip` ($1.99 tip jar). Handles connection retry, product query, purchase flow, restorePurchases, DataStore persistence. No server-side receipt verification (client-only). `PromoCodeManager.kt` (151 lines) has XOR-obfuscated client-side promo codes. Freemium gate is live in UI: `ui/components/UpgradeModal.kt`, `PromoCodeDialog.kt`, `GamePickerSheet.kt`. 5 free games (ROAST_CONSENSUS, POISON_PITCH, FILL_IN_FINISHER, SCATTERBLAST, CONFESSION_OR_CAP); rest locked. An `internal` product flavor sets UNLOCK_ALL=true; debug builds also unlock all. Note: this is a "one person pays" model already — but per-device, not per-room.
7	
8	### 2. Networking — ZERO
9	AndroidManifest.xml declares only VIBRATE, FOREGROUND_SERVICE, BIND_DEVICE_ADMIN. **No INTERNET permission.** `usesCleartextTraffic="false"`. No okhttp/retrofit/ktor/firebase/nearby/bluetooth/play-services deps in any build.gradle. No socket/HTTP code in the 180 Kotlin files (only exception-type name checks in ErrorBoundary.kt and connectivity strings in AppUtils.kt). `telemetry/Telemetry.kt` is an explicit no-op sink. Play Billing works via Play Store IPC so it needs no INTERNET. Conclusion: the entire Jackbox-style layer (room codes, WebSockets, browser clients, backend) is greenfield — nothing to reuse, nothing to fight.
10	
11	### 3. Build/release — CI exists, Play Store NOT ready
12	`codemagic.yaml`: mac_mini_m2, triggers on push to main; runs ktlint+detekt, `testProductionReleaseUnitTest`, builds `assembleProductionRelease` APK and `bundleProductionRelease` AAB; artifacts emailed to owner. **No Play Store publishing step** (no google_play integration/track). **Release signing is commented out** (app/build.gradle:103 `// signingConfig signingConfigs.release`) so release artifacts are unsigned. versionCode 2 / versionName 1.0.1, arm64-v8a only.
13	**App size is a blocker:** `app/src/main/assets/models/` bundles qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf (1.0GB) + tinyllama-1.1b-chat-v1.0.Q4_K_M.gguf (638MB) = **1.7GB of uncompressed assets** (`noCompress 'gguf'`). Play's AAB base-module limit is ~200MB (larger only via Play Asset Delivery, not configured) — this AAB cannot be published as-is. Runtime also copies the model from assets to filesDir on first run (`ContentEngineProvider.scheduleModelPreparation`), so device footprint is ~2.7GB. A duplicate 1.7GB copy sits in repo-root `models/`. GGUFs are gitignored but `.git` is still 2.8GB.
14	
15	### 4. llama.cpp native layer — tiny, cleanly isolated, pivot-safe
16	Surface area: `CMakeLists.txt` (31 lines), `app/src/main/cpp/llama_jni.cpp` (159 lines), `llama_android_compat.h` (20 lines). CMake gracefully builds a stub .so if `third_party/llama.cpp` (99MB submodule, commit 55945d2, Oct 2025) is absent; Kotlin catches UnsatisfiedLinkError and runs "without paraphrasing". Kotlin side: `llm/LocalLLM.kt` interface (`generate`, `classifyZeroShot`), `LlamaCppLLM.kt` (158 lines), `ModelManager.kt` (48 lines), test-only `ProxyLLM.kt`. Consumers (LLMCardGenerator/V2, Augmentor, FunnyJudge, ContentEngineProvider) all program against `LocalLLM`, and the engine already degrades to gold cards + templates when LLM is null. **Verdict: a server-side `LocalLLM` implementation drops in with zero call-site changes; host-only on-device generation works as-is; deleting the layer entirely removes the cmake block, cpp dir, submodule, and 1.7GB of assets.**
17	
18	### 5. Tech stack
19	Kotlin 1.9.25, Jetpack Compose (BOM 2024.06.00, compiler ext 1.5.15, Material3), AGP 8.5.2, Gradle 8.7, Java 17, KSP, Room 2.6.1, DataStore, Navigation Compose 2.7.7. Three serialization libs (kotlinx-serialization, Gson, SnakeYAML). minSdk 21, target/compileSdk 34. Modules: `:app` + `:macrobenchmark` only. ~50.8k lines of Kotlin across 180 files. **No KMP, no iOS, no web/JS/wasm anywhere.** Single-activity portrait Compose app with kiosk-mode device-admin receiver (`admin/HelldeckDeviceAdminReceiver`).
20	
21	### 6. Abandoned/bloat a pivot can drop
22	- `Heimdall/` — 16MB git submodule (Samsung firmware flasher), referenced nowhere in build or code; it recently broke Codemagic ("Fix Codemagic submodule" commit). Delete.
23	- `loader/helldeck_loader.py` — Tkinter desktop ADB installer/kiosk tool, unused by build.
24	- `models/` — duplicate 1.7GB GGUFs + HuggingFace cache dirs.
25	- Kiosk-mode machinery (device admin, BIND_DEVICE_ADMIN, excludeFromRecents) — obsolete under phone-per-player.

# read:infra-monetization

## HELLDECK Infrastructure Readiness Report

### 1. Monetization — YES, real Play Billing exists
Dependency: `com.android.billingclient:billing-ktx:6.0.1` (app/build.gradle:186). Implementation in `app/src/main/java/com/helldeck/billing/PurchaseManager.kt` (469 lines): singleton with two INAPP products — `helldeck_premium` ($4.99 one-time, unlocks 9 premium games) and `helldeck_tip` ($1.99 tip jar). Handles connection retry, product query, purchase flow, restorePurchases, DataStore persistence. No server-side receipt verification (client-only). `PromoCodeManager.kt` (151 lines) has XOR-obfuscated client-side promo codes. Freemium gate is live in UI: `ui/components/UpgradeModal.kt`, `PromoCodeDialog.kt`, `GamePickerSheet.kt`. 5 free games (ROAST_CONSENSUS, POISON_PITCH, FILL_IN_FINISHER, SCATTERBLAST, CONFESSION_OR_CAP); rest locked. An `internal` product flavor sets UNLOCK_ALL=true; debug builds also unlock all. Note: this is a "one person pays" model already — but per-device, not per-room.

### 2. Networking — ZERO
AndroidManifest.xml declares only VIBRATE, FOREGROUND_SERVICE, BIND_DEVICE_ADMIN. **No INTERNET permission.** `usesCleartextTraffic="false"`. No okhttp/retrofit/ktor/firebase/nearby/bluetooth/play-services deps in any build.gradle. No socket/HTTP code in the 180 Kotlin files (only exception-type name checks in ErrorBoundary.kt and connectivity strings in AppUtils.kt). `telemetry/Telemetry.kt` is an explicit no-op sink. Play Billing works via Play Store IPC so it needs no INTERNET. Conclusion: the entire Jackbox-style layer (room codes, WebSockets, browser clients, backend) is greenfield — nothing to reuse, nothing to fight.

### 3. Build/release — CI exists, Play Store NOT ready
`codemagic.yaml`: mac_mini_m2, triggers on push to main; runs ktlint+detekt, `testProductionReleaseUnitTest`, builds `assembleProductionRelease` APK and `bundleProductionRelease` AAB; artifacts emailed to owner. **No Play Store publishing step** (no google_play integration/track). **Release signing is commented out** (app/build.gradle:103 `// signingConfig signingConfigs.release`) so release artifacts are unsigned. versionCode 2 / versionName 1.0.1, arm64-v8a only.
**App size is a blocker:** `app/src/main/assets/models/` bundles qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf (1.0GB) + tinyllama-1.1b-chat-v1.0.Q4_K_M.gguf (638MB) = **1.7GB of uncompressed assets** (`noCompress 'gguf'`). Play's AAB base-module limit is ~200MB (larger only via Play Asset Delivery, not configured) — this AAB cannot be published as-is. Runtime also copies the model from assets to filesDir on first run (`ContentEngineProvider.scheduleModelPreparation`), so device footprint is ~2.7GB. A duplicate 1.7GB copy sits in repo-root `models/`. GGUFs are gitignored but `.git` is still 2.8GB.

### 4. llama.cpp native layer — tiny, cleanly isolated, pivot-safe
Surface area: `CMakeLists.txt` (31 lines), `app/src/main/cpp/llama_jni.cpp` (159 lines), `llama_android_compat.h` (20 lines). CMake gracefully builds a stub .so if `third_party/llama.cpp` (99MB submodule, commit 55945d2, Oct 2025) is absent; Kotlin catches UnsatisfiedLinkError and runs "without paraphrasing". Kotlin side: `llm/LocalLLM.kt` interface (`generate`, `classifyZeroShot`), `LlamaCppLLM.kt` (158 lines), `ModelManager.kt` (48 lines), test-only `ProxyLLM.kt`. Consumers (LLMCardGenerator/V2, Augmentor, FunnyJudge, ContentEngineProvider) all program against `LocalLLM`, and the engine already degrades to gold cards + templates when LLM is null. **Verdict: a server-side `LocalLLM` implementation drops in with zero call-site changes; host-only on-device generation works as-is; deleting the layer entirely removes the cmake block, cpp dir, submodule, and 1.7GB of assets.**

### 5. Tech stack
Kotlin 1.9.25, Jetpack Compose (BOM 2024.06.00, compiler ext 1.5.15, Material3), AGP 8.5.2, Gradle 8.7, Java 17, KSP, Room 2.6.1, DataStore, Navigation Compose 2.7.7. Three serialization libs (kotlinx-serialization, Gson, SnakeYAML). minSdk 21, target/compileSdk 34. Modules: `:app` + `:macrobenchmark` only. ~50.8k lines of Kotlin across 180 files. **No KMP, no iOS, no web/JS/wasm anywhere.** Single-activity portrait Compose app with kiosk-mode device-admin receiver (`admin/HelldeckDeviceAdminReceiver`).

### 6. Abandoned/bloat a pivot can drop
- `Heimdall/` — 16MB git submodule (Samsung firmware flasher), referenced nowhere in build or code; it recently broke Codemagic ("Fix Codemagic submodule" commit). Delete.
- `loader/helldeck_loader.py` — Tkinter desktop ADB installer/kiosk tool, unused by build.
- `models/` — duplicate 1.7GB GGUFs + HuggingFace cache dirs.
- Kiosk-mode machinery (device admin, BIND_DEVICE_ADMIN, excludeFromRecents) — obsolete under phone-per-player.
- 28 root-level .md files (ALL_WORK_COMPLETE.md, AUDIT_COMPLETE.md, etc.), `app/app/build/reports` stray dir, `.trae/.windsurf/.qodo/.autoheal` agent configs, `pyproject.toml`/`.ruff_cache`, `STREAMLINING_CLEANUP.sh`, `app/.cxx` (186MB), 2.8GB `.git`.
- If generation goes server-side: `third_party/llama.cpp` (99MB) + cpp dir + both GGUFs.

## KEY FACTS
- Google Play Billing IS implemented: billing-ktx:6.0.1 with PurchaseManager.kt (469 lines) selling helldeck_premium ($4.99 one-time, 9 premium games) and helldeck_tip ($1.99), plus promo codes and UpgradeModal paywall UI — but verification is client-only and the model is per-device, not per-room.
- Networking is ZERO: no INTERNET permission in AndroidManifest.xml, no HTTP/WebSocket/Firebase/Nearby/Bluetooth deps or code anywhere; the entire Jackbox-style multiplayer layer must be built greenfield.
- Play Store is NOT ready: release signingConfig is commented out (app/build.gradle:103, unsigned artifacts) and codemagic.yaml has no Play publishing step — it only builds APK/AAB and emails toledonick98@gmail.com.
- App size is a publishing blocker: 1.7GB of GGUF models (Qwen2.5-1.5B 1.0GB + TinyLlama-1.1B 638MB) live in app/src/main/assets/models with noCompress, far over Play's ~200MB AAB base limit; the model is also copied to filesDir at first run (~2.7GB on device).
- The llama.cpp layer is tiny and pivot-safe: 159-line JNI (app/src/main/cpp/llama_jni.cpp), 31-line CMakeLists with a graceful stub when the 99MB third_party/llama.cpp submodule is absent, and all card-gen consumers program against the LocalLLM interface — a server-backed or host-only implementation swaps in with zero call-site changes.
- The engine already degrades without the LLM: gold cards (917 in assets/gold/gold_cards_v2.json, 259KB) + template/lexicon fallback, with a safe 'gold-only mode' path in ContentEngineProvider.
- Stack: Kotlin 1.9.25, Compose BOM 2024.06.00/Material3, AGP 8.5.2, Gradle 8.7, Room, DataStore, minSdk 21 / target 34, modules :app + :macrobenchmark only, ~50.8k lines Kotlin in 180 files; NO KMP, iOS, or web code exists.
- Confirmed bloat to drop: Heimdall/ 16MB Samsung-flasher submodule referenced nowhere (and recently broke CI), loader/ Tkinter ADB installer, duplicate 1.7GB models/ dir, kiosk device-admin machinery, 28 root-level AI-session .md files, app/.cxx 186MB build cache, and a 2.8GB .git.
- A duplicate freemium escape hatch exists: 'internal' product flavor and all debug builds force UNLOCK_ALL=true, bypassing billing entirely.
- Free tier is 5 of 14 games: ROAST_CONSENSUS, POISON_PITCH, FILL_IN_FINISHER, SCATTERBLAST, CONFESSION_OR_CAP (PurchaseManager.FREE_GAMES).

S"[5]

42	**relationship_fails.json** (75): "they said 'I love you' and you said 'thank you'"[1] · "they found the pros and cons list you made about them"[2] · "they found your burner account"[3] · "you moaned someone else's name"[4] · "they caught you using Tinder on their WiFi"[5].
43	**would_you_rather_costs.json** (75): "always having wet socks"[1] · "your Spotify Wrapped being public knowledge"[2] · "your internet history becoming a Netflix documentary"[3] · "your nudes leaking to 10 people you know"[4] · "your sex life being narrated by Morgan Freeman on live TV"[5].
44	**awkward_contexts.json** (72): "waving at someone who was waving at the person behind you"[1] · "your card getting declined on a first date"[2] · "matching with your boss on a dating app"[3] · "running into your one-night stand at a family event"[4]. Mostly spice 1-2 relatable-tweet observations.
45	**red_flag_traits.json** (78): "they use 'alpha' and 'sigma' unironically"[1] · "they have a podcast about themselves"[2] · "they love-bomb you then go completely cold"[3] — the spice 3-5 half is genuinely dark abuse description ("they isolate you from your friends slowly and deliberately", "they threaten to expose your secrets if you leave") — disturbing, not funny.
46	
47	## Templates_v3
48	**roast_consensus_enhanced.json**: blueprints like `"Most likely to get caught " + {sexual_innuendo} + " at " + {awkward_contexts} + "."` → yields grammatical garbage: "Most likely to get caught **eat out... at that new restaurant** at **being the only person who dressed up for a casual event**." Verbs don't conjugate; contexts are gerund phrases, not places. Also 12 near-identical "justify" templates that only swap the stem ("Most likely to / Who would totally / Who would absolutely / Who would definitely / Name the player who would") + {chaotic_plan} + "because" + {evidence_reason}.
49	**fill_in_finisher.json**: 10 double-blank frames, e.g. "My therapist said _____ but I heard _____", "My villain origin story started with _____ and ended with _____", "Breaking news: _____ has been cancelled due to _____".
50	**text_trap.json**: 4 variants of `{text_senders} texts: "{text_messages}"` — pure mad-libs concatenation.
51	
52	## Broken / smells
53	1. **quality_score=10 on all 917 cards** — curation signal is fake.
54	2. **Placeholder leaks shown to players**: text_thread_trap_4 "Unknown number: 'Hey, this is [your crush's name]...'" and text_thread_trap_9 "...noticed [your name] has been acting weird" — literal brackets in display text.
55	3. **Near-duplicate**: fill_in_finisher_20 vs _56 identical except trailing period.
56	4. **RED_FLAG_RALLY: 20/50 cards' structured `red_flag`/`perk` fields contradict the display text** (two unrelated jokes stapled to one card, e.g. text says Guy Fieri voice, field says framed ex photos); optionA/optionB are just SMASH/PASS on every card.
57	5. **Tonal violations of "UNHINGED, never WHOLESOME"**: Hot Seat Imposter spice 2-3 is corporate-icebreaker tier ("What do they order at Starbucks?", "comfort food when they're feeling down"); Over/Under "times they've said 'sorry' today"; Reality Check "crying during a Pixar movie"; Title Fight recycles decade-old Reddit memes (duck-sized horses, hot dog sandwich, gorilla vs toddlers).
58	6. **Template pipeline can't be funny**: lexicon entries are premise fragments (relatable observations), templates concatenate them without grammar agreement or semantic pairing, so generated cards read as broken mad-libs — the gold cards are the only human-grade content, and only ~1/3 of those land above "seen it on Twitter".
59	
60	## KEY FACTS
61	- Gold deck = 917 cards across 14 games in app/src/main/assets/gold/gold_cards_v2.json; largest ROAST_CONSENSUS (140), smallest TITLE_FIGHT (43).
62	- quality_score is 10 for every single one of the 917 cards, so the field provides zero curation signal; spice ranges only 2-5 (no 1), with 53% clustered at spice 3.
63	- Two TEXT_THREAD_TRAP cards leak literal placeholders to players: '[your crush's name]' (text_thread_trap_4) and '[your name]' (text_thread_trap_9).
64	- 20 of 50 RED_FLAG_RALLY cards have structured red_flag/perk fields that contradict their display text (two different jokes stapled together), and optionA/optionB are just SMASH/PASS everywhere.
65	- One near-duplicate pair exists: fill_in_finisher_20 and fill_in_finisher_56 differ only by a trailing period.
66	- Tonal violations of 'UNHINGED never WHOLESOME' concentrate at spice 2-3: Starbucks orders, comfort food, Pixar crying, saying 'sorry', and recycled Reddit debates (duck-sized horses, hot dog sandwich).
67	- lexicons_v2 has 42 files of ~75 entries each with per-entry spice/tone tags; most entries are spice 1-2 relatable-tweet observations, not jokes.
68	- sexual_innuendo.json is entirely '...bait-and-switch' fragments (e.g. 'get pegged... for the promotion at work') that break grammatically when inserted into templates.
69	- roast_consensus_enhanced.json concatenates slots without grammar agreement ('Most likely to get caught eat out... at that new restaurant at being the only person who dressed up'), and contains 12 near-identical 'justify' templates differing only in stem synonyms.
70	- red_flag_traits.json spice 3-5 entries describe literal abuse (isolation, blackmail, stalking) played straight — dark without being funny.
71	- The strongest gold content is CONFESSION_OR_CAP and ALIBI_DROP (specific, escalating, spice-5 material); the weakest is HOT_SEAT_IMPOSTER (icebreaker questions) and the template-generated pipeline overall.
72	- Template+lexicon fallback (templates_v3 + lexicons_v2) is pure mad-libs concatenation, so any card not from the gold deck reads as broken filler — the gold deck is the only human-grade corpus.

## Global stats (917 gold cards)
Counts: ROAST_CONSENSUS 140, POISON_PITCH 85, CONFESSION_OR_CAP 80, FILL_IN_FINISHER 80, ALIBI_DROP 73, OVER_UNDER 62, HOT_SEAT_IMPOSTER 53, THE_UNIFYING_THEORY 53, SCATTERBLAST 53, RED_FLAG_RALLY 50, TEXT_THREAD_TRAP 50, TABOO_TIMER 50, REALITY_CHECK 45, TITLE_FIGHT 43. **quality_score = 10 on ALL 917 cards — the field carries zero signal.** Spice: 2→115, 3→488, 4→205, 5→109 (no spice 1; 53% sits at spice 3).

## Verbatim corpus sample (spice in brackets)

**ROAST_CONSENSUS** — "Who would get catfished and still go on the date?"[2] · "Who would accidentally call the teacher 'Mom' in college?"[2] · "Who would panic-buy 40 cans of beans during a mild weather warning?"[2] · "Who would get into a fistfight over a parking spot at Target?"[3] · "Who would fake their own birthday for free dessert at a restaurant?"[3] · "Who would still be on their phone during the actual apocalypse?"[3] · "Who would hook up at a funeral and call it 'celebrating life'?"[4] · "Who would absolutely sell feet pics if the money was right?"[4] · "Who would create an OnlyFans 'as a joke' and then take it very seriously?"[5] · "Who has the biggest gap between their public persona and who they actually are at 3am?"[5]

**CONFESSION_OR_CAP** — "I once got banned from a Build-A-Bear for 'inappropriate stuffing behavior'"[3] · "I accidentally joined a cult for 2 weeks and didn't realize until the robes arrived"[3] · "I have a 47-slide PowerPoint saved on my phone titled 'Why My Ex Was Wrong'"[3] · "I once tried to manifest a text from my crush by staring at my phone for three straight hours. It worked. It was a promotional text from DoorDash."[3] · "I have pretended to know what NFTs are for over a year now."[4] · "I once returned a book to the library 11 years late and blamed it on my 'former roommate.'"[4] · "I have kept hooking up with someone purely because they had a good mattress."[5] · "I once texted 'I miss you' to my ex, got left on read, and then texted 'sorry wrong person' to save face. They knew."[5] · "I once brought a date to a friend's party and my date left with someone else. From MY friend group."[5]

**POISON_PITCH** — "sweat maple syrup permanently OR cry glitter every time you get emotional?"[2] · "a pebble permanently stuck in your shoe OR an eyelash permanently stuck in your eye?"[2] · "browser history read aloud at your wedding OR Spotify Wrapped projected at every job interview for life?"[3] · "everyone hear your thoughts for 10 seconds every hour OR sneeze violently every time you lie?"[3] · "your ex write your LinkedIn recommendation OR your mom manage your dating profile?"[3] · "therapist's notes published as a bestseller OR group chat messages read at your funeral?"[4] · "every one-night stand finds your LinkedIn the next day OR your mom gets a notification every time you match?"[4] · "watch a sex tape of your parents OR have your parents critique a sex tape of you?"[5] · "find out your partner has a secret family OR they've been a different person the entire time?"[5]

**FILL_IN_FINISHER** — "The worst thing to whisper during a hug is _____"[3] · "My therapist finally quit after I told them about _____"[3] · "I got kicked out of _____ for _____"[3] · "The real reason I'm single is _____"[3] · "The worst thing to say during a eulogy is _____."[4] · "According to my ex, my worst quality is _____."[4] · "The thing I absolutely cannot explain on my credit card statement is _____."[4] · "During sex, the last thing you want to hear is _____."[5] · "The most disturbing thing I've found on a partner's phone is _____."[5]

**RED_FLAG_RALLY** — "PERK: They cook like a Michelin star chef. RED FLAG: but they narrate everything in a Guy Fieri voice."[2] · "PERK: They look like a model. RED FLAG: but they have 47,000 Funko Pops and call them 'the family'."[2] · "PERK: They're a literal doctor. RED FLAG: but they self-diagnosed themselves with 'too much sex appeal'."[3] · "PERK: They're rich and generous. RED FLAG: but they Venmo-request you for their half of everything, including water."[3] · "PERK: They're amazing in bed. RED FLAG: but they require a post-game performance review on a scale of 1-10."[4] · "PERK: They're loyal to a fault. RED FLAG: but 'loyalty' means tracking your location 24/7 and calling it 'love'."[4] · "PERK: They have an amazing body. RED FLAG: They have a mirror on their bedroom ceiling and narrate what they see."[5] · "PERK: They have a great car. RED FLAG: There's a shrine to you in the trunk."[5]

**HOT_SEAT_IMPOSTER** — "What's their go-to karaoke song?"[2] · "What's the weirdest thing in their fridge right now?"[2] · "What do they order at Starbucks?"[2] · "What's the first thing they do when they wake up?"[3] · "What's their comfort food when they're feeling down?"[3] · "What's their most irrational fear?"[4] · "What's their biggest pet peeve in a relationship?"[4] · "What's the most unhinged thing in their search history right now?"[5] · "What's their body count and are they lying right now?"[5]

**TEXT_THREAD_TRAP** — "Your mom texted: 'We found something interesting in your old room. Call me.'"[3] · "Your roommate texted: 'Don't come home right now. I'll explain later.'"[3] · "Your GPS texted: 'Based on your recent locations, are you okay? Like genuinely.'"[3] · "Your ex texted: 'I think I left my dignity at your place.'"[4] · "Your landlord texted: 'We need to talk about what the cameras caught.'"[4] · "Your best friend texted: 'I hooked up with your ex. Before you find out from someone else.'"[5] · "Your dad texted: 'Your mother and I found your internet history. Dinner is at 6. We'll be discussing it.'"[5] · "Your situationship texted: 'I think we should define the relationship.' Then immediately: 'Wait wrong person sorry'"[5]

**TABOO_TIMER** — INFLUENCER (forbid Social/Media/Followers/Content/Sponsor)[2] · CLOUT[2] · STAN[2] · GHOSTING (Text/Disappear/Dating/Ignore/Message)[3] · CATFISH[3] · CRINGE[3] · WALK OF SHAME (Walk/Morning/Party/Home/Night)[4] · SITUATIONSHIP[4] · ONE-NIGHT STAND (Hook/Sleep/Night/Bed/Stranger)[5] · SUGAR DADDY[5]

**THE_UNIFYING_THEORY** — "A Ponzi scheme, a CrossFit class, and a timeshare presentation."[3] · "A Ouija board, a Magic 8 Ball, and your horoscope app."[3] · "A haunted house, your ex's apartment, and an IKEA on a Saturday."[3] · "Your browser history, your diary, and a crime scene."[4] · "Your Spotify Wrapped, your therapist's notes, and your mugshot."[4] · "A confessional booth, a Twitter thread, and a first date with a therapist."[4] · "An OnlyFans creator, a megachurch pastor, and a MLM boss babe."[5] · "A strip club DJ, a kindergarten teacher, and your Uber driver at 2am."[5]

**TITLE_FIGHT** — "Hot dog is a sandwich vs Hot dog is NOT a sandwich"[2] · "Every toddler in the world vs One silverback gorilla"[2] · "100 duck-sized horses vs 1 horse-sized duck"[2] · "Knowing how you die vs Knowing when you die"[3] · "Cereal is a soup vs Cereal is NOT a soup"[3] · "Cancel culture: necessary accountability vs mob justice"[4] · "Your ex writing your eulogy vs Your ex writing your dating profile"[5] · "Drunk you vs Hungry you — who causes more damage?"[5]

**ALIBI_DROP** (must use 3 words) — "What were you doing in the restricted area of the zoo?" [selfie stick / night vision goggles / tuxedo][3] · "Why is there a mannequin in your trunk?" [carpool lane / fashion show / Loneliness][3] · "Why did you run from the cops in a banana costume?" [charity run / dare / potassium deficiency][3] · "Why were you at your ex's apartment at 3am?" [lost cat / ouija board / lasagna][4] · "What happened in Vegas that you refuse to discuss?" [chapel / magic show volunteer incident / iguana][4] · "Why were you seen buying a shovel, bleach, and a tarp at 3am?" [Birthday party / Organic gardening / Feng shui][5] · "Why did your ex file a restraining order the day after you 'ran into them' at the gym?" [Smoothie / Zodiac sign / Coincidence][5]

**REALITY_CHECK** — "Your ability to parallel park on a scale of 'first try' to 'just take the bus.'"[2] · "Your likelihood of crying during a Pixar movie."[2] · "How long you'd survive in a horror movie before doing something stupid."[3] · "How unhinged your screen time is."[3] · "How many nights per week you eat dinner standing over the sink."[3] · "How quickly you'd fold under police interrogation."[4] · "How fast you'd sell out the group for a million dollars."[4] · "How many red flags you'd ignore if the person was hot enough."[5]

**SCATTERBLAST** — "Excuses for being late. Letter: T"[2] · "Things that should be illegal but aren't. P"[2] · "Things you shouldn't say on a first date. I"[3] · "Reasons to call in sick. M"[3] · "Reasons your ex is your ex. L"[4] · "Things you'd confess to a priest. S"[4] · "Things you'd find in a serial killer's search history. H"[5] · "Things that would get you banned from heaven. S"[5]

**OVER_UNDER** — "Number of alarms they set to wake up on a workday."[2] · "Number of times they've said 'sorry' today."[2] · "Number of unread emails in their inbox right now."[3] · "Number of times they've cried this month."[3] · "Number of people they've kissed."[4] · "Number of times they've been ghosted."[4] · "Number of times they've stalked an ex's profile this week."[5] · "Number of people in this room they've thought about hooking up with."[5]

## Lexicons_v2 (42 files, ~75 entries each, schema: text/tags/tone/spice/locality)
**sexual_innuendo.json** (75): all bait-and-switch one-liners — "go all night without getting tired"[2] · "handle a big load... of laundry"[2] · "eat out... at that new restaurant"[3] · "stroke... the cat"[3] · "get pegged... for the promotion at work"[5] · "69... is a great temperature outside"[5] · "rub one out... the stain on your shirt"[5].
**bodily_functions.json** (75): "sneezing with your eyes open and everyone sees it"[1] · "crop-dusting an entire aisle at the grocery store"[2] · "swamp crotch on a first date in summer"[2] · "sprinting to the bathroom because you trusted a fart"[3] · "sharting at a formal event in white clothes"[4] · "trusting a fart in bed with someone new and being catastrophically wrong"[5].
**relationship_fails.json** (75): "they said 'I love you' and you said 'thank you'"[1] · "they found the pros and cons list you made about them"[2] · "they found your burner account"[3] · "you moaned someone else's name"[4] · "they caught you using Tinder on their WiFi"[5].
**would_you_rather_costs.json** (75): "always having wet socks"[1] · "your Spotify Wrapped being public knowledge"[2] · "your internet history becoming a Netflix documentary"[3] · "your nudes leaking to 10 people you know"[4] · "your sex life being narrated by Morgan Freeman on live TV"[5].
**awkward_contexts.json** (72): "waving at someone who was waving at the person behind you"[1] · "your card getting declined on a first date"[2] · "matching with your boss on a dating app"[3] · "running into your one-night stand at a family event"[4]. Mostly spice 1-2 relatable-tweet observations.
**red_flag_traits.json** (78): "they use 'alpha' and 'sigma' unironically"[1] · "they have a podcast about themselves"[2] · "they love-bomb you then go completely cold"[3] — the spice 3-5 half is genuinely dark abuse description ("they isolate you from your friends slowly and deliberately", "they threaten to expose your secrets if you leave") — disturbing, not funny.

## Templates_v3
**roast_consensus_enhanced.json**: blueprints like `"Most likely to get caught " + {sexual_innuendo} + " at " + {awkward_contexts} + "."` → yields grammatical garbage: "Most likely to get caught **eat out... at that new restaurant** at **being the only person who dressed up for a casual event**." Verbs don't conjugate; contexts are gerund phrases, not places. Also 12 near-identical "justify" templates that only swap the stem ("Most likely to / Who would totally / Who would absolutely / Who would definitely / Name the player who would") + {chaotic_plan} + "because" + {evidence_reason}.
**fill_in_finisher.json**: 10 double-blank frames, e.g. "My therapist said _____ but I heard _____", "My villain origin story started with _____ and ended with _____", "Breaking news: _____ has been cancelled due to _____".
**text_trap.json**: 4 variants of `{text_senders} texts: "{text_messages}"` — pure mad-libs concatenation.

## Broken / smells
1. **quality_score=10 on all 917 cards** — curation signal is fake.
2. **Placeholder leaks shown to players**: text_thread_trap_4 "Unknown number: 'Hey, this is [your crush's name]...'" and text_thread_trap_9 "...noticed [your name] has been acting weird" — literal brackets in display text.
3. **Near-duplicate**: fill_in_finisher_20 vs _56 identical except trailing period.
4. **RED_FLAG_RALLY: 20/50 cards' structured `red_flag`/`perk` fields contradict the display text** (two unrelated jokes stapled to one card, e.g. text says Guy Fieri voice, field says framed ex photos); optionA/optionB are just SMASH/PASS on every card.
5. **Tonal violations of "UNHINGED, never WHOLESOME"**: Hot Seat Imposter spice 2-3 is corporate-icebreaker tier ("What do they order at Starbucks?", "comfort food when they're feeling down"); Over/Under "times they've said 'sorry' today"; Reality Check "crying during a Pixar movie"; Title Fight recycles decade-old Reddit memes (duck-sized horses, hot dog sandwich, gorilla vs toddlers).
6. **Template pipeline can't be funny**: lexicon entries are premise fragments (relatable observations), templates concatenate them without grammar agreement or semantic pairing, so generated cards read as broken mad-libs — the gold cards are the only human-grade content, and only ~1/3 of those land above "seen it on Twitter".

## KEY FACTS
- Gold deck = 917 cards across 14 games in app/src/main/assets/gold/gold_cards_v2.json; largest ROAST_CONSENSUS (140), smallest TITLE_FIGHT (43).
- quality_score is 10 for every single one of the 917 cards, so the field provides zero curation signal; spice ranges only 2-5 (no 1), with 53% clustered at spice 3.
- Two TEXT_THREAD_TRAP cards leak literal placeholders to players: '[your crush's name]' (text_thread_trap_4) and '[your name]' (text_thread_trap_9).
- 20 of 50 RED_FLAG_RALLY cards have structured red_flag/perk fields that contradict their display text (two different jokes stapled together), and optionA/optionB are just SMASH/PASS everywhere.
- One near-duplicate pair exists: fill_in_finisher_20 and fill_in_finisher_56 differ only by a trailing period.
- Tonal violations of 'UNHINGED never WHOLESOME' concentrate at spice 2-3: Starbucks orders, comfort food, Pixar crying, saying 'sorry', and recycled Reddit debates (duck-sized horses, hot dog sandwich).
- lexicons_v2 has 42 files of ~75 entries each with per-entry spice/tone tags; most entries are spice 1-2 relatable-tweet observations, not jokes.
- sexual_innuendo.json is entirely '...bait-and-switch' fragments (e.g. 'get pegged... for the promotion at work') that break grammatically when inserted into templates.
- roast_consensus_enhanced.json concatenates slots without grammar agreement ('Most likely to get caught eat out... at that new restaurant at being the only person who dressed up'), and contains 12 near-identical 'justify' templates differing only in stem synonyms.
- red_flag_traits.json spice 3-5 entries describe literal abuse (isolation, blackmail, stalking) played straight — dark without being funny.
- The strongest gold content is CONFESSION_OR_CAP and ALIBI_DROP (specific, escalating, spice-5 material); the weakest is HOT_SEAT_IMPOSTER (icebreaker questions) and the template-generated pipeline overall.
- Template+lexicon fallback (templates_v3 + lexicons_v2) is pure mad-libs concatenation, so any card not from the gold deck reads as broken filler — the gold deck is the only human-grade corpus.

aiTitle: Redesign card game for fun multiplayer gameplay

input.content:
# HELLDECK 2.0 — Gameplay Model Spec v1 ("The Descent")

_Design target: Jackbox without the TV. Every player on their own phone, same room, comedy stays out loud. One person pays; everyone else joins in a mobile browser._

## 0. Identity constraints (non-negotiable)
- UNHINGED, never WHOLESOME. Dive bar with great lighting; group chat that went too far at 2am; a roast where everyone's in on it.
- The game is a machine for making FRIENDS funny about EACH OTHER. Screens carry secrets and votes; mouths carry the comedy. The phone is never the performer.
- Cards reference REAL player names, not seats.

## 1. Platform model
- **Host**: native Android app (paid unlock). Creates a "Night," gets a 4-letter room code + QR.
- **Joiners**: any phone browser at helldeck page /CODE (QR encodes the URL). No install, no account, name + avatar only. PWA install prompt only AFTER a completed night.
- **Backend**: one Cloudflare Durable Object per room (free tier), WebSocket relay. Host app is authoritative for game logic; DO relays + persists room state for reconnects (rejoin = same code + same name, seat held 90s with auto-play).
- **No TV anywhere in the loop.** Optional cast/big-screen later, never required.
- Host is also a player (unlike Jackbox where the screen is furniture).

## 2. The shared-moment problem (no TV) — "Stage" design
- All phones run synchronized phases; REVEALS render on EVERY phone simultaneously with a 3-2-1 sync beat.
- The HOST phone additionally acts as **the Stage**: it plays the audio (stings, timers, countdown ticks) for the room and can be placed face-up mid-table ("the candle on the table"). Stage mode is a toggle; if host keeps their phone in hand, audio still plays from it and reveals stay synced on all phones.
- Performance games (pitches, alibis, impressions) happen OUT LOUD to the room; phones show the audience only a vote UI + timer, so heads come UP during performances.

## 3. Session structure — "The Descent"
- A Night = a descent through **Circles** (Dante, on-brand). Group picks depth at lobby: **Quick Dip (5 circles ≈ 20 min), Standard Descent (7 ≈ 30 min), Full Damnation (9 ≈ 40 min)**.
- **Spice is diegetic**: content heat is a function of depth. Circle 1-2 = warm-up (spice 2), mid-circles = spice 3-4, final circle = spice 5, most personal content. This IS the 36-questions escalation curve; there is no separate spice slider mid-game (host sets a ceiling in lobby: "How deep does this group go?" caps the final circles for tamer groups).
- Each Circle = one mini-game round, ~3-4 min. Game arc is pre-shuffled by the app for an energy curve: open with simultaneous-input games (everyone acts, low spotlight), physical/stage spike mid-descent, spotlight performance games once the group is warm, finale = the most personal game the group's depth allows.
- **The Ninth Circle (finale)**: points ×3, one climactic round, then THE JUDGMENT: winner crowned + auto-generated superlatives from the night's data ("Most Roasted," "The Liar," "Voted Most Likely To Die First"). Definite end. Option to "descend again."
- No elimination ever. Comeback mechanic: last place gets a hidden ×2 on one late circle ("Devil's Bargain").

## 4. Game triage (14 → 12 played, 2 reworked/cut)
**Class A — simultaneous private input (own-phone native):**
1. Roast Consensus — everyone secretly votes a victim on their phone, synced reveal shows vote spread + victim's name in lights. Room Heat bonus (80%+ consensus) kept.
2. Fill-In Finisher → becomes **Quiplash-structured**: setup shown on all phones, everyone (except a rotating Reader) writes a punchline; anonymous answers appear on all phones; group secret-votes (can't vote for self); Reader performs the winners aloud. "Panic button" gives 2 curated fallback answers at half points.
3. Over/Under — subject named, group debates the line OUT LOUD, everyone secretly bets on their phone, subject enters the true number from their own phone (receipts!), synced reveal.
4. Confession or Cap — confessor privately locks TRUE/FALSE, tells the story out loud, jury secretly votes believe/cap, synced reveal.
5. Alibi Drop — accused gets 3 mandatory words ONLY on their phone, improvises alibi aloud; jury privately guesses the 3 planted words from an 8-word lineup on their phones; structured reveal (word hunt = active listening job).
6. Taboo Timer — clue-giver sees word+forbidden list; ONE designated Buzzer phone also sees the forbidden list (role finally works); guessers' phones show timer only.
7. Hot Seat Imposter — roles delivered privately (Spyfall pattern); askers get suggested questions on their phones; performance aloud; secret REAL/FAKE vote.

**Class B — verbal performance, phones only gate + vote:**
8. Poison Pitch — two pitchers assigned privately, dilemma on all phones, pitches aloud 30s each (stage phone runs the bell), secret vote for best pitch.
9. Red Flag Rally — defender assigned, perk+flag on all phones, 45s defense aloud, secret SMASH/PASS, synced verdict.
10. Text Thread Trap — performer gets incoming text + mandatory tone privately; reads the text aloud and improvises the reply IN TONE; group votes SURVIVED/BLOCKED.
11. Reality Check — subject privately self-rates 1-10; the GROUP DEBATE stays fully out loud in front of them (do not privatize); group's number entered on stage phone by consensus; synced ego-gap reveal.

**Class C — stage games (one phone in the middle):**
12. Scatterblast — stage phone IS the bomb (hidden fuse, audio ticking); turn passes around the circle by voice; category+letter on all phones so nobody cranes.

**Reworked/cut:**
13. Title Fight — CUT from the auto-rotation (physical duels don't need an app; lowest phone value). Kept as a manual "Fight Break" the host can trigger between circles.
14. The Unifying Theory — CUT for v1 (highest cognitive load, worst dead-air risk, weakest votes). Revisit as a premium pack later.

## 5. Pacing rules (Jack Principles, enforced by server)
- One task per phone per moment. Never two questions at once.
- ALL input is simultaneous; no sequential turns anywhere.
- Server timer advances the game WITHOUT stragglers: unanswered = auto-abstain (votes) or auto-panic-answer (writing). Slow players get "the game moved on" — never the reverse.
- Dead-air budget: no phase where <100% of players have something to do OR someone is performing aloud. Between circles: 8s "descend" transition with next-game explainer on all phones.
- Explainers: first time a game appears for this GROUP, a 15s "how it works" card; after that, 5s title card only.

## 6. Scoring & feedback
- Simple: +2 winner-aligned, +1 secondary, Room Heat +1 (80% consensus moments), finale ×3. Points visible as "Descent Rank" ladder between circles (5s).
- Card feedback is INVISIBLE: reaction taps (🔥 fire-spam allowed, like Twitch chat) during reveals double as telemetry. No LOL/MEH/TRASH modal, no feedback phase. Reaction bursts show as ephemeral flames on all phones.
- Superlatives at THE JUDGMENT are generated from real telemetry (most-voted, biggest lie, biggest ego-gap, fastest fingers).

## 7. Personalization & memory (no LLM in v1 runtime)
- Cards have {NAME} slots filled with REAL player names from the lobby, selection-weighted so everyone gets targeted roughly equally (spotlight fairness counter).
- **Crew memory** (evolved Brainpack): the room remembers this GROUP across nights — past superlatives, biggest roast moments, win history. Callbacks appear as content ("Last descent, Nick was voted Most Likely To Join A Cult. Has he improved?"). Stored in host app, synced to DO room state.
- Generation-time AI (cloud, host-paid, pennies/session) is a LATER content-refresh feature, never a runtime dependency, never the joke author — it only personalizes hand-written structures.

## 8. Safety valves (required for spice 4-5 to work)
- **Every player gets 2 BRIMSTONE tokens per night**: burn one to veto a card before it plays (anonymous, instant re-deal, no discussion, "the card burns" animation) or to skip being the subject. Untouched tokens = +1 point each at Judgment (cost to use, reward to hold).
- Lobby depth ceiling (host-set) caps max spice.
- No card ever targets appearance, family death, or protected traits — writing-level rule, not runtime filter.

## 9. Monetization (verified store-legal)
- Free to download. **A new host's first full Night is free** (the Jackbox demo loop — joiners already experienced the product at someone's party; the pitch at conversion is hosting power).
- **$9.99 one-time "Host the Descent" unlock** (Play Billing in-app; web checkout link-out later). Joiners always free, unlimited.
- Content packs ($2.99-4.99) later. No subscription. No ads ever (Psych!'s death was mid-party ads).

## 10. Failure handling
- Disconnect: seat held 90s (auto-abstain/auto-panic meanwhile), rejoin via same name+code. iOS Safari backgrounding handled by wake-lock + resync-on-visibilitychange.
- Host phone dies: room state persists in DO for 10 min; host relaunch restores the Night at the current circle.
- Latecomers: join as **Imps** (audience — they vote with 0.5 weight and fuel Room Heat) until the next circle starts, then become full players. Player range 3-10 active + up to 20 imps.
