This IS the 36-questions escalation curve; there is no separate spice slider mid-game (host sets a ceiling in lobby: "How deep does this group go?" caps the final circles for tamer groups).
- Each Circle = one mini-game round, ~3-4 min. Game arc is pre-shuffled by the app for an energy curve: open with simultaneous-input games (everyone acts, low spotlight), physical/stage spike mid-descent, spotlight performance games once the group is warm, finale = the most personal game the group's depth allows.
- **The Ninth Circle (finale)**: points ×3, one climactic round, then THE JUDGMENT: winner crowned + auto-generated superlatives from the night's data ("Most Roasted," "The Liar," "Voted Most Likely To Die First"). Definite end. Option to "descend again."
- No elimination ever. Comeback mechanic: last place gets a hidden ×2 on one late circle ("Devil's Bargain").

## 4. Game triage (14 → 12 played, 2 reworked/cut)
**Class A — simultaneous private input (own-phone native):**
1. Roast Consensus — everyone secretly votes a victim on their phone, synced reveal shows vote spread + victim's name in lights. Room Heat bonus (80%+ consensus) kept.
2. Fill-In Finisher → becomes **Quiplash-structured**: setup shown on all phones, everyone (except a rotating Reader) writes a punchline; anonymous answers appear on all phones; group secret-votes (can't vote for self); Reader performs the winners aloud. "Panic button" gives 2 curated fallback answers at half points.
3. Over/Under — subject named, group debates the line OUT LOUD, everyone secretly bets on their phone, subject enters the true number from their own phone (receipts!), synced reveal.
4. Confession or Cap — confessor privately locks TRUE/FALSE, tells the story out loud, jury secretly votes believe/cap, synced reveal.
5. Alibi Drop — accused gets 3 mandatory words ONLY on their phone, improvises alibi aloud; jury privately guesses the 3 planted words from an 8-word lineup on their phones; structured reveal (word hunt = active listening job).
6. Taboo Timer — clue-giver sees word+forbidden list; ONE designated Buzzer phone also sees the forbidden list (role finally works); guessers' phones show timer only.
7. Hot Seat Imposter — roles delivered privately (Spyfall pattern); askers get suggested questions on their phones; performance aloud; secret REAL/FAKE vote.

**Class B — verbal performance, phones only gate + vote:**
8. Poison Pitch — two pitchers assigned privately, dilemma on all phones, pitches aloud 30s each (stage phone runs the bell), secret vote for best pitch.
9. Red Flag Rally — defender assigned, perk+flag on all phones, 45s defense aloud, secret SMASH/PASS, synced verdict.
10. Text Thread Trap — performer gets incoming text + mandatory tone privately; reads the text aloud and improvises the reply IN TONE; group votes SURVIVED/BLOCKED.
11. Reality Check — subject privately self-rates 1-10; the GROUP DEBATE stays fully out loud in front of them (do not privatize); group's number entered on stage phone by consensus; synced ego-gap reveal.

**Class C — stage games (one phone in the middle):**
12. Scatterblast — stage phone IS the bomb (hidden fuse, audio ticking); turn passes around the circle by voice; category+letter on all phones so nobody cranes.

**Reworked/cut:**
13. Title Fight — CUT from the auto-rotation (physical duels don't need an app; lowest phone value). Kept as a manual "Fight Break" the host can trigger between circles.
14. The Unifying Theory — CUT for v1 (highest cognitive load, worst dead-air risk, weakest votes). Revisit as a premium pack later.

## 5. Pacing rules (Jack Principles, enforced by server)
- One task per phone per moment. Never two questions at once.
- ALL input is simultaneous; no sequential turns anywhere.
- Server timer advances the game WITHOUT stragglers: unanswered = auto-abstain (votes) or auto-panic-answer (writing). Slow players get "the game moved on" — never the reverse.
- Dead-air budget: no phase where <100% of players have something to do OR someone is performing aloud. Between circles: 8s "descend" transition with next-game explainer on all phones.
- Explainers: first time a game appears for this GROUP, a 15s "how it works" card; after that, 5s title card only.

## 6. Scoring & feedback
- Simple: +2 winner-aligned, +1 secondary, Room Heat +1 (80% consensus moments), finale ×3. Points visible as "Descent Rank" ladder between circles (5s).
- Card feedback is INVISIBLE: reaction taps (🔥 fire-spam allowed, like Twitch chat) during reveals double as telemetry. No LOL/MEH/TRASH modal, no feedback phase. Reaction bursts show as ephemeral flames on all phones.
- Superlatives at THE JUDGMENT are generated from real telemetry (most-voted, biggest lie, biggest ego-gap, fastest fingers).

## 7. Personalization & memory (no LLM in v1 runtime)
- Cards have {NAME} slots filled with REAL player names from the lobby, selection-weighted so everyone gets targeted roughly equally (spotlight fairness counter).
- **Crew memory** (evolved Brainpack): the room remembers this GROUP across nights — past superlatives, biggest roast moments, win history. Callbacks appear as content ("Last descent, Nick was voted Most Likely To Join A Cult. Has he improved?"). Stored in host app, synced to DO room state.
- Generation-time AI (cloud, host-paid, pennies/session) is a LATER content-refresh feature, never a runtime dependency, never the joke author — it only personalizes hand-written structures.

## 8. Safety valves (required for spice 4-5 to work)
- **Every player gets 2 BRIMSTONE tokens per night**: burn one to veto a card before it plays (anonymous, instant re-deal, no discussion, "the card burns" animation) or to skip being the subject. Untouched tokens = +1 point each at Judgment (cost to use, reward to hold).
- Lobby depth ceiling (host-set) caps max spice.
- No card ever targets appearance, family death, or protected traits — writing-level rule, not runtime filter.

## 9. Monetization (verified store-legal)
- Free to download. **A new host's first full Night is free** (the Jackbox demo loop — joiners already experienced the product at someone's party; the pitch at conversion is hosting power).
- **$9.99 one-time "Host the Descent" unlock** (Play Billing in-app; web checkout link-out later). Joiners always free, unlimited.
- Content packs ($2.99-4.99) later. No subscription. No ads ever (Psych!'s death was mid-party ads).

## 10. Failure handling
- Disconnect: seat held 90s (auto-abstain/auto-panic meanwhile), rejoin via same name+code. iOS Safari backgrounding handled by wake-lock + resync-on-visibilitychange.
- Host phone dies: room state persists in DO for 10 min; host relaunch restores the Night at the current circle.
- Latecomers: join as **Imps** (audience — they vote with 0.5 weight and fuel Room Heat) until the next circle starts, then become full players. Player range 3-10 active + up to 20 imps.

python3 << 'EOF'
import json, collections, os
with open('/Users/nicholastoledo/Development/active/HELLDECK/app/src/main/assets/gold/gold_cards_v2.json') as f:
    cards = json.load(f)
outdir = '/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-nicholastoledo-Development-active-HELLDECK/4eb51e65-3e38-49e9-b745-6d128c919d2d/scratchpad/corpus'
os.makedirs(outdir, exist_ok=True)
by_game = collections.defaultdict(list)
for c in cards: by_game[c['game']].append(c)
for g, cs in by_game.items():
    with open(f'{outdir}/{g.lower()}.md', 'w') as f:
        f.write(f'# {g} — {len(cs)} gold cards (full deck)\n\n')
        for c in cs:
            extra = {k: v for k, v in c.items() if k not in ('id','game','family','text','quality_score','locality','spice')}
            f.write(f"- [{c['id']}] (spice {c.get('spice')}) {c.get('text','')}")
            if extra: f.write(f"  || extra fields: {json.dumps(extra, ensure_ascii=False)}")
            f.write('\n')
    print(g, len(cs))
EOF

aiTitle: Redesign card game for fun multiplayer gameplay

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import json, glob
files = sorted(glob.glob('/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-nicholastoledo-Development-active-HELLDECK/4eb51e65-3e38-49e9-b745-6d128c919d2d/scratchpad/playtest_*.json'))
for fn in files:
    with open(fn) as f: v = json.load(f)
    name = fn.split('playtest_')[-1].replace('.json','')
    print(f"\n{'='*80}\n{name}  [{v['group_verdict']} {v['fun_score_1_10']}/10]\n{'='*80}")
    print("FAILURES (FATAL/MAJOR only):")
    for fl in v['failures']:
        if fl['severity'] in ('FATAL','MAJOR'):
            print(f"  [{fl['severity']}] {fl['where']}\n     WHAT: {fl['what'][:400]}\n     FIX: {fl['fix'][:300]}")
    print("  MINOR count:", sum(1 for fl in v['failures'] if fl['severity']=='MINOR'))
EOF

================================================================================
bar6  [MIXED 6.5/10]
================================================================================
FAILURES (FATAL/MAJOR only):
  [MAJOR] Spec section 2 (Stage design) + section 4 Scatterblast + Poison Pitch bell + 3-2-1 audio beats
     WHAT: Every shared-moment cue is audio-first from the stage phone, and the stated venue is a loud bar: the Scatterblast boom, the pitch bell, and countdown ticks are all inaudible. iOS Safari joiners also have no Vibration API. The shared-moment layer the spec leans on simply does not exist in its own target environment.
     FIX: Make every sync moment visual-first on ALL phones (full-screen flash for boom/bell/3-2-1, max brightness pulse), with audio as garnish and haptics host-Android-only. The synced-reveal machinery already exists — route the cues through it.
  [MAJOR] Spec section 4, game 2 (Fill-In Finisher phase order)
     WHAT: Answers render on all phones BEFORE voting and the Reader performs only the WINNERS after votes close. First contact with the funniest line happens silently and asynchronously across six screens (40s heads-down mid-party), and the aloud performance is a spoiled rerun. This inverts the anonymity+performance+shared-attention mechanism the design's own research identifies as the core of Quiplash.
     FIX: Flip the order: Reader performs ALL answers aloud from a teleprompter view on their phone; other phones show only a numbered ballot that unlocks when reading ends; then secret vote; then author + winner reveal.
  [MAJOR] Spec section 5 (pacing) — reveal-to-next-circle advancement is unspecified
     WHAT: If the server timer that 'advances without stragglers' also owns the reveal phase (the old app auto-advanced reveals in 5s), it will cut off the post-reveal table roast — which the simulation shows is where most actual laughter lives. The machine tramples the very moments it exists to create.
     FIX: Reveals hold until the host taps DESCEND (soft cap ~45s), with fire-reaction spam live during the hold. Server timers own input phases only; humans own aftermath.
  [MAJOR] Spec section 8 (Brimstone: 'skip being the subject')
     WHAT: Card-veto is anonymous but subject-skip cannot be — the room watches the game re-target, so the shy player's safety valve is a public flinch. Ana's guaranteed spotlight (fairness counter) plus a public-only opt-out reproduces the 'challenge cannot be refused' flaw the design's own research flags.
     FIX: Deliver subject assignments privately with a 5-second burn window BEFORE the room learns who was picked; on burn, re-deal silently. The skip becomes genuinely anonymous and the fairness counter just moves on.
  [MAJOR] Spec sections 1+2 (host is a player AND host phone is the Stage)
     WHAT: In 10 of 12 games the host has secret input (votes, roles, truth-locks), so the face-up mid-table stage phone must be grabbed and shielded nearly every round — either the secret leaks or the 'candle on the table' fiction breaks within two circles. In the bar simulation the Stage contributed nothing except during Scatterblast.
     FIX: Demote Stage to (a) an optional second device, or (b) an auto-mode: stage layout only during phases where the host holds no secret (Scatterblast, transitions, reveals), reverting to in-hand controller otherwise. Never design a phase assuming the stage phone is stationary.
  MINOR count: 7

================================================================================
birthday13  [MIXED 6.5/10]
================================================================================
FAILURES (FATAL/MAJOR only):
  [FATAL] spec_v1.md section 10 (Latecomers/Imps) vs section 3 player cap — 'become full players... Player range 3-10 active'
     WHAT: The Imp conversion promise is structurally impossible at a full table. 10 active players + 3 latecomers: spec says Imps 'become full players when the next circle starts,' but the active cap is 10. Undefined collision. If cap wins, the 3 birthday latecomers are permanently 0.5-weight ghost voters: never named in a card, never write a punchline, never get a spotlight, likely absent from Judgment sup
     FIX: Pick one and spec it: (a) raise the active cap to 12-16 with N-scaled systems (see vote-math failure), or (b) make Imp a designed role with citizenship: Imp names enter the {NAME} card pool (roastable = included), Imps write punchlines in Fill-In (audience bracket), each Imp gets one guaranteed came
  [MAJOR] spec_v1.md section 4, game 2 (Fill-In Finisher) at N=10
     WHAT: Two compounding breaks in the flagship game. (1) Beat inversion: answers appear silently on phones and the group votes BEFORE the Reader performs — so first exposure to every punchline is a solo heads-down read; the 'room moment' is a re-run of jokes everyone already consumed. A joke read twice lands at half strength; Quiplash's whole engine is laugh-at-presentation, vote-after. (2) The 9-answer w
     FIX: At N≥7, split into parallel prompts Quiplash-style: 2-3 setups, 3-4 writers each, then head-to-head face-offs where the Reader performs BOTH answers ALOUD before the vote opens (phones show only VOTE A / VOTE B during the performance). Restores first-exposure laughs to the room, caps read-load at 2 
  [MAJOR] spec_v1.md section 2 (Stage design) vs section 1 'Host is also a player'
     WHAT: When Stage mode is on, the host phone lies face-up mid-table — but the host is a player who must cast secret votes, write punchlines, and lock private answers on that same phone every circle. Their inputs render on the public table screen, breaking secret ballot (the anti-bandwagon core of Roast Consensus, Confession juries, all Class A games) for one player in every single round. Spec never addre
     FIX: Lift-to-vote: accelerometer flips the Stage phone to the host's private input screen when picked up, back to public Stage face when laid flat (audio uninterrupted). Ship it as a signature mechanic — 'lay the candle flat for the room, lift it to sin in private.' Also define what the Stage face shows 
  [MAJOR] spec_v1.md section 4 Class B games + section 5 dead-air budget
     WHAT: Every Class B circle gambles ~3 minutes of the night on one randomly-assigned human being present AND willing. Simulated Circle 5: Poison Pitch assigned the shy player, who gave 8 seconds of mumble and 22 seconds of bell-timer silence — the audience had nothing on their screens but a timer, and half checked Instagram. Worse: a performer who's in the kitchen when assigned (constant at real parties)
     FIX: Three valves: (1) volunteer-first assignment — 5s 'WHO WANTS BLOOD?' claim button, random only as fallback, weighted toward players who haven't had a spotlight; (2) audience SKIP-'EM pile-on button — if 60% tap it, re-deal with a mocking sting (converts a stall into a laugh at the game's expense, on
  [MAJOR] spec_v1.md sections 4 and 6 — vote resolution at N=8-10 and with Imps
     WHAT: Vote math is undefined or dead exactly where this scenario stresses it. Roast Consensus at 10 voters over 10 candidates: 3-3-2-1-1 spreads and plurality TIES are frequent, and the reveal ('victim's name in lights') has no defined tie behavior. Room Heat's 80% threshold = 8/10 on one victim, which effectively never triggers at N≥8 — the bonus mechanic is silently dead at large tables. Hot Seat Impo
     FIX: Spec an N-scaled resolution table: plurality ties in Roast Consensus become a framed feature ('THE ROOM IS TORN — DOUBLE ROAST', both names in lights); Room Heat threshold scales (80% at N≤6, 60% at N≥8) or becomes 'strict majority on one head'; even-jury ties resolve pro-defendant with a 'HUNG JURY
  MINOR count: 6

houseparty8  [FUN 7/10]
================================================================================
FAILURES (FATAL/MAJOR only):
  [FATAL] Spec section 8 — Brimstone tokens (the safety valve licensing spice 4-5)
     WHAT: The veto's anonymity is illusory exactly when it matters. Subject-targeted cards render on all phones simultaneously, so by the time Jess sees 'Number of people they've kissed — subject: Jess,' the whole room has read it; burning it triggers a room-wide 'the card burns' animation and everyone infers the burner (it's obviously the subject or her partner). The valve converts a risky card into a guar
     FIX: (1) Subject pre-view: any card that names a subject renders on the subject's phone 3 seconds before the room, with a silent burn option — on burn, the room simply sees a normal deal; no animation, no trace, burn events for subject-cards are never surfaced. (2) Kill the +1 hoarding reward; unused tok
  [MAJOR] Spec section 7 — {NAME} slot fairness counter + subject selection for knowledge-dependent games
     WHAT: The system has no concept of 'nobody knows this person.' The fairness counter actively steers targeting toward Alex to equalize spotlight, but Hot Seat Imposter, Reality Check, and personal-history Over/Under structurally REQUIRE group familiarity (the rules report itself flags Hot Seat as 'awkward with strangers'). With 8 players and ~4 single-subject circles, there's a ~40% chance the stranger l
     FIX: Crew memory already knows who's new — use it. Auto-flag first-time names as 'Fresh Meat': (a) exclude them from knowledge-dependent subject roles for the night, (b) swap their {NAME} slots to first-impression card variants (which work great on strangers), (c) keep them full players in all anonymous-
  [MAJOR] Spec section 3 — host sets the depth ceiling publicly in the lobby
     WHAT: 'How deep does this group go?' is answered by one person in front of everyone with drinks flowing and UNHINGED branding on screen. Nobody vetoes down in that social context — the ceiling defaults to max, so the only spice gate silently fails in exactly the mixed-trust rooms (new partner, two couples) that need it. In the sim, Nick glanced around, said 'we're all adults,' and picked full depth; Jes
     FIX: Private consent vote: during lobby join, every player privately picks their max depth on their own phone; room ceiling = the minimum (or second-lowest to resist one troll), displayed only as the final depth with zero attribution. One screen, no content changes.
  [MAJOR] Spec section 4, game 2 — Fill-In Finisher reveal staging
     WHAT: Anonymous answers appear on ALL phones, the group votes, and only then does the Reader 'perform the winners aloud.' The laugh gets spent during ~40 seconds of the room silently reading 7 answers heads-down (asynchronous snorts, no shared beat), and the Reader's performance is a rerun of jokes everyone already consumed. The flagship Quiplash conversion currently deletes its own room moment.
     FIX: Answers render only on the Reader's phone. Reader performs each aloud; voters' phones show numbered vote tiles that unlock one at a time as the Reader taps through (Jackbox's exact staging). Voting opens only after the last answer is read. Heads stay up, delivery adds the second comedy layer the des
  [MAJOR] Spec section 2 — Stage mode ('candle on the table') vs host-as-player
     WHAT: The host's phone cannot be public table furniture and a private ballot simultaneously, and the spec never resolves this. In the sim, Nick grabbed the candle every input phase to cast his secret vote, visibly telegraphing his timing, then re-docked it — by circle 3 the table phone read as 'Nick's phone' and the shared-object illusion was dead.
     FIX: Sequence it: input phases open with a 6-second 'host votes first' window on the host phone, then the UI flips to stage visuals and Nick docks it; stage countdown/audio begins on dock. Alternatively demote the candle to audio-only (it's the speaker that matters) and let reveals live on the joiners' p
  [MAJOR] Spec section 6 — '+2 winner-aligned' scoring
     WHAT: Rewarding voters for aligning with the eventual majority corrupts every ballot: the rational vote is the predictable answer, not the funniest one (a metagame Jackbox deliberately avoids — voters earn nothing in Quiplash). It also means a passive player who never risks a spotlight can win the night: in the sim, Jess was crowned having performed exactly zero times, a deflating final beat for a game 
     FIX: Voters earn nothing (or +1 flat for participating). Points concentrate on performers/writers/subjects: winning punchlines, survived pitches, believed alibis, successful lies. Keep majority-alignment scoring only in Roast Consensus, where consensus literally is the game.
  [MAJOR] Spec section 4, game 4 — Confession or Cap single-deal at spice 5
     WHAT: One dealt confession forces a public truth-claim about (e.g.) sexual history in front of a partner, with no deniability structure. This is the structure demanding a 10/10 card — one that is spicy AND plausibly deniable for whoever drew it — on every single deal. Tom's round nearly detonated; only group goodwill absorbed it.
     FIX: Deal 3 confessions, confessor privately picks 1. Consent and strategy in one move: the player chooses the story that is ambiguous FOR THEM, which is also better gameplay (harder to read). Reduces the required card hit-rate from 100% to 'one of three.'
  MINOR count: 5

================================================================================
FAILURES (FATAL/MAJOR only):
  [FATAL] spec §4 game 7 — Hot Seat Imposter
     WHAT: At N=3 the game cannot function: Target is mute, Imposter performs, leaving exactly one asker/voter. A secret ballot of size 1 with a synced-reveal countdown is not a game — no jury tension, no spread, no reveal drama. The circle it occupies is 3 dead minutes.
     FIX: Set minPlayers=5 on Hot Seat Imposter (2+ askers, 3+ voters) and exclude it from any N<5 arc. Do not ship an N=3 variant — the role structure consumes too many bodies.
  [MAJOR] spec §4 + §10 (player range 3-10, no per-game gates)
     WHAT: The spec advertises 3 active players but defines zero per-game minimum-N metadata, so the pre-shuffled arc will deal mathematically broken games (Imposter, Roast, Fill-In as spec'd) at the advertised minimum. After honest gating, the N=3 pool is ~9 games for a 9-circle Full Damnation — zero slack, and the spec is silent on whether games may repeat within a Night.
     FIX: Add minN/maxN to every game's metadata; make the arc builder N-aware; define within-night repeat rules (allow proven repeaters like Scatterblast and Over/Under to appear twice at small N); ship N=3 variants for Roast and Fill-In (below).
  [MAJOR] spec §4 game 1 — Roast Consensus
     WHAT: Small-N math kills both promises: at N=3 every spread — (2,1,0) or (1,1,1) — lets every player deduce every vote (in the 2-1-0 case the 1-vote holder can only have been voted by the victim; in 1-1-1 each player's own vote resolves the cycle), so the secret ballot is void 100% of rounds. Room Heat needs 80% consensus but max consensus with a self-vote ban is (N-1)/N: 67% at N=3, 75% at N=4 — the ad
     FIX: Scale Room Heat to 'unanimous minus victim' at N≤5. At N≤4, stop pretending: flip the reveal to attributed open-roast mode ('FACE YOUR ACCUSERS' — show who voted for whom as the feature), which converts the deanonymization bug into on-brand content.
  [MAJOR] spec §4 game 2 — Fill-In Finisher
     WHAT: At N=3 the Quiplash structure collapses to a single judge: the two writers cannot self-vote so they must vote for each other (ballots cancel every round), the Reader decides 100% of outcomes, and each writer knows the other's authorship — the exact CAH-czar pathology the redesign cites research to escape.
     FIX: N=3 variant: drop the Reader role entirely — all 3 write, answers are performed aloud (rotating reader or the author's left neighbor), then all 3 vote among the other two answers, giving real (2,1,0)/(1,1,1) outcomes instead of a predetermined 2-1.
  [MAJOR] spec §4 game 2 — vote-then-perform ordering (all N)
     WHAT: Answers appear on phones for silent voting BEFORE the Reader performs the winner, so the room's first contact with every joke is heads-down and atomized; the aloud performance is a rerun of a joke everyone already read. This contradicts the project's own research (report_research_design-theory: someone else reading your anonymous joke ALOUD is the laugh engine — shared attention plus delivery).
     FIX: Invert the order: Reader performs EVERY answer aloud first (phones show only 'listen' + the answer currently being performed), then the vote opens. The laugh becomes a room moment and the vote becomes informed.
  [MAJOR] spec §4 game 8 — Poison Pitch (and any judge-of-2 game) at N=3
     WHAT: With one audience member, the 'secret vote + 3-2-1 synced reveal' reveals one named person's choice — secrecy is definitionally impossible with a single voter. Worse, it inverts the design goal: instead of removing social pressure, the ceremony spotlights the lone judge publicly ranking one friend over another.
     FIX: At N=3, replace winner-pick with a non-zero-sum verdict: the judge rates each pitch on a 1-5 'damage meter' (both pitchers can score), or add a layer where each pitcher secretly predicts the judge's pick for bonus points.
  [MAJOR] spec §3 — circle composition undefined
     WHAT: Rules define per-round loops of 20-90s (Roast 45s, Scatterblast 20-60s, Text Thread 60s) but §3 claims a circle is 3-4 min and Full Damnation is ~40 min. Nothing specifies prompts-per-circle, so as written a 9-circle night could run ~15 minutes, or an arc engine could stretch a 45s game past its fun length. The session-length promise is unimplementable from the spec.
     FIX: Define a per-game circle recipe: Roast Consensus = 3 prompts, Scatterblast = best-of-3 bombs, Over/Under = 2 subjects, Poison Pitch/Imposter = 1 loop. Validate that each recipe hits 3-4 min and the depth options sum to their advertised durations.
  [MAJOR] spec §2 — Stage phone vs host-as-player
     WHAT: The face-up mid-table 'candle' is also the host's ONLY private input device, and the host is a player in every circle. Every game where the host holds a secret (votes, confession lock, alibi words, self-rating — most of the deck) forces picking the stage phone up off the table or leaking the secret. At N=3 this collision happens every single circle, so table-stage mode is effectively unusable at t
     FIX: Stage layer renders public-only content, with an explicit choreographed 'HOST: pick up to answer' beat before stage-down each circle — or auto-disable table-stage below N=5 and keep stage audio on the in-hand host phone.
  [MAJOR] spec §4/§6 — even-jury tie handling
     WHAT: Every 1-performer verdict game at N=3 has a 2-voter jury, so 1-1 splits hit roughly half of all rounds — and the spec defines no outcome for a tied SMASH/PASS, SURVIVED/BLOCKED, or believe/cap vote, nor who is 'winner-aligned' for the +2. In simulation the first tie produced a confused 'so... who won?' beat that broke pacing.
     FIX: Codify 'performer wins ties' (rewards bold performance, on-brand) and give ties a named reveal state ('HUNG JURY') so the app narrates it instead of stalling.
  MINOR count: 4

FAILURES (FATAL/MAJOR only):
  [MAJOR] Spec section 2 (Stage design) + section 4 Scatterblast + Poison Pitch bell + 3-2-1 audio beats
     WHAT: Every shared-moment cue is audio-first from the stage phone, and the stated venue is a loud bar: the Scatterblast boom, the pitch bell, and countdown ticks are all inaudible. iOS Safari joiners also have no Vibration API. The shared-moment layer the spec leans on simply does not exist in its own target environment.
     FIX: Make every sync moment visual-first on ALL phones (full-screen flash for boom/bell/3-2-1, max brightness pulse), with audio as garnish and haptics host-Android-only. The synced-reveal machinery already exists — route the cues through it.
  [MAJOR] Spec section 4, game 2 (Fill-In Finisher phase order)
     WHAT: Answers render on all phones BEFORE voting and the Reader performs only the WINNERS after votes close. First contact with the funniest line happens silently and asynchronously across six screens (40s heads-down mid-party), and the aloud performance is a spoiled rerun. This inverts the anonymity+performance+shared-attention mechanism the design's own research identifies as the core of Quiplash.
     FIX: Flip the order: Reader performs ALL answers aloud from a teleprompter view on their phone; other phones show only a numbered ballot that unlocks when reading ends; then secret vote; then author + winner reveal.
  [MAJOR] Spec section 5 (pacing) — reveal-to-next-circle advancement is unspecified
     WHAT: If the server timer that 'advances without stragglers' also owns the reveal phase (the old app auto-advanced reveals in 5s), it will cut off the post-reveal table roast — which the simulation shows is where most actual laughter lives. The machine tramples the very moments it exists to create.
     FIX: Reveals hold until the host taps DESCEND (soft cap ~45s), with fire-reaction spam live during the hold. Server timers own input phases only; humans own aftermath.
  [MAJOR] Spec section 8 (Brimstone: 'skip being the subject')
     WHAT: Card-veto is anonymous but subject-skip cannot be — the room watches the game re-target, so the shy player's safety valve is a public flinch. Ana's guaranteed spotlight (fairness counter) plus a public-only opt-out reproduces the 'challenge cannot be refused' flaw the design's own research flags.
     FIX: Deliver subject assignments privately with a 5-second burn window BEFORE the room learns who was picked; on burn, re-deal silently. The skip becomes genuinely anonymous and the fairness counter just moves on.
  [MAJOR] Spec sections 1+2 (host is a player AND host phone is the Stage)
     WHAT: In 10 of 12 games the host has secret input (votes, roles, truth-locks), so the face-up mid-table stage phone must be grabbed and shielded nearly every round — either the secret leaks or the 'candle on the table' fiction breaks within two circles. In the bar simulation the Stage contributed nothing except during Scatterblast.
     FIX: Demote Stage to (a) an optional second device, or (b) an auto-mode: stage layout only during phases where the host holds no secret (Scatterblast, transitions, reveals), reverting to in-hand controller otherwise. Never design a phase assuming the stage phone is stationary.

  [MAJOR] Spec section 6 — '+2 winner-aligned' scoring

     FIX: Stage renders public-only phases and blanks with a 'Pick up your phone, Dex' prompt for host-private input; Reality Check consensus entry is always assigned to a named non-subject player's phone (e.g., the current scribe), never 'the stage phone'.

     WHAT: Some inputs are load-bearing ground truth and cannot be auto-played: Over/Under's true number (the subject must app-switch to Settings to fetch the receipt — guaranteed backgrounding of the one phone the round depends on) and Confession or Cap's truth-lock. Auto-advancing fabricates the fact the entire reveal hinges on, and the spec's own visibilitychange-resync makes this collision routine, not r
     FIX: Define two input classes: skippable (votes, writing) keep auto-advance; blocking (subject truths, confessor locks, role acknowledgments) pause the server timer with a public 'waiting on Sam…' shame countdown and stage audio — Jack Principles explicitly permits 'show the program is waiting.'
  [MAJOR] spec §4/§6 vote mechanics at small N (Poison Pitch, Hot Seat Imposter, Room Heat 80%)
     WHAT: At 4 players, two-performer games leave 2 voters: Poison Pitch ties ~50% of the time with no tie rule specced (the round ended in a shrug after two performances), 2-voter secret ballots aren't secret, and Room Heat's 80% threshold is unreachable (3/4 = 75%) so the bonus never fires at the game's minimum-viable group size of 3-5.
     FIX: N-aware math everywhere: Room Heat = N-1 of N when N≤5; ties trigger a 10s sudden-death rebuttal + revote; the arc shuffler down-weights two-performer games below 5 players (Hot Seat Imposter and Poison Pitch should be rare deals at N=4).
  [MAJOR] spec §3 diegetic spice curve: circles 1-2 locked at spice 2
     WHAT: No fast-path for already-warm groups. At N=3 this collision happens every single circle, so table-stage mode is effectively unusable at t
     FIX: Stage layer renders public-only content, with an explicit choreographed 'HOST: pick up to answer' beat before stage-down each circle — or auto-disable table-stage below N=5 and keep stage audio on the in-hand host phone.
  [MAJOR] spec §4/§6 — even-jury tie handling
     WHAT: Every 1-performer verdict game at N=3 has a 2-voter jury, so 1-1 splits hit roughly half of all rounds — and the spec defines no outcome for a tied SMASH/PASS, SURVIVED/BLOCKED, or believe/cap vote, nor who is 'winner-aligned' for the +2. In simulation the first tie produced a confused 'so... who won?' beat that broke pacing.
     FIX: Codify 'performer wins ties' (rewards bold performance, on-brand) and give ties a named reveal state ('HUNG JURY') so the app narrates it instead of stalling.
  MINOR count: 4

input.content:
# HELLDECK 2.0 — Gameplay Model Spec v2 ("The Descent")

_v2 incorporates fixes from 6 simulated playtests (roommates4, houseparty8, trio3, bar6, birthday13, internetfriends5). Changes from v1 marked ★._

## 0. Identity constraints (non-negotiable)
- UNHINGED, never WHOLESOME. Dive bar with great lighting; group chat that went too far at 2am; a roast where everyone's in on it.
- The game is a machine for making FRIENDS funny about EACH OTHER. Screens carry secrets and votes; mouths carry the comedy. The phone is never the performer.
- Cards reference REAL player names, not seats.

## 1. Platform model
- **Host**: native Android app (paid unlock). Creates a "Night," gets a 4-letter room code + QR.
- **Joiners**: any phone browser via QR/code URL. No install, no account, name + avatar. PWA prompt only AFTER a completed night.
- **Backend**: one Cloudflare Durable Object per room (free tier), WebSocket relay. Host app authoritative; DO persists room state (rejoin = same code + same name, seat held 90s).
- Host is also a player.
- ★ **Every sync moment is VISUAL-FIRST on all phones** (full-screen flash/pulse for booms, bells, 3-2-1 counts); audio from the host phone is garnish, never load-bearing (bars are loud; iOS Safari has no vibration API).

## 2. The Stage (host phone mid-table) — ★ reworked
- All phones run synchronized phases; reveals render on EVERY phone simultaneously.
- **Stage mode is optional and adaptive**, default ON at N≥5, OFF below (in-hand host phone still plays audio):
  - Stage face renders PUBLIC-ONLY content: timers, tally counts ("4/7 votes in"), reveal spectacle, transitions.
  - ★ **Lift-to-sin**: when the host has private input, the stage shows "NICK — PICK UP TO SIN." Lifting flips to the host's private ballot (accelerometer/manual tap fallback); laying it flat restores the public face and starts stage countdown/audio. Signature mechanic, choreographed: host votes in a dedicated 6s window so the grab never leaks timing.
  - No phase may ever ASSUME the stage phone is stationary or host-readable-only.
- Consensus/scribe entries (e.g. Reality Check group number) are ★ always assigned to a named NON-SUBJECT player's phone, never "the stage phone."

## 3. Session structure — "The Descent"
- Lobby: group picks depth — **Quick Dip (5 circles), Standard Descent (7), Full Damnation (9)**.
- ★ **Private consent, not public bravado**: during join, EVERY player privately picks their max depth on their own phone; the room ceiling = the second-lowest pick (min at N≤4). Displayed only as the final depth, zero attribution. Nobody has to be the coward out loud.
- ★ **Vibe check sets the STARTING rung**: host answers "How's the room right now? SOBER / WARMED UP / FERAL" — feral starts at spice 3; sober starts at 2. Escalation to the ceiling still happens (36-questions ramp preserved).
- ★ **Circle recipes** (a circle = 3-4 min, arithmetic honest): Roast Consensus = 3 prompts; Scatterblast = best-of-3 bombs; Over/Under = 2 subjects; Fill-In = 1 setup (N≤6) or 2 parallel setups (N≥7); single-performer games = 1 loop. Advertised durations: 5 circles ≈ 18-22 min, 7 ≈ 26-32 min, 9 ≈ 34-42 min.
- Arc shuffler is ★ N-aware and familiarity-aware (see §4 gates, §7 Fresh Meat): opens with simultaneous-input games, physical spike mid-descent, spotlight games once warm.
- ★ **Finale (final circle, ×3)**: restricted to all-players-score games (Roast Consensus, Fill-In Finisher, Over/Under) at the night's max spice. THE JUDGMENT follows: winner + telemetry superlatives + ★ a screenshot-shaped share card (share artifact is the viral loop).
- No elimination. ★ Devil's Bargain (last place hidden ×2) attaches only to circles where every player can score, and is REVEALED with drama at Judgment ("SAM SOLD THEIR SOUL AT CIRCLE 6"), never silently mutated into scores.
- Within-night repeats allowed at small N for proven repeaters (Scatterblast, Over/Under, Roast) with fresh content; never repeat single-performer games.

## 4. Games (12 active; per-game minN/maxN gates ★)
**Class A — simultaneous private input:**
1. **Roast Consensus** (minN 3) — 3 prompts/circle; secret victim vote; synced reveal, victim's name in lights. ★ Room Heat scales: unanimity-minus-victim at N≤5, 60% at N≥8. ★ Plurality tie = "THE ROOM IS TORN — DOUBLE ROAST" (both names in lights, both roasted, everyone who voted either gets the point). ★ At N=3-4 flips to attributed open-roast mode: "FACE YOUR ACCUSERS" (votes shown with names — deanonymization becomes the feature).
2. **Fill-In Finisher** (minN 3) — ★ ORDER FIXED (perform-then-vote): everyone but the Reader writes; answers go ONLY to the Reader's teleprompter view, one at a time; Reader performs each aloud (all phones show just "LISTEN UP" + current answer number, sting between answers); vote grid unlocks on ALL phones only after the last read; secret vote (no self-votes); winner + author reveal. Panic button = 2 curated fallbacks at half points. ★ N≥7: two parallel setups, writers split, head-to-head face-offs (Reader performs BOTH, phones show only VOTE A/VOTE B). ★ N=3: no Reader — each answer read aloud by its author's left neighbor, all 3 vote among the other two.
3. **Over/Under** (minN 4) — 2 subjects/circle; line debated ALOUD, secret bets, subject enters the true number. ★ The receipt fetch (app-switch to Settings/screen-time) is a BLOCKING input: server timer pauses with a public "WAITING ON SAM'S RECEIPTS…" shame countdown + stage sting; auto-advance never fabricates ground truth.
4. **Confession or Cap** (minN 4) — ★ confessor is dealt 3 confessions, privately picks 1 (consent + strategy + card hit-rate now "best of 3"); locks TRUE/FALSE; tells it aloud; jury secretly votes; synced reveal. ★ Jury tie = confessor wins ("HUNG JURY — THE LIAR WALKS").
5. **Alibi Drop** (minN 4) — words delivered only to the accused; alibi aloud; jury privately picks 3 suspected planted words from an 8-word lineup. ★ Reveal is stage-sequenced word-by-word (each planted word confirmed with a sting, glanceable one-word-at-a-time on all phones), not a text-grid dump.
6. **Taboo Timer** (minN 4) — clue-giver + designated Buzzer phone (sees forbidden list); guessers see timer only.
7. **Hot Seat Imposter** (★ minN 5) — private roles; askers get suggested questions; secret REAL/FAKE vote. Rare below N=6.

**Class B — verbal performance (phones gate + vote):** ★ All Class B use spotlight valves (§5).
8. **Poison Pitch** (minN 4) — private assignments; pitches aloud; secret vote. ★ Tie = 10s sudden-death rebuttal each, revote; second tie = split pot. ★ At N=4 (2 voters) down-weighted in arc; ★ N=3 variant: judge rates each pitch 1-5 on a "damage meter" (non-zero-sum) + pitchers secretly predict their own score for bonus.
9. **Red Flag Rally** (minN 4) — defense aloud; secret SMASH/PASS; ★ tie = SMASH (performer wins ties, bold is rewarded).
10. **Text Thread Trap** (minN 4) — performer reads incoming text aloud, improvises reply in mandatory tone; SURVIVED/BLOCKED vote; ★ tie = SURVIVED.
11. **Reality Check** (minN 4) — subject privately self-rates; group debate stays FULLY ALOUD in front of them; ★ group number entered by a named non-subject scribe; synced ego-gap reveal.

**Class C — stage games:**
12. **Scatterblast** (minN 3) — stage phone (or any volunteered phone) is the bomb; category+letter on all phones; ★ boom attribution: on boom, all phones instantly show "WHO DIED?" — everyone taps the holder's name (majority = truth, 5s); disputes become part of the bit ("THE TABLE HAS SPOKEN").

**Cut/manual:** Title Fight = host-triggered "Fight Break" between circles only. Unifying Theory = cut from v1 (future pack).

## 5. Pacing rules (Jack Principles, server-enforced) — ★ reworked
- One task per phone per moment; ALL input simultaneous; no sequential turns.
- ★ **Two input classes**: SKIPPABLE (votes, punchlines — auto-abstain/auto-panic on timer, game never waits) vs BLOCKING (truth-locks, subject numbers, role acks — timer pauses, public "WAITING ON X…" shame countdown with sting; Jack Principles permits "show the program is waiting").
- ★ **Server owns inputs; humans own aftermath**: reveals HOLD until the host taps DESCEND (soft cap 45s, fire-reaction spam live during the hold). The post-reveal table roast is where the actual laughter lives — the machine never tramples it.
- ★ **Spotlight valves (Class B)**: (1) volunteer-first — 5s "WHO WANTS BLOOD?" claim button before random assignment (weighted toward players without a spotlight yet); (2) audience pile-on SKIP-'EM — if 60% tap it during a stalled performance, re-deal with a mocking sting (a stall becomes a laugh at the game's expense); (3) private pre-assignment burn window (§8).
- Explainers: first appearance for this GROUP = 15s how-it-works; after = 5s title card.
- Reveal grammar: any reveal payload must be glanceable in <2s (a name, a number, a verdict). Anything longer gets stage-sequenced beats.

## 6. Scoring — ★ reworked
- ★ **Points concentrate on the bold**: winning punchlines, survived pitches, believed lies, planted words found/missed — performers, writers, subjects. Voters get nothing for alignment (kills the predictable-vote metagame); flat +1 participation for voting at all.
- Exception: Roast Consensus keeps majority-alignment scoring (consensus IS that game) with Room Heat bonus.
- Finale ×3; Devil's Bargain per §3. Descent Rank ladder shown 5s between circles.
- Feedback invisible: 🔥 reaction spam during reveals doubles as telemetry. No feedback phase, ever.

## 7. Personalization & memory
- {NAME} slots use real lobby names; spotlight-fairness counter spreads targeting.
- ★ **Fresh Meat protocol**: first-time names (crew memory knows) are excluded from knowledge-dependent subject roles (Hot Seat, Reality Check, personal-history Over/Under) for the night, and their {NAME} slots draw first-impression card variants ("Who's most likely to have already judged everyone's shoes?"). Full players in all anonymous-input games from circle 1.
- ★ **IRL-familiarity gate**: lobby asks (once per crew) "Has this crew hung out IRL?" Online-first crews get persona/phone-legible card pools (screen time, group-chat behavior) and Hot Seat/Reality Check gated out.
- Crew memory across nights: past superlatives, roast history, win records; callbacks appear as content. Stored in host app + DO room state.
- Generation-time AI (cloud, pennies/session) = later content-refresh only; never runtime dependency, never the joke author.

## 8. Safety valves — ★ reworked (anonymity made real)
- **Brimstone tokens, 2/player/night**: burn to veto a card or dodge a spotlight.
  - ★ **Subject pre-view**: any card naming a subject renders on the SUBJECT's phone 3s before the room, with a silent BURN option — on burn, the room just sees a normal next deal. No animation, no trace, no counter shown for subject-burns. (Room-visible burn animation only for generic non-targeted cards, where it's fun.)
  - ★ **Spotlight burn window**: performer assignments are delivered privately 5s BEFORE the room learns who was picked; burning re-deals silently.
  - ★ **No hoarding reward** (v1's +1 punished the sensitive). Tokens are use-or-lose; unused = nothing.
- Depth ceiling by private consent vote (§3). No card targets appearance, family death, or protected traits (writing-level rule).

## 9. Monetization
- Free download; new host's first full Night free; **$9.99 one-time "Host the Descent" unlock** (Play Billing; web checkout link-out later). Joiners always free. Content packs ($2.99-4.99) later. No subscription. No ads ever.
- ★ Purchase moment is AFTER a night ends (on the Judgment screen of a free night: "Host your next descent — $9.99"), never mid-party.

## 10. Failure handling & scale
- Disconnect: seat held 90s (skippable inputs auto-play; blocking inputs shame-countdown), rejoin same name+code. Wake-lock + visibilitychange resync on web.
- Host phone dies: DO holds Night state 10 min; host relaunch restores at current circle.
- ★ **Players 3-12 active** (systems N-scaled per §4) + **Imps with citizenship**: latecomers join as Imps; Imp names ENTER the {NAME} card pool (roastable = included), Imps vote at 0.5 weight, fuel Room Heat, and write punchlines in an audience bracket at N≥7 Fill-In; conversion to full player at next circle ONLY if active count <12, else they stay Imps with a guaranteed cameo (one card names them before Night's end).

Stopwatch: 15 explainer + 60 write + 4×(20 read-pair + 10 vote + 3 beat + 45 hold) = 6:27 with per-face-off holds, 5:00 even with one final hold — 25-61% over the 4-min cap, and 80-120s of one amateur's voice. §5's SKIP-'EM valve is explicitly Class-B-only, so a mumbling Reader has no escape. §10 makes it worse: Imp audience-bracket punchlines feed the SAME Reader's stack, unbounded. N=6 is fine (5 answers ≈ 50-75s, circle ≈ 3:20).","fix":"At N≥7 use TWO Readers, one per setup, alternating face-offs (halves solo time, doubles spotlight coverage); extend SKIP-'EM to Reader performances; cap Imp bracket at 1 curated answer, shown on-screen as a cameo, never performed."},{"claim":"Full Damnation's advertised 34-42 min is unreachable — the arithmetic is top-loaded on max reveal-holds","severity":"MAJOR","evidence":"Summing recipes at N=6 with EVERY hold ridden to the 45s soft cap: Quick Dip 20:28 (advert 18-22 ✓), Standard 26:08 (26-32, floor barely ✓), Full Damnation 31:23 — 7.7% UNDER the advertised FLOOR even at theoretical max. Realistic 20s-average holds: ~17 / ~22 / ~27 min; midpoint-to-midpoint the 9-circle claim is off ~26% (>25% threshold). Cause: 9 circles force in the short single-performer recipes (Confession 2:15, Reality 2:25, Alibi 2:50, Red Flag 2:20 — all violating the 'circle = 3-4 min' invariant from below), and 'never repeat single-performer games' prevents padding.","fix":"Either re-advertise Full Damnation as 28-34 min, or fix the short recipes: single-performer circles run 2 loops with 2 DIFFERENT performers (Confession = 2 confessors, Text Thread = 2 texts, Reality = 2 subjects) — hits 3-4 min AND doubles spotlight distribution. State all durations 'gameplay, excluding ~3 min lobby.'"},{"claim":"★ Lift-to-sin is only specified for 6s ballots, but the host has LONG private inputs in half the games — and the choreography serializes the room behind the host","severity":"MAJOR","evidence":"§2: 'laying it flat restores the public face and starts stage countdown.' Applied literally to Fill-In, the room's 60s write timer cannot start until the host finishes their own 60s write on the lifted stage phone: +60s serialization per Fill-In circle. Undefined entirely for: host as Over/Under subject (receipt fetch = app-switch ON the stage phone), host as Taboo clue-giver (60s with forbidden list = no stage all round), host as confessor (25s pick-of-3). Even votes-only, ~15 lift events per 7-circle night × 6-10s = 1.5-2.5 min of pure choreography, a toll booth at the top of every input phase by circle 4.","fix":"Lift-to-sin = short ballots ONLY, and run it CONCURRENT with the room's window (host's 6s window is the first 6s of everyone's countdown, not before it). For long-form host inputs the stage auto-suspends to a public 'THE HOST IS SINNING' banner + room timer; if host is a receipt-fetch subject, the fetch happens after laying the phone back flat is disallowed — banner persists until re-lift-and-confirm."},{"claim":"Fill-In vote grid content is unspecified — voting from memory of 5-8 heard-once punchlines collapses into recency bias","severity":"MINOR","evidence":"§4.2: phones show 'just LISTEN UP + current answer number' during performance, then 'vote grid unlocks.' If the grid shows only numbers, voters must recall 5 (N=6) or 8 (N=9) answers heard once, aloud, in a bar — the last answer read wins systematically. If it shows full texts, that must be said, since it reintroduces 15-20s of heads-down re-reading (acceptable as memory aid post-performance, but it is unbudgeted time). Also: the Reader has zero task for the entire 60s write phase.","fix":"Specify: grid shows answer texts as memory aids, ordered randomly per phone; budget +15s. Give the Reader a job during the write: pick the sting/intro tone for the read ('read these as: a eulogy / a 911 call'), which also raises performance quality."},{"claim":"Silent burn chains produce unexplained dead air with no cover state — the anonymity leaks through the pacing","severity":"MINOR","evidence":"§8: spotlight burn = 5s private window, re-deal 'silently'; next performer gets another 5s window and can also burn. Each chained burn = ~10s where every phone shows nothing happening. Two chained burns = 20-30s of limbo that is DISTINGUISHABLE from a normal deal (normal = instant), so the room infers someone burned — defeating the no-trace guarantee. Base valve overhead itself is fine: 5s WHO-WANTS-BLOOD + 5s burn window × 2-3 Class B rounds ≈ 30s/night, and subject pre-views (3s × ~6/night) ≈ 20s — negligible.","fix":"Give every deal a variable-length cover animation (3-15s randomized 'consulting the ledger…' even when nobody burns) so burn-limbo is statistically indistinguishable and has a face. Cap SKIP-'EM at one re-deal per circle to bound the +45-70s replacement-performance cost."},{"claim":"The bar6 dead-timer flaw survived into v2: performers who finish early still stand in dead air","severity":"MINOR","evidence":"bar6 playtest (MINOR, 'no I rest my case button exists') is not fixed anywhere in spec v2. Red Flag: 15s defense against a 45s fixed timer = 30s dead air. The only v2 valve, SKIP-'EM (§5), fires at 60% pile-on and RE-DEALS with a mocking sting — the wrong verb for a finished performance; it punishes completion instead of advancing to the vote.","fix":"Performer gets an 'I REST MY CASE' button that kills their own timer and unlocks the ballot (the bell moment). SKIP-'EM stays reserved for genuine stalls."}],"summary":"Stopwatch audit at N=6 using FEATURES.md timers + spec v2 constants (15s explainers, 5s ladder, 45s soft-cap holds, 6s lift-to-sin, 5s+5s valves). python3 << 'EOF'
import json, os
with open('/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-nicholastoledo-Development-active-HELLDECK/4eb51e65-3e38-49e9-b745-6d128c919d2d/tasks/wwrj0myl0.output') as f:
    data = json.load(f)
res = data['result']
outdir = '/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-nicholastoledo-Development-active-HELLDECK/4eb51e65-3e38-49e9-b745-6d128c919d2d/scratchpad'
for k, v in res.items():
    if v is None: print(f"{k}: NULL"); continue
    fn = os.path.join(outdir, f"r2_{k.replace(':','_')}.json")
    with open(fn, 'w') as f: json.dump(v, f, indent=1)
    if 'verdict' in v and 'kill_shots' in v:
        sev = [ks['severity'] for ks in v['kill_shots']]
        print(f"{k}: {v['verdict']} | kill_shots: FATAL={sev.count('FATAL')} MAJOR={sev.count('MAJOR')} MINOR={sev.count('MINOR')}")
    else:
        print(f"{k}: verdict={v.get('group_verdict')} fun={v.get('fun_score_1_10')} (v1 was: trio3 5.5, bar6 6.5, birthday13 6.5) again={v.get('would_play_again')} pay={v.get('would_someone_pay_to_host')} fixed_from_v1={len(v.get('fixed_from_v1',[]))} new_failures={sum(1 for fl in v.get('failures',[]) if fl['severity'] in ('FATAL','MAJOR'))}maj/{len(v.get('failures',[]))}tot")
EOF

import json
base = '/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-nicholastoledo-Development-active-HELLDECK/4eb51e65-3e38-49e9-b745-6d128c919d2d/scratchpad'

        if ks['severity'] in ('FATAL','MAJOR'):

================================================================================
attack_social  [HOLDS_WITH_FIXES]
================================================================================

[FATAL] The 3-second subject pre-view fails exactly when it matters: a tipsy subject cannot read, parse 'this is about ME', risk-evaluate, and find BURN in 3s — and the dangerous cards are the slow ones.
  EVIDENCE: Sober adults read ~3-4 words/sec; spice-5 subject cards in the corpus run 12-20 words ('Number of people in this room they've thought about hooking up with' + subject banner). Add alcohol, a loud room, and the fact that a genuinely threatening card triggers deliberation/freeze, not reflex — the valve fires reliably for harmless cards and misses landmines. houseparty8's FATAL finding (Tom playing the grenade because the valve was worse than the ca
  FIX: Kill the fixed 3s. Wrap every subject deal in a fixed-length room-side ritual (5-6s 'THE DECK IS CHOOSING ITS VICTIM' roulette on all phones — on-brand dread, cover, and reading time), with the subject's window ending on ACTION or generous timeout, never at 3s. Better: deal 2 cards to the subject, they pick which one the room sees (auto-pick at tim

[MAJOR] A burn leaks anyway through timing and faces: burn → re-deal → second 3s pre-view produces an observable double-length deal gap, and the subject's 3 seconds of private squinting during a heads-up hold is read by everyone — especially partners.
  EVIDENCE: §8 gives every subject card a 3s stagger (good, uniform), but a burn necessarily restarts the window: the room learns the deal rhythm within two circles and a 6s+ gap = 'someone burned.' If the re-dealt card names a DIFFERENT subject, the prior likely subject is the obvious suspect; if it's visibly softer on the SAME subject, worse ('what is Jess hiding' — the exact failure houseparty8 documented, resurrected via timing side-channel). Meanwhile t
  FIX: Fixed-duration deal ceremony on room phones that absorbs exactly one burn cycle with zero timing delta (pre-fetch the backup card into the same window). Deliver pre-views only during heads-down phases (while the room is on its own deal ceremony), never during a heads-up hold. Re-deals must preserve the subject-vs-generic mix so distribution doesn't

  EVIDENCE: §3 displays 'only the final depth, zero attribution' — but a room that hyped Full Damnation out loud and sees a capped number will ask 'ok who picked 3?' out loud (bar6 sim: Nick yells 'all the way'; the same Nick reads the capped screen and hunts). Anonymity of one aggregate number against known group priors is no anonymity: everyone already knows who the 'likely' low vote is, the suspect can't prove innocence, and the accusation hangs over all

Nobody can distinguish 'capped at 3' from 'the deck just dealt that way' — and invisible subject-burns already add cover noise. Deniability requires the number to not exi

  FIX: Split ceilings by target: room ceiling (second-lowest) governs GENERIC cards; any card naming subject X obeys X's own private pick. Invisible (server-side targeting filter), implementable, and it dissolves both 'one prude

  FIX: Every BLOCKING subject input permanently carries a 'PLEAD THE FIFTH' option: forfeit the round's points, outward-aimed sting ('THE WITNESS TAKES THE FIFTH. [FATAL] Twelve games get built twice: every game UI must exist in the greenfield web client (joiners) AND in Kotlin/Compose (host is a player with full private-input UIs plus stage faces). Every ★ rule tweak lands in two codebases and drifts.
  EVIDENCE: Spec §1/§2: joiners are browser-only, host is native and also a player, stage renders its own faces. Infra report: 50.8k lines Kotlin across 180 files, ZERO web/JS/KMP code, zero networking — the web side is 100% greenfield. §4 alone contains 7+ per-game tie-break/order rules (Fill-In perform-then-vote ordering, double-roast, hung jury, N=3 variants) that must render identically on both stacks. That is ~1.8x build cost and permanent 2x QA on bar-
  FIX: One game-UI runtime: the web client. Kotlin host app becomes a thin shell (Play Billing, room create, foreground service, accelerometer JS-bridge, stage audio) hosting the SAME web bundle in a WebView — the AirConsole/Jackbox-controller pattern. Keep the joiner bundle <200KB so bar-LTE join stays <2s. Explicitly do NOT reach for Compose Multiplatfo

[FATAL] "Host app authoritative" contradicts the spec's own failure modes: the authority is a phone that backgrounds, and host is a player who app-switches.
  EVIDENCE: Spec §1 (host authoritative, DO persists) vs §10 (host dies → DO restores) vs §3 (host can be the Over/Under subject who app-switches to Settings for receipts). When the host backgrounds their own app — receipt fetch, phone call, notification — Android suspends the room's brain for all 12+ players. On host crash, any inputs received since the last DO mirror are lost, producing lost/double votes on restore. And every joiner resync (§10 visibilityc
  FIX: Make the Durable Object the single authoritative rules engine (TypeScript); the host app is a privileged client with host-only controls (DESCEND, void-round, stage claim). All phones become dumb renderers of DO state. This also collapses the double-build in kill-shot #1: one rules engine, one UI runtime, native shell for platform services.

[MAJOR] Lift-to-sin leaks private ballots and destroys the §8 silent-burn guarantee whenever the host is the target.
  EVIDENCE: The stage lies face-up mid-table. A false trigger (wobbly table producing sustained tilt, a non-host grab, drink slam) flips the HOST'S PRIVATE BALLOT onto the public face for the whole table; the flip-down arc shows the ballot mid-swing before any threshold settles. Missed triggers are ironic: the privacy-natural grip — lift low and flat, shielded — is exactly the motion a tilt threshold cannot see. Worst: §8 subject pre-view (3s early, silent b
  FIX: (a) Detect orientation from the low-passed gravity vector with a baseline calibrated at stage-engage (bar tables aren't level); show private face at >50° sustained 250ms; hide INSTANTLY at first downward motion below ~40° (asymmetric hysteresis). (b) Never render the ballot directly: lift shows an 'EYES OFF' shield, thumb-hold-to-reveal shows the b

[MAJOR] "Reveals render on EVERY phone simultaneously" has no synchronization mechanism anywhere in the spec, and naive WS broadcast on bar LTE skews 100-500ms+.
  EVIDENCE: Spec §2 asserts simultaneity; nothing specifies how. Crowded-venue LTE WS jitter runs p95 200-500ms across mixed carriers/wifi. Phones inches apart flashing >150ms apart read as broken; >400ms murders the 3-2-1 and the Scatterblast boom→'WHO DIED?' attribution (wrong holder blamed systematically, and not as a bit). Per-tick countdown messages drift audibly. Event-based reveals are also unrecoverable: a phone reconnecting 2s late missed the boom e
  FIX: (1) NTP-style clock offset per client over the WS (5 pings, median) refreshed periodically. (2) Server schedules reveals ~1s ahead at server-time T; each client renders at its local mapping of T — achievable skew ~30-80ms, p95 <150ms. (3) Countdowns derive from the synced clock and a single deadline, never per-tick messages. (4) Reveals are idempot

[MAJOR] Blocking input + permanently dead phone = the whole room deadlocks forever; the spec provides no escape hatch.
  EVIDENCE: Spec §3: 'auto-advance never fabricates ground truth' (Over/Under). §5: BLOCKING pauses the server timer. §10: seat held 90s. Walk it: Sam's battery dies inside Settings during the receipt fetch → shame countdown runs → 90s seat lapses → the timer is still paused, ground truth can never arrive, and no rule anywhere advances or voids the round. Same for a confessor truth-lock or role-ack. Twelve people stare at 'WAITING ON SAM'S RECEIPTS…' until t
  FIX: When a seat lapses during a BLOCKING input, unlock a host-only control: 'THE WITNESS FLED — VOID THE ROUND' (mocking sting, no points, arc deals a replacement round) plus optional swap-subject where rules allow. Human escape, never fabricated truth — consistent with the game's voice.

  EVIDENCE: Research report: iOS kills WS ~30-60s after backgrounding; iOS also jettisons the tab under memory pressure (a 'camera roll count' receipt opens Photos — prime jettison trigger). Walk: subject returns → (a) if jettisoned, full page reload; with session identity only in JS memory they land on the join form and must retype code+name while the room sits in a shame countdown; (b) if not jettisoned, the frozen setTimeout/rAF queue thaws and fires PRE-
  FIX: (1) Room code lives in the URL; player token in localStorage; page load silently auto-rejoins with zero typing (spec §10's 'rejoin = same code + same name' must mean automatic, and say so). (2) Epoch counter on every scheduled render; bump epoch on any disconnect/hidden event; discard stale-epoch callbacks on thaw. (3) On visible: freeze into a 'RE

[MAJOR] Free-tier arithmetic: the binding constraint is DURATION, not messages — a hard ceiling of ~43 room-nights per UTC day, and past any cap Cloudflare FAILS operations mid-party rather than billing.
  EVIDENCE: An active room never idles long enough to hibernate (inputs/timers every few seconds), so a 40-min night bills ~2,400s × 0.125GB = 300 GB-s against the 13,000 GB-s/day free duration cap → ~43 nights/day ceiling regardless of message discipline. Requests are the second cliff: uncoalesced fire-spam at 12 players + 20 Imps = 32 devices × 4 taps/s × 45s hold × ~27 reveal moments ≈ 155k incoming msgs/night = ~7.8k billable requests (20:1) against 100k
  FIX: Fine for playtests and soft launch; make the $5/mo Workers Paid flip a LAUNCH PRECONDITION, not a reaction (one $9.99 host unlock covers two months; at paid rates a night costs ~$0.004 of duration). Instrument request/duration counters per room from day one and alert at 60% of daily caps. Coalescing (kill-shot #9) is still mandatory for client sani

[MAJOR] DO hibernation/eviction silently eats the 90s seat-hold and the 10-min host-death restore if they're in-memory timers, and host-death restore loses the round unless full state is persisted per-mutation.
  EVIDENCE: Spec §10 names both timers but the DO can be evicted from memory on hibernation or at Cloudflare's discretion; in-memory setTimeout and un-persisted state vanish. The host-death path triggers this exactly: host phone dies → players pocket phones → all WS idle/drop → DO hibernates within seconds → host relaunches at minute 9 and the DO wakes with only whatever hit SQLite. 'Restore at current circle' is worthless if mid-round votes, Devil's Bargain
  FIX: (1) All deadlines (seat-hold, host-death, phase timers) via the DO Alarms API — persisted, survives eviction (SQLite-backed DOs, which is the free-tier default). (2) Write the full round-state snapshot to SQLite storage on every mutation; in-memory state is pure cache. (3) Heartbeats via setWebSocketAutoResponse ping/pong so keepalives neither wake

[MAJOR] Uncoalesced reaction spam floods the clients worse than the server: 128 msg/s in, ~4,100 msg/s fanned out, animated on mid-tier Androids in battery saver at the night's emotional peak.
  EVIDENCE: Spec §5 makes fire-spam live during the 45s reveal hold and §6 makes it the telemetry channel — so it can't be dropped. 32 devices × 4 taps/s = 128 inbound msg/s; naive per-tap fan-out = 32× that outbound. Outgoing is free in dollars but not in phone CPU: a $150 Android at 15% battery rendering 128 discrete reaction events/s during the reveal will jank and thermal-throttle exactly when the room is watching. It's also the dominant term in the requ
  FIX: Taps animate LOCALLY at zero latency (the tapper needs feedback, not the network). Client coalesces to 1 msg per 500ms carrying tap-count. DO aggregates and broadcasts room heat at ≤4 Hz as count buckets — §6 telemetry survives intact as counts. Per-client accounting cap ~10 taps/s. Reaction cost drops ~40x with zero perceptible loss.

[FATAL] Android-only host severs the entire growth engine. The viral loop the pricing report prescribes (joiner plays free at someone's party -> 'I want to host this' -> pays $9.99) dies at the platform gate for ~75-80% of prospects: US under-35s are ~78% iPhone (Piper Sandler teens 87%; 18-29 ~75-80%). The hot lead is always an individual, not a group, so 3 of 4 conversion attempts bounce at 'sorry, Android only.' Group-level it's also bad: P(zero Android in a group of 6) = 0.78^6 ≈ 22% under independence, but iMessage homophily clusters friend groups by device — realistically 30-40% of under-30 US groups have zero Android, and 'zero WILLING host' (owns Android AND pays AND surrenders phone to the table as Stage) is plausibly 45-55%. You are asking the group's least-likely ringleader to be your only buyer.
  EVIDENCE: spec_v2.md §1 ('Host: native Android app (paid unlock)') + §9 (Play Billing). report_research_pricing-model.md: 'the only conversion needed is I want to host this myself' — that conversion is impossible for ~4 in 5 of the target demo. report_research_browser-vs-app.md already proves the fix is cheap: the DO relay is mandatory anyway, wake lock works on iOS Safari 16.4+, and Google Play policy is irrelevant to web-sold hosting. Jackbox/Kahoot/Deat
  FIX: Move game-loop authority from the Kotlin app into the Durable Object (host-logic-on-server) and ship a browser host page — any iPhone can then host via web checkout (Stripe ~3% beats Play's 15%). Cost ranking: (1) server-authoritative + web host: ~2-4 wks incremental NOW, arguably negative net cost pre-build (one TypeScript game loop instead of Kot

[MAJOR] The $9.99 trigger is placed at the moment of minimum need. Walk it: 11:40pm, Judgment screen mid-table, share card circulating, Ubers ordered. The only person who CAN buy (the host) is drunk, satiated, and just consumed the free night — willingness-to-pay is at its floor, and the upsell pollutes the emotional peak that IS the viral share artifact. Meanwhile the spec never defines what stops reinstall = infinite free nights.
  EVIDENCE: spec_v2.md §9: 'Purchase moment is AFTER a night ends (on the Judgment screen of a free night).' The pricing report's own Option B logic contradicts this: passes are 'bought at the moment of party... purchase must be <30 seconds' — i.e., buy at blocked intent, not at satisfaction. Heads Up! burstiness data (300k/day baseline vs 5.5M Christmas) means 2-8 weeks elapse before need returns; a Judgment-screen impression is long forgotten.
  FIX: Split the trigger. Judgment screen = seed only: 'Your crew's sins are saved' + share card that carries a 'host your own' link on EVERY joiner's phone (this is where the Jackbox demo loop actually converts — and it requires kill-shot #1's browser hosting to work). Hard paywall = second night's 'Begin the Descent' button, with the lobby already assem

[MAJOR] Solo-authored 1,200-card corpus at Jackbox/CAH writer-room quality is not credible, and a weekly crew exhausts the context-effective pool in ~4-8 nights. Consumption math: a 7-circle Standard night burns ~25-30 content units (Roast=3 prompts x ~2 appearances, O/U=2 subjects, Scatterblast=3 bombs, Confession deals 3 to use 1, plus Brimstone burns and panic fallbacks). The 1,200 corpus partitions hard: 12 per-game pools (~100 each), then by spice tier (a night's ramp touches ~3 of 5 tiers), then by Fresh Meat / IRL-familiarity variants — a given crew's drawable pool per game per context is realistically 20-40 cards. Template shapes ({NAME} 'most likely to...') get recognized before literal repeats appear (CAH decay mechanism). Production side: CAH's ~550 cards took 8 writers years; at a sustained ~10 keepers/day (after Jackbox's 'reject any prompt you can't answer 3 ways fast' cull), 1,200 cards = ~6 months of pure writing on top of building the entire product solo.
  EVIDENCE: spec_v2.md §3 circle recipes + §7 pool partitioning + §7 'Generation-time AI... later content-refresh only.' report_research_design-theory.md: CAH decays via deck memorization; ~95% of party-game design effort is prompts; LLM humor is formulaic (90% of ChatGPT jokes = 25 templates). report_read_rules-interactions.md confirms the 3-tier stack (LLM -> gold -> template) already exists.
  FIX: Cut launch to 8 games with the 4 proven repeaters (Roast, Fill-In, O/U, Scatterblast) 150+ deep instead of 12 x 100 thin. Lean on structurally cheap/zero-card games as the repeat backbone: O/U phone receipts, Reality Check dimensions, Text Thread's 22 tones, Scatterblast categories — these don't burn punchline-grade writing. Formalize the AI-draft 

[MAJOR] Spice-5 sexual content served to browser joiners with ZERO age gate is a live policy exposure, and the differentiator-of-record on Play is a Mature 17+ app whose cards target real named people. The Android host app will rate Mature 17+ via IARC (survivable — Picolo/CAH-clone precedent), but the web joiner surface delivers explicit sexual prompts about named real persons with no interstitial, in 20+ US states with 2025-26 age-verification/harmful-to-minors statutes (TX HB 1181 upheld June 2025; app-store accountability laws in TX/UT hit the Android side). Fill-In punchlines are player-written content displayed to others -> Play UGC policy technically requires report/moderation affordances. COPPA itself is low (general-audience, no actual knowledge) but 'name + avatar + persistent crew memory' of an under-13 who scans the QR at a family party is the trap that converts to actual knowledge.
  EVIDENCE: spec_v2.md §1 (joiners: 'No install, no account, name + avatar' — no age mention anywhere in the spec), §3 (spice ceiling to 5), §7 (crew memory persistence). Play policy: sexual themes OK at Mature 17+, 'primarily sexually gratifying' banned; UGC policy requires moderation hooks; harassment policy prohibits content facilitating bullying of real persons — a reviewer seeing spice-5 cards naming real people won't read your consent-architecture spec
  FIX: Cheap and immediate: (1) 18+ attestation interstitial on the web join page (one tap) plus host attests the room is adults at Night creation; tie it to the existing private-consent depth vote — depth 4-5 requires the attestation, which turns your consent mechanic into your compliance mechanic. (2) Host gets a strike/skip on any Fill-In punchline (sa

[MAJOR] Crew memory as spec'd is a server-side dossier of named real people's roast history and (potentially) sexual confessions — GDPR special-category data (Art. 9 'sex life') processed about people with no account, no privacy notice, and no deletion path. 'Roast history' + Confession or Cap outcomes at spice 5, keyed to real first names + avatars, persisted in Durable Object storage across nights: the data subjects never agreed to persistence (a join-tap is not consent to a durable behavioral record), and anyone can enter anyone's name. CCPA blast radius is ~zero (solo pre-revenue dev is far under all thresholds) and EU enforcement against a US solo dev is improbable — but the Play Data Safety form must truthfully declare this collection (misdeclaration = app removal), and one viral 'HELLDECK keeps a server database of your drunk sexual confessions' post kills the trust the whole product runs on.
  EVIDENCE: spec_v2.md §7: 'Crew memory across nights: past superlatives, roast history, win records... Stored in host app + DO room state.' §4 Confession or Cap locks TRUE/FALSE sexual confessions at spice 5. Joiners have 'no account' (§1) — so no DSR mechanism exists even in principle.
  FIX: Decide storage architecture BEFORE build (retrofit is expensive): (1) persistence whitelist — scores, win records, superlative TITLES only; never card text, never confession truth values, never who-voted-whom; (2) crew memory lives on the HOST device (or as a host-keyed encrypted blob), DO state auto-expires after ~30 days idle — server becomes a t
