  EVIDENCE: §3: ceiling = second-lowest at N≥5. The closeted player / grieving player / person with their boss present picks 2; seven feral friends pick 5; the room runs at 5. Their protection budget is 2 Brimstones (§8) against ~27 prompts, several targeted at them; after two burns there is NO valve left, and the corpus's spice-5 targeted cards ('people they've kissed', search-history, hookup counts) are precisely the outing class. The mechanism reads their
  FIX: Split ceilings by target: room ceiling (second-lowest) governs GENERIC cards; any card naming subject X obeys X's own private pick. Nobody ever faces a card about themselves above their own number; the feral majority still gets a feral night about each other. Invisible (server-side targeting filter), implementable, and it dissolves both 'one prude 

[MAJOR] SKIP-'EM at 60% is crueler than the stall: a public supermajority vote to yank a struggling friend off stage is a quantified rejection with a laugh-track, and it doubles as a heckle button against good performers.
  EVIDENCE: §5: '60% tap it during a stalled performance, re-deal with a mocking sting.' The sting mocks the game; the memory is 'everyone pressed the eject button while I was choking.' A stall ends with the timer and sympathy; a pile-on ends with arithmetic (60% of my friends). If the tally is visible mid-performance, the performer watches thumbs hover — a live disapproval meter; if hidden, the re-deal is an ambush. Drunk tables will also mash it against pe
  FIX: SKIP-'EM re-deals the CARD, never the human: same performer, new ammo, sting blames the card ('THAT CARD WAS TRASH — NEW WEAPON'). Add the valve v2 forgot: a performer self-eject ('I REST MY CASE' / bail at half points, which bar6 proposed and v2 dropped) — freezing becomes agency. Only the performer or host mercy-skip can end a performance. Tally 

[FATAL] FACE YOUR ACCUSERS confuses deducible with declared: printing JULES → DEV converts banter-grade inference into an on-record verdict, repeated up to 27 times a night in the group size (trio, couple+1) where votes are relationship statements.
  EVIDENCE: trio3's own data: the open-vote salvage got ONE laugh ('wait, YOU voted me') — novelty that decays while cost compounds. Attribution strips the benign frame exactly as spice ramps (the design's own research: roasting bonds only when 'mutual, clearly playful'): 'Who has the biggest gap between their public persona and who they really are at 3am' [spice 5], attributed, in a trio, is a friend formally filing 'I think you're fake' — and a girlfriend 
  FIX: Cap attributed mode at spice 3 (attribution and depth must never both be maxed). Never print the same voter→victim edge twice a night. Or replace the mode: at N≤4 the group must argue ALOUD to unanimity on a victim — the negotiation is the comedy, there's no false secrecy and no printed record.

[MAJOR] Roast fatigue is structural: ~7 of 9 circles put a real person on trial, the finale mandate is MORE targeting at max spice, and v2 demoted the only pure unifier (Title Fight) to a manual trigger that will never fire.
  EVIDENCE: Deck audit: only Scatterblast and Taboo are truly non-targeting; Poison Pitch/Red Flag/Text Thread target fictional material (semi-outward); everything else exposes a present human. §3 restricts the ×3 finale to Roast Consensus / Fill-In / Over/Under — all person-targeting — so the climax lands on a room already drained by ~30 minutes of threat-monitoring ('am I next?') and reveal monotony (the 5th 'person exposed' beat lands at half strength). t
  FIX: Arc grammar rule: after circle 4, never two subject-targeting circles back-to-back; guarantee one aim-OUTWARD circle per third of the night (Scatterblast, Taboo, Poison Pitch, Red Flag, Title Fight, explicitly classed 'outward'). Put Title Fight back in the shuffler as the second physical spike. A palate cleanser is not wholesome — it's the room un

[MAJOR] The punishment for using consent is the corporate deck: a capped or low-starting night routes to the spice-2-3 corpus that is documented icebreaker filler, so the safety system's actual output is a WHOLESOME night — the identity's one forbidden word.
  EVIDENCE: Card report: spice 2-3 = Starbucks orders, comfort food, Pixar crying, 'times they said sorry', decade-old Reddit debates — 53% of the corpus sits at spice 3. roommates4: 'this is giving corporate icebreaker' said OUT LOUD in the low-spice opening. v2's whole consent apparatus (ceiling, vibe check, fresh-meat variants) shunts rooms toward exactly this content. The corpus conflates two axes: low spice = tame AND impersonal; high spice = wild AND p
  FIX: Decouple the axes in the writing guide: spice caps EXPOSURE (how personal/targeted), never CHAOS (how deranged). Commission a feral-but-impersonal band at spice 1-3 ('Who would bite a raccoon back?' energy) so a consent-capped night is still unhinged. The cap should remove the knife, not the chaos.

[FATAL] Room-scoped spice-5 cards defeat the entire consent apparatus by harming non-consenting third parties: the subject's pre-view protects the subject, but the ANSWER implicates bystanders — partners, exes, coworkers, closeted players — who get no valve and no warning.
  EVIDENCE: Corpus, live today: 'Number of people in this room they've thought about hooking up with' [O/U spice 5], 'Number of people they've kissed' [with exes present], 'What's their body count and are they lying right now' [Hot Seat 5]. A drunk subject who cheerfully answers '8' publishes a desire graph over people who never saw a pre-view; depending on room composition the answer alone outs orientation or fidelity. §8's writing-level rule bans appearanc
  FIX: Writing-level rule extension: above spice 3, no card whose truthful answer names or implicates specific present players — kill or rewrite the 'in this room' desire/history class so the game never asks WHO; extend the protected-traits rule to 'no card whose ANSWER can out orientation, fidelity, or health.' This is a corpus purge plus one sentence in

[MAJOR] There is no mid-round exit: once the burn window has passed, a subject trapped in a BLOCKING input faces a public shame countdown whose only exits are disclose, lie, or stall — the anti-fabrication machinery becomes a pressure-cooker aimed at the exact person the valves were built for.
  EVIDENCE: §5/§3: BLOCKING inputs pause the timer with 'WAITING ON SAM'S RECEIPTS…' + sting; §8 burns exist only at deal time. Chain: 3s pre-view fails (see kill shot 1), card goes live, subject realizes mid-round that the true number is a disclosure ('times they've cried this month' for someone actually depressed; hookup count with a partner beside them). Now the room chants, the countdown shames, auto-advance is forbidden by design ('never fabricates grou
  FIX: Every BLOCKING subject input permanently carries a 'PLEAD THE FIFTH' option: forfeit the round's points, outward-aimed sting ('THE WITNESS TAKES THE FIFTH. COWARDICE NOTED.'), costs no token, always available. One button; keeps ground-truth integrity (no fabrication — the round just voids) while ending the trap.

================================================================================
attack_technical  [HOLDS_WITH_FIXES]
================================================================================

[FATAL] Twelve games get built twice: every game UI must exist in the greenfield web client (joiners) AND in Kotlin/Compose (host is a player with full private-input UIs plus stage faces). Every ★ rule tweak lands in two codebases and drifts.
  EVIDENCE: Spec §1/§2: joiners are browser-only, host is native and also a player, stage renders its own faces. Infra report: 50.8k lines Kotlin across 180 files, ZERO web/JS/KMP code, zero networking — the web side is 100% greenfield. §4 alone contains 7+ per-game tie-break/order rules (Fill-In perform-then-vote ordering, double-roast, hung jury, N=3 variants) that must render identically on both stacks. That is ~1.8x build cost and permanent 2x QA on bar-
  FIX: One game-UI runtime: the web client. Kotlin host app becomes a thin shell (Play Billing, room create, foreground service, accelerometer JS-bridge, stage audio) hosting the SAME web bundle in a WebView — the AirConsole/Jackbox-controller pattern. Keep the joiner bundle <200KB so bar-LTE join stays <2s. Explicitly do NOT reach for Compose Multiplatfo

[FATAL] "Host app authoritative" contradicts the spec's own failure modes: the authority is a phone that backgrounds, and host is a player who app-switches.
  EVIDENCE: Spec §1 (host authoritative, DO persists) vs §10 (host dies → DO restores) vs §3 (host can be the Over/Under subject who app-switches to Settings for receipts). When the host backgrounds their own app — receipt fetch, phone call, notification — Android suspends the room's brain for all 12+ players. On host crash, any inputs received since the last DO mirror are lost, producing lost/double votes on restore. And every joiner resync (§10 visibilityc
  FIX: Make the Durable Object the single authoritative rules engine (TypeScript); the host app is a privileged client with host-only controls (DESCEND, void-round, stage claim). All phones become dumb renderers of DO state. This also collapses the double-build in kill-shot #1: one rules engine, one UI runtime, native shell for platform services.

[MAJOR] Lift-to-sin leaks private ballots and destroys the §8 silent-burn guarantee whenever the host is the target.
  EVIDENCE: The stage lies face-up mid-table. A false trigger (wobbly table producing sustained tilt, a non-host grab, drink slam) flips the HOST'S PRIVATE BALLOT onto the public face for the whole table; the flip-down arc shows the ballot mid-swing before any threshold settles. Missed triggers are ironic: the privacy-natural grip — lift low and flat, shielded — is exactly the motion a tilt threshold cannot see. Worst: §8 subject pre-view (3s early, silent b
  FIX: (a) Detect orientation from the low-passed gravity vector with a baseline calibrated at stage-engage (bar tables aren't level); show private face at >50° sustained 250ms; hide INSTANTLY at first downward motion below ~40° (asymmetric hysteresis). (b) Never render the ballot directly: lift shows an 'EYES OFF' shield, thumb-hold-to-reveal shows the b

[MAJOR] "Reveals render on EVERY phone simultaneously" has no synchronization mechanism anywhere in the spec, and naive WS broadcast on bar LTE skews 100-500ms+.
  EVIDENCE: Spec §2 asserts simultaneity; nothing specifies how. Crowded-venue LTE WS jitter runs p95 200-500ms across mixed carriers/wifi. Phones inches apart flashing >150ms apart read as broken; >400ms murders the 3-2-1 and the Scatterblast boom→'WHO DIED?' attribution (wrong holder blamed systematically, and not as a bit). Per-tick countdown messages drift audibly. Event-based reveals are also unrecoverable: a phone reconnecting 2s late missed the boom e
  FIX: (1) NTP-style clock offset per client over the WS (5 pings, median) refreshed periodically. (2) Server schedules reveals ~1s ahead at server-time T; each client renders at its local mapping of T — achievable skew ~30-80ms, p95 <150ms. (3) Countdowns derive from the synced clock and a single deadline, never per-tick messages. (4) Reveals are idempot

[MAJOR] Blocking input + permanently dead phone = the whole room deadlocks forever; the spec provides no escape hatch.
  EVIDENCE: Spec §3: 'auto-advance never fabricates ground truth' (Over/Under). §5: BLOCKING pauses the server timer. §10: seat held 90s. Walk it: Sam's battery dies inside Settings during the receipt fetch → shame countdown runs → 90s seat lapses → the timer is still paused, ground truth can never arrive, and no rule anywhere advances or voids the round. Same for a confessor truth-lock or role-ack. Twelve people stare at 'WAITING ON SAM'S RECEIPTS…' until t
  FIX: When a seat lapses during a BLOCKING input, unlock a host-only control: 'THE WITNESS FLED — VOID THE ROUND' (mocking sting, no points, arc deals a replacement round) plus optional swap-subject where rules allow. Human escape, never fabricated truth — consistent with the game's voice.

[MAJOR] The iOS resume path desyncs at three specific points the spec doesn't cover: jettisoned-tab identity loss, stale frozen timers firing on thaw, and rendering before resync.
  EVIDENCE: Research report: iOS kills WS ~30-60s after backgrounding; iOS also jettisons the tab under memory pressure (a 'camera roll count' receipt opens Photos — prime jettison trigger). Walk: subject returns → (a) if jettisoned, full page reload; with session identity only in JS memory they land on the join form and must retype code+name while the room sits in a shame countdown; (b) if not jettisoned, the frozen setTimeout/rAF queue thaws and fires PRE-
  FIX: (1) Room code lives in the URL; player token in localStorage; page load silently auto-rejoins with zero typing (spec §10's 'rejoin = same code + same name' must mean automatic, and say so). (2) Epoch counter on every scheduled render; bump epoch on any disconnect/hidden event; discard stale-epoch callbacks on thaw. (3) On visible: freeze into a 'RE

[MAJOR] Free-tier arithmetic: the binding constraint is DURATION, not messages — a hard ceiling of ~43 room-nights per UTC day, and past any cap Cloudflare FAILS operations mid-party rather than billing.
  EVIDENCE: An active room never idles long enough to hibernate (inputs/timers every few seconds), so a 40-min night bills ~2,400s × 0.125GB = 300 GB-s against the 13,000 GB-s/day free duration cap → ~43 nights/day ceiling regardless of message discipline. Requests are the second cliff: uncoalesced fire-spam at 12 players + 20 Imps = 32 devices × 4 taps/s × 45s hold × ~27 reveal moments ≈ 155k incoming msgs/night = ~7.8k billable requests (20:1) against 100k
  FIX: Fine for playtests and soft launch; make the $5/mo Workers Paid flip a LAUNCH PRECONDITION, not a reaction (one $9.99 host unlock covers two months; at paid rates a night costs ~$0.004 of duration). Instrument request/duration counters per room from day one and alert at 60% of daily caps. Coalescing (kill-shot #9) is still mandatory for client sani

[MAJOR] DO hibernation/eviction silently eats the 90s seat-hold and the 10-min host-death restore if they're in-memory timers, and host-death restore loses the round unless full state is persisted per-mutation.
  EVIDENCE: Spec §10 names both timers but the DO can be evicted from memory on hibernation or at Cloudflare's discretion; in-memory setTimeout and un-persisted state vanish. The host-death path triggers this exactly: host phone dies → players pocket phones → all WS idle/drop → DO hibernates within seconds → host relaunches at minute 9 and the DO wakes with only whatever hit SQLite. 'Restore at current circle' is worthless if mid-round votes, Devil's Bargain
  FIX: (1) All deadlines (seat-hold, host-death, phase timers) via the DO Alarms API — persisted, survives eviction (SQLite-backed DOs, which is the free-tier default). (2) Write the full round-state snapshot to SQLite storage on every mutation; in-memory state is pure cache. (3) Heartbeats via setWebSocketAutoResponse ping/pong so keepalives neither wake

[MAJOR] Uncoalesced reaction spam floods the clients worse than the server: 128 msg/s in, ~4,100 msg/s fanned out, animated on mid-tier Androids in battery saver at the night's emotional peak.
  EVIDENCE: Spec §5 makes fire-spam live during the 45s reveal hold and §6 makes it the telemetry channel — so it can't be dropped. 32 devices × 4 taps/s = 128 inbound msg/s; naive per-tap fan-out = 32× that outbound. Outgoing is free in dollars but not in phone CPU: a $150 Android at 15% battery rendering 128 discrete reaction events/s during the reveal will jank and thermal-throttle exactly when the room is watching. It's also the dominant term in the requ
  FIX: Taps animate LOCALLY at zero latency (the tapper needs feedback, not the network). Client coalesces to 1 msg per 500ms carrying tap-count. DO aggregates and broadcasts room heat at ≤4 Hz as count buckets — §6 telemetry survives intact as counts. Per-client accounting cap ~10 taps/s. Reaction cost drops ~40x with zero perceptible loss.

================================================================================
attack_business  [HOLDS_WITH_FIXES]
================================================================================

[FATAL] Android-only host severs the entire growth engine. The viral loop the pricing report prescribes (joiner plays free at someone's party -> 'I want to host this' -> pays $9.99) dies at the platform gate for ~75-80% of prospects: US under-35s are ~78% iPhone (Piper Sandler teens 87%; 18-29 ~75-80%). The hot lead is always an individual, not a group, so 3 of 4 conversion attempts bounce at 'sorry, Android only.' Group-level it's also bad: P(zero Android in a group of 6) = 0.78^6 ≈ 22% under independence, but iMessage homophily clusters friend groups by device — realistically 30-40% of under-30 US groups have zero Android, and 'zero WILLING host' (owns Android AND pays AND surrenders phone to the table as Stage) is plausibly 45-55%. You are asking the group's least-likely ringleader to be your only buyer.
  EVIDENCE: spec_v2.md §1 ('Host: native Android app (paid unlock)') + §9 (Play Billing). report_research_pricing-model.md: 'the only conversion needed is I want to host this myself' — that conversion is impossible for ~4 in 5 of the target demo. report_research_browser-vs-app.md already proves the fix is cheap: the DO relay is mandatory anyway, wake lock works on iOS Safari 16.4+, and Google Play policy is irrelevant to web-sold hosting. Jackbox/Kahoot/Deat
  FIX: Move game-loop authority from the Kotlin app into the Durable Object (host-logic-on-server) and ship a browser host page — any iPhone can then host via web checkout (Stripe ~3% beats Play's 15%). Cost ranking: (1) server-authoritative + web host: ~2-4 wks incremental NOW, arguably negative net cost pre-build (one TypeScript game loop instead of Kot

[MAJOR] The $9.99 trigger is placed at the moment of minimum need. Walk it: 11:40pm, Judgment screen mid-table, share card circulating, Ubers ordered. The only person who CAN buy (the host) is drunk, satiated, and just consumed the free night — willingness-to-pay is at its floor, and the upsell pollutes the emotional peak that IS the viral share artifact. Meanwhile the spec never defines what stops reinstall = infinite free nights.
  EVIDENCE: spec_v2.md §9: 'Purchase moment is AFTER a night ends (on the Judgment screen of a free night).' The pricing report's own Option B logic contradicts this: passes are 'bought at the moment of party... purchase must be <30 seconds' — i.e., buy at blocked intent, not at satisfaction. Heads Up! burstiness data (300k/day baseline vs 5.5M Christmas) means 2-8 weeks elapse before need returns; a Judgment-screen impression is long forgotten.
  FIX: Split the trigger. Judgment screen = seed only: 'Your crew's sins are saved' + share card that carries a 'host your own' link on EVERY joiner's phone (this is where the Jackbox demo loop actually converts — and it requires kill-shot #1's browser hosting to work). Hard paywall = second night's 'Begin the Descent' button, with the lobby already assem

[MAJOR] Solo-authored 1,200-card corpus at Jackbox/CAH writer-room quality is not credible, and a weekly crew exhausts the context-effective pool in ~4-8 nights. Consumption math: a 7-circle Standard night burns ~25-30 content units (Roast=3 prompts x ~2 appearances, O/U=2 subjects, Scatterblast=3 bombs, Confession deals 3 to use 1, plus Brimstone burns and panic fallbacks). The 1,200 corpus partitions hard: 12 per-game pools (~100 each), then by spice tier (a night's ramp touches ~3 of 5 tiers), then by Fresh Meat / IRL-familiarity variants — a given crew's drawable pool per game per context is realistically 20-40 cards. Template shapes ({NAME} 'most likely to...') get recognized before literal repeats appear (CAH decay mechanism). Production side: CAH's ~550 cards took 8 writers years; at a sustained ~10 keepers/day (after Jackbox's 'reject any prompt you can't answer 3 ways fast' cull), 1,200 cards = ~6 months of pure writing on top of building the entire product solo.
  EVIDENCE: spec_v2.md §3 circle recipes + §7 pool partitioning + §7 'Generation-time AI... later content-refresh only.' report_research_design-theory.md: CAH decays via deck memorization; ~95% of party-game design effort is prompts; LLM humor is formulaic (90% of ChatGPT jokes = 25 templates). report_read_rules-interactions.md confirms the 3-tier stack (LLM -> gold -> template) already exists.
  FIX: Cut launch to 8 games with the 4 proven repeaters (Roast, Fill-In, O/U, Scatterblast) 150+ deep instead of 12 x 100 thin. Lean on structurally cheap/zero-card games as the repeat backbone: O/U phone receipts, Reality Check dimensions, Text Thread's 22 tones, Scatterblast categories — these don't burn punchline-grade writing. Formalize the AI-draft 

[MAJOR] Spice-5 sexual content served to browser joiners with ZERO age gate is a live policy exposure, and the differentiator-of-record on Play is a Mature 17+ app whose cards target real named people. The Android host app will rate Mature 17+ via IARC (survivable — Picolo/CAH-clone precedent), but the web joiner surface delivers explicit sexual prompts about named real persons with no interstitial, in 20+ US states with 2025-26 age-verification/harmful-to-minors statutes (TX HB 1181 upheld June 2025; app-store accountability laws in TX/UT hit the Android side). Fill-In punchlines are player-written content displayed to others -> Play UGC policy technically requires report/moderation affordances. COPPA itself is low (general-audience, no actual knowledge) but 'name + avatar + persistent crew memory' of an under-13 who scans the QR at a family party is the trap that converts to actual knowledge.
  EVIDENCE: spec_v2.md §1 (joiners: 'No install, no account, name + avatar' — no age mention anywhere in the spec), §3 (spice ceiling to 5), §7 (crew memory persistence). Play policy: sexual themes OK at Mature 17+, 'primarily sexually gratifying' banned; UGC policy requires moderation hooks; harassment policy prohibits content facilitating bullying of real persons — a reviewer seeing spice-5 cards naming real people won't read your consent-architecture spec
  FIX: Cheap and immediate: (1) 18+ attestation interstitial on the web join page (one tap) plus host attests the room is adults at Night creation; tie it to the existing private-consent depth vote — depth 4-5 requires the attestation, which turns your consent mechanic into your compliance mechanic. (2) Host gets a strike/skip on any Fill-In punchline (sa

[MAJOR] Crew memory as spec'd is a server-side dossier of named real people's roast history and (potentially) sexual confessions — GDPR special-category data (Art. 9 'sex life') processed about people with no account, no privacy notice, and no deletion path. 'Roast history' + Confession or Cap outcomes at spice 5, keyed to real first names + avatars, persisted in Durable Object storage across nights: the data subjects never agreed to persistence (a join-tap is not consent to a durable behavioral record), and anyone can enter anyone's name. CCPA blast radius is ~zero (solo pre-revenue dev is far under all thresholds) and EU enforcement against a US solo dev is improbable — but the Play Data Safety form must truthfully declare this collection (misdeclaration = app removal), and one viral 'HELLDECK keeps a server database of your drunk sexual confessions' post kills the trust the whole product runs on.
  EVIDENCE: spec_v2.md §7: 'Crew memory across nights: past superlatives, roast history, win records... Stored in host app + DO room state.' §4 Confession or Cap locks TRUE/FALSE sexual confessions at spice 5. Joiners have 'no account' (§1) — so no DSR mechanism exists even in principle.
  FIX: Decide storage architecture BEFORE build (retrofit is expensive): (1) persistence whitelist — scores, win records, superlative TITLES only; never card text, never confession truth values, never who-voted-whom; (2) crew memory lives on the HOST device (or as a host-keyed encrypted blob), DO state auto-expires after ~30 days idle — server becomes a t

================================================================================
replay_trio3  [MIXED 6/10] pay=False
================================================================================
NEW FATAL/MAJOR FAILURES:

[FATAL] spec §4 minN gates vs §3 repeat/finale lists (N=3 pool starvation)
  WHAT: The minN=4 stamps on Over/Under, Confession or Cap, Alibi Drop, and Reality Check delete four of the five games that LANDED for this exact group in v1 — including the trio's proven best game (Over/Under receipts) and their finale-grade closer (Reality Check). The legal N=3 pool is 3 games (Roast, Fill-In, Scatterblast) for a 9-circle Full Damnation, forcing Roast ×4 + Scatterblast ×3. §3 simultane
  FIX: Set minN=3 on Over/Under, Confession or Cap, Alibi Drop, Reality Check (and Red Flag/Text Thread — their new tie=SMASH/SURVIVED rules exist precisely for 2-voter juries). Reserve minN=4+ only where role structure truly consumes bodies (Taboo's 3 roles + crowd; Hot Seat's 5). Re-r

[MAJOR] spec §4 game 8 — Poison Pitch metadata self-contradiction
  WHAT: Poison Pitch declares 'minN 4' AND ships an 'N=3 variant' (damage meter + self-prediction) in the same line. As written the arc builder can never deal the variant — it is dead code, and the already-starved N=3 pool loses its only fresh Class B game. Simulated as-if-dealt, the variant itself plays well: non-zero-sum 1-5 ratings kill the v1 'publicly crown one best friend' pathology, and the predict
  FIX: Set minN=3 with an automatic variant swap at N=3 (winner-pick → damage meter). One field change; the variant design is already correct.

[MAJOR] spec §3 Descent arc + finale at N=3
  WHAT: Every mechanic that carries personal disclosure — truth-lock (Confession), self-rating (Reality Check), receipts (Over/Under), planted words (Alibi) — is minN 4+, so the N=3 spice ramp climbs to 5 with only joke-writing and shouting games left to express it. The 36-questions escalation the spec preserves has no mechanical payoff; the finale list (Roast/Fill-In/Over-Under) collapses to a fourth Roa
  FIX: Restore the four games per the FATAL fix, then hard-prefer Reality Check or Over/Under as the N≤4 finale (both all-players-score at N=3 if bettors/debaters score; Reality Check was this group's proven ×3 climax).

[MAJOR] spec §4 game 2 — Fill-In N=3 'author's left neighbor reads'
  WHAT: The reading assignment is publicly deterministic, so authorship is common knowledge the moment each read starts — anonymity is zero by construction, the 'winner + author reveal' beat reveals nothing, and the plausible-deniability license the design theory says powers bold spice-5 writing is gone. At max trust it still functions (attribution comedy), but it silently converts the deck's #1-ranked an
  FIX: Assign reads by random derangement (2 exist at N=3; reader ≠ author guaranteed) so each read answer stays 50/50 ambiguous between the two non-readers, keeping the author reveal a real beat. Same handoff count, anonymity restored.

[MAJOR] spec §3 depth picker vs pool size
  WHAT: The lobby happily sells Full Damnation (9 circles) at N=3 against a 3-4 game pool — the marquee mode is structurally guaranteed to grind repeats at the advertised minimum player count. This group's back half (Roast #2-4, Scatterblast #2-3) decayed from ~2.7 laughs/round to ~1.1, and the $9.99 ask lands immediately after that weakest stretch.
  FIX: Gate depth options by pool arithmetic (circles ≤ unique games + sanctioned repeat slots): at N=3 cap at Standard Descent, or show an honest on-brand warning ('9 circles for 3 sinners means the wheel comes back around — bring more bodies for Full Damnation').

NARRATIVE: Same trio, same quiet apartment: Dev hosts, Maya and Jules join by QR, all three privately pick depth 5, FERAL vibe check, Full Damnation. The first twenty minutes are the best HELLDECK has ever played at N=3 — then the spec's own minN column strangles the night. Circle 1: FACE YOUR ACCUSERS puts Dev's and Jules's votes for Maya on screen with names; Maya's counter-roast is the hardest laugh of the night. The v1 salvage is now the designed game. Circle 2: Fill-In's no-Reader variant delivers a real 3-way vote and aloud reads — but the left-neighbor rule means everyone knows Dev wrote the line

================================================================================
NEW FATAL/MAJOR FAILURES:

[MAJOR] §3 Private consent ('room ceiling = the second-lowest pick')
  WHAT: The second-lowest rule zeroes out exactly the persona it was built for: one shy player among five ghouls (the most common real composition) picks 3, everyone else picks 5, ceiling = 5. Ana's private consent changed nothing. Brimstone can't backstop her either for vote-emergent victims — Roast Consensus victims are chosen BY the vote, so there is no subject pre-view and no burn; whenever the finale
  FIX: Ceiling = lowest pick at N≤6 (a lone troll can't nuke a small table that actually knows each other), second-lowest only at N≥7; or the minimum caps the finale by one rung. Rename the private pick 'heat ceiling' to kill the depth/length collision.

A missed window ships the card to the room: the v1 public-flinch scenario returns as the DEFAULT outcome for any inattentive subject, and the player who most ne
  FIX: Pre-view window ≥10s, uniform on ALL named-card deals so delay length leaks nothing (costs ~2-3 min/night, worth it); spotlight burn window 10s with the room shown a generic 'DEALING…' face. Never make a safety valve faster than a sip of beer.

  FIX: Performer gets an 'I REST MY CASE' button that ends their own timer (bold, on-brand); SKIP-'EM is disabled once the performer rests or in the final 10s. Also generalize Fill-In's ballot-lock to all Class B: vote UI stays locked (timer-only) until the performance ends.

  FIX: In finale Fill-In, the Bargain-holder/last-place player is never dealt Reader; give the finale Reader a performance score (audience 🔥 during the read converts to points) so the classification becomes true; or drop Fill-In from the N≤6 finale pool.

That one change roughly doubled the night's laugh count by itself. input.content:
# HELLDECK 2.0 — Gameplay Model Spec v3 (FINAL) — "The Descent"

_v3 = v2 + fixes from round-2 adversarial attacks (pacing/social/technical/business, all HOLDS_WITH_FIXES) and edge re-validations (bar6 6.5→8.0 FUN, birthday13 6.5→7.5 FUN, trio3 fixes folded). Changes from v2 marked ◆._

## 0. Identity constraints (non-negotiable)
- UNHINGED, never WHOLESOME. Dive bar with great lighting; group chat that went too far at 2am; a roast where everyone's in on it.
- The game makes FRIENDS funny about EACH OTHER. Screens carry secrets and votes; mouths carry the comedy. The phone is never the performer.
- Cards use REAL player names.
- ◆ **Spice caps EXPOSURE, never CHAOS.** Low-spice content must still be feral ("Who would bite a raccoon back?" energy). The consent system removes the knife, never the chaos. (Corpus rule, enforced in the content bible.)

## 1. Platform model — ◆ ARCHITECTURE REWORKED
- ◆ **The game brain is a Cloudflare Durable Object (TypeScript) — one per room, server-authoritative.** Every phone (host included) is a dumb renderer of DO state over WebSocket. No phone is ever the authority (phones background, die, and leave for the kitchen).
- ◆ **One game-UI runtime: the web client.** Joiners: any phone browser via QR/room code, no install, no account. Host: same web bundle — either in the browser (any platform, incl. iPhone) or inside the **Android shell app** (a thin WebView shell adding Play Billing, reliable stage audio via foreground service, accelerometer bridge for lift-to-sin, wake-lock). Never build a game screen twice.
- ◆ **Anyone can host from any phone.** Web hosts pay via web checkout (Stripe ~3%); Android shell hosts may pay via Play Billing. iOS shell = later, only if data demands it.
- Sync: NTP-style clock offset per client (median of 5 pings, refreshed); server schedules reveals ~1s ahead at server-time T; countdowns derive from one deadline, never per-tick messages; reveals are idempotent state (late/rejoining phones render the current truth). Target skew ≤150ms p95.
- Reaction taps animate locally, coalesce client-side (1 msg/500ms with count), DO broadcasts heat buckets ≤4 Hz.
- Rejoin is AUTOMATIC: room code in URL, player token in localStorage; page reload lands back in the seat with zero typing. Epoch counter discards stale timer callbacks on tab-thaw; on visible → "RECONNECTING" freeze → render DO truth.
- ◆ Launch precondition: Workers Paid ($5/mo) — free-tier duration math caps at ~43 room-nights/day and FAILS mid-party past caps. (Playtests run free.)
- Every sync moment is VISUAL-FIRST on all phones (full-screen flash/pulse); host-phone audio is garnish, never load-bearing.

## 2. The Stage (host phone mid-table) — optional, adaptive
- Default ON at N≥5, OFF below. Stage face renders PUBLIC-ONLY content: timers, tally counts, reveal spectacle, transitions, audio.
- **Lift-to-sin** (short ballots ONLY, ≤10s inputs): stage shows "NICK — PICK UP TO SIN"; lifting flips to the host's private ballot; laying flat restores the public face. ◆ Host's window runs CONCURRENT with everyone's input window (first 6s), never before it. ◆ Orientation from low-passed gravity vector, baseline calibrated at stage-engage (bar tables aren't level); private face at >50° sustained 250ms; hide INSTANTLY below ~40° (asymmetric hysteresis). ◆ Lift shows an "EYES OFF" shield; thumb-hold-to-reveal shows the ballot. Fallback: manual tap toggle.
- ◆ Long-form host inputs (writing, receipt fetch, clue-giving): stage auto-suspends to a public "THE HOST IS SINNING" banner; restores on re-lay-flat + confirm. Host private long inputs NEVER render on the stage face.
- Consensus/scribe entries always go to a named NON-SUBJECT player's phone, never "the stage."

## 3. Session structure — "The Descent"
- Lobby: group picks depth — **Quick Dip (5 circles ≈ 17-22 min), Standard Descent (7 ≈ 24-30 min), Full Damnation (9 ≈ 28-36 min)** (◆ honest arithmetic, gameplay only, excludes ~3 min lobby). ◆ Single-performer circles run 2 loops with 2 DIFFERENT performers (2 confessors, 2 texts, 2 subjects…) — keeps every circle 3-4 min AND doubles spotlight spread.
- ◆ **Depth gating by pool arithmetic**: circles ≤ unique legal games + sanctioned repeat slots at this N. At N=3 the lobby caps at Standard with an honest on-brand warning ("9 circles for 3 sinners means the wheel comes back around — bring more bodies for Full Damnation").
- **Private consent — ◆ reworked (per-target ceilings, nothing rendered):**
  - During join, every player privately picks their **heat ceiling** (1-5) on their own phone.
  - ◆ Cards NAMING subject X never exceed X's own ceiling. (The lone shy player is fully protected; the feral majority still gets a feral night about each other.)
  - ◆ Generic (no-subject) cards obey the room generic ceiling: second-lowest pick (lowest at N≤4).
  - ◆ Vote-emergent-victim games (Roast Consensus — anyone can become the subject) obey the LOWEST pick above spice 3.
  - ◆ **No ceiling number is EVER rendered anywhere** — the ceiling is a silent server-side pool filter. Nobody can tell "capped at 3" from "the deck dealt that way." No witch hunts.
- **Vibe check** sets the STARTING rung (SOBER/WARMED UP/FERAL). ◆ Precedence: ceiling always wins; starting rung = min(vibe rung, generic ceiling − 2), floor 1 — at least two escalation steps every night. Escalation = the 36-questions ramp.
- ◆ **Arc grammar** (anti roast-fatigue): after circle 4, never two subject-targeting circles back-to-back; at least one aim-OUTWARD circle (Scatterblast, Taboo, Poison Pitch, Red Flag, Title Fight) per third of the night. ◆ Title Fight returns to the shuffler as the second physical spike.
- **Finale (×3)**: all-players-score games at the night's max legal spice. ◆ At N≤4 prefer Reality Check or Over/Under (proven small-N climaxes). ◆ In finale Fill-In, last place/Bargain-holder is never dealt Reader, and the Reader earns performance points (🔥 during the read converts) — nobody is excluded from the ×3 climax.
- THE JUDGMENT: winner + telemetry superlatives + screenshot-shaped share card. ◆ The share card lands on EVERY joiner's phone carrying a "host your own descent" link (the conversion seed — see §9).
- Devil's Bargain (last place, hidden ×2) attaches only to all-players-score circles; revealed with drama at Judgment.
- No elimination. Repeats at small N allowed for proven repeaters (Roast, Scatterblast, Over/Under) with fresh content; never repeat single-performer games.

## 4. Games — ◆ LAUNCH ROSTER = 8 deep, not 12 thin
_(Business finding: solo corpus of 12×100 writer-room-grade cards isn't credible; 8 games with the 4 repeaters 150+ deep is. The other 4 ship as the first free content drop when the corpus supports them.)_

**LAUNCH 8:**
1. **Roast Consensus** (minN 3) — 3 prompts/circle; secret victim vote; synced reveal, name in lights; reveal HOLDS for the table roast. Room Heat: unanimity-minus-victim at N≤5, 60% at N≥8. Plurality tie = "THE ROOM IS TORN — DOUBLE ROAST." ◆ N=3-4 attributed mode ("FACE YOUR ACCUSERS") capped at spice ≤3, same voter→victim edge never printed twice a night.
2. **Fill-In Finisher** (minN 3) — perform-then-vote. Writers write (panic button = 2 curated fallbacks, half points); answers go ONLY to the Reader's teleprompter; ◆ during the write the Reader picks the read-tone ("read these as: a eulogy / a 911 call / a hostage tape") — a job + a comedy layer; Reader performs each aloud (phones show "LISTEN UP"); ◆ vote grid then shows answer TEXTS as memory aids, randomized order per phone (+15s budgeted); secret vote, no self-votes; winner + author reveal. ◆ N≥7: TWO Readers, one per setup, alternating; per setup Reader performs ≤4 answers in one run → one vote picks the top answer → single cross-setup grand face-off (caps reads at ~8, votes at 3). Imp bracket contributes 1 curated answer shown as an on-screen cameo, never performed. ◆ N=3: no Reader; reads assigned by random derangement (reader ≠ author, authorship stays 50/50 ambiguous); all 3 vote among the other two.
3. **Over/Under** (◆ minN 3) — 2 subjects/circle; line debated ALOUD; secret bets; subject enters truth (receipt fetch = blocking input with pause + shame countdown). Phone-receipt stats preferred (screen time, camera roll, unread emails) — verifiable, works for strangers and online-first crews.
4. **Confession or Cap** (◆ minN 3) — confessor dealt 3, picks 1 (consent + strategy); locks TRUE/FALSE; tells it aloud; jury votes; tie = confessor wins ("HUNG JURY — THE LIAR WALKS").
5. **Scatterblast** (minN 3) — stage/volunteer phone is the bomb; category+letter on all phones; boom → "WHO DIED?" majority tap (5s).
6. **Poison Pitch** (◆ minN 3) — private assignments; pitches aloud; secret vote; tie = 10s sudden-death rebuttal + revote, second tie = split pot. ◆ N=3 auto-variant: judge rates each pitch 1-5 damage meter (non-zero-sum) + pitchers secretly predict their own score for bonus. Down-weighted at N=4 (2 voters).
7. **Red Flag Rally** (◆ minN 3) — defense aloud; secret SMASH/PASS; tie = SMASH (performer wins ties).
8. **Alibi Drop** (◆ minN 3) — words only to the accused; alibi aloud; jury picks 3 suspected plants from an 8-word lineup; stage-sequenced word-by-word reveal (sting per word).

**CONTENT DROP 1 (post-launch, free):** Text Thread Trap (minN 3, tie = SURVIVED), Reality Check (◆ minN 3; scribe = non-subject; N≤4-finale-preferred), Taboo Timer (minN 4 — 3 roles), Hot Seat Imposter (minN 5; gated to IRL-familiar crews).
**Manual:** Title Fight = shuffler-scheduled physical spike + host-triggered Fight Break. **Cut:** Unifying Theory.

## 5. Pacing (Jack Principles, DO-enforced)
- One task per phone per moment; ALL input simultaneous; no sequential turns.
- **SKIPPABLE inputs** (votes, punchlines): auto-abstain/auto-panic at timer; the game never waits.
- **BLOCKING inputs** (truth-locks, subject numbers, role acks): timer pauses, public "WAITING ON SAM…" shame countdown. ◆ **Terminal states, always**: at 30-45s the room gets "DRAG THEM BACK or FEED THEM TO THE PIT" (60% PIT = round voided, mocking sting, circle re-deals a no-subject game) AND the host gets a VOID ROUND button; a seat-expiry (90s) auto-voids any blocking input it owns; ◆ scribe-downgrade option (subject's verbal claim entered, flagged "UNVERIFIED"). Never fabricate ground truth; never wait forever.
- ◆ **PLEAD THE FIFTH**: every blocking subject input permanently carries it — forfeit round points, outward sting ("THE WITNESS TAKES THE FIFTH. COWARDICE NOTED."), costs no token, always available. The trap (disclose/lie/stall) never closes on anyone.
- **Reveal-holds**: reveals HOLD for the table roast; ◆ at 45s soft-cap DESCEND propagates to ALL phones (anyone advances), plus reaction-decay auto-advance (no 🔥 for 8s past the 20s mark). Server owns inputs; humans own aftermath; the bathroom costs ≤53s.
- ◆ **I REST MY CASE**: performers can end their own timer (kills dead air, rewards boldness, unlocks the ballot). SKIP-'EM disabled once rested and in the final 10s.
- ◆ **SKIP-'EM re-deals the CARD, never the human**: same performer, new ammo, sting blames the card ("THAT CARD WAS TRASH — NEW WEAPON"). One re-deal per circle. Tally hidden until it fires.
- ◆ **Deal ceremony**: every deal runs a fixed-length room-side ritual (5-6s "THE DECK IS CHOOSING ITS VICTIM…" roulette on all phones) that absorbs exactly one burn cycle with the backup card pre-fetched — zero timing delta between burned and clean deals.
- Explainers: 15s first time per GROUP, 5s title card after. Reveal grammar: payloads glanceable in <2s or stage-sequenced into beats.

## 6. Scoring
- Points concentrate on the bold (winning punchlines, survived pitches, believed lies, planted words); voters get flat +1 participation, nothing for alignment. Exception: Roast Consensus keeps majority-alignment + Room Heat (consensus IS that game).
- Finale ×3; Devil's Bargain per §3; Descent Rank ladder 5s between circles; 🔥 spam = invisible telemetry; no feedback phase.

## 7. Personalization & memory
- {NAME} slots use real lobby names; spotlight-fairness counter spreads targeting.
- Fresh Meat protocol: first-night names excluded from knowledge-dependent subject roles; first-impression card variants; full players in anonymous-input games from circle 1.
- IRL-familiarity gate (once per crew): online-first crews get persona/phone-legible pools; Hot Seat/Reality Check gated out.
- ◆ **Crew memory = privacy whitelist ONLY**: scores, win records, superlative TITLES, night count. **NEVER stored: card text, confession truth values, who-voted-whom, answers.** Crew memory lives host-side (host-keyed blob); DO room state auto-expires ~30 days idle. Play Data Safety form declares truthfully.
- Generation-time AI (cloud, pennies/session) = content-refresh pipeline only (draft → human curation); never runtime, never the joke author, never a judge.

## 8. Safety valves (consent that stays unhinged)
- **Brimstone tokens, 2/player/night, use-or-lose** (no hoarding reward): veto a card or dodge a spotlight.
- ◆ **Subject pre-view ≥10s** inside the uniform deal ceremony ("never make a safety valve faster than a sip of beer"); silent BURN → backup card ships with zero timing delta; no animation, no trace, no counter for subject-burns. Pre-views delivered during heads-down phases only.
- ◆ Optional upgrade (test in beta): subject sees 2 candidate cards, picks which the room gets (auto-pick at timeout) — consent as strategy, invisible as a valve.
- **Spotlight burn window 10s**, room sees generic "DEALING…" face; burning re-deals silently.
- Per-target heat ceilings (§3) + 18+ attestation: web join page carries a one-tap 18+ attestation; host attests the room at Night creation; **depth 4-5 requires it** (the consent mechanic doubles as the compliance mechanic).
- ◆ UGC hook: host can strike any Fill-In punchline at the teleprompter ("BURNED — NEW LINE") — satisfies Play UGC moderation affordances and kills slur-bombs before the room hears them.
- Writing-level rules (content bible): no card targets appearance, family death, or protected traits; ◆ above spice 3, no card whose TRUTHFUL ANSWER names or implicates specific present third parties (kills the "in this room" desire/history class); no card whose answer can out orientation, fidelity, or health.

## 9. Monetization
- Free for every joiner, always. **A new host's first full Night is free.**
- **$9.99 one-time "Host the Descent" unlock.** ◆ Purchase trigger moved to the moment of BLOCKED INTENT: the second Night's "BEGIN THE DESCENT" button, with the lobby already assembled behind it (buy in <30s or the party stalls — maximum willingness-to-pay). The Judgment screen only SEEDS: share card on every joiner's phone with "host your own descent" link. Never an upsell mid-party, never ads, no subscription.
- ◆ Web checkout (Stripe) for browser hosts; Play Billing in the Android shell. Content packs ($2.99-4.99) later.
- Play rating: Mature 17+ (IARC). ◆ Device/localStorage nights are attributable enough for the free-night gate v1; account only at purchase.

## 10. Failure handling & scale
- Players 3-12 active + Imps (latecomers/overflow): Imp names enter the {NAME} pool (roastable = included), 0.5-weight votes, Room Heat fuel, 1 curated cameo answer in N≥7 Fill-In. ◆ Conversion in JOIN ORDER up to the cap at each circle boundary, announced as a role ("ZOE IS TONIGHT'S IMP — feed her souls"); residual Imps get a guaranteed Judgment superlative + first-in-line next conversion.
- Disconnects: seat held 90s (skippables auto-play; blocking inputs auto-void at expiry), automatic rejoin (URL+token). Host phone dies: DO is the authority, nothing is lost; host controls (DESCEND, VOID) fail over to the next player in join order until the host returns.
- ◆ All deadlines (seat-hold, phase timers, restore windows) live in DO Alarms (persisted, eviction-proof); full round-state snapshot written to DO SQLite on every mutation; heartbeats via setWebSocketAutoResponse.

## 11. Build order (v1 scope)
1. DO rules engine (TypeScript) + web client (join, lobby, consent, 8 games, Judgment) — the whole game, playable by anyone including iPhone hosts.
2. Android shell (WebView + Play Billing + stage audio foreground service + lift-to-sin bridge + wake-lock).
3. Content: rebuilt corpus for the LAUNCH 8 (see content bible / card rebuild phase).
4. Beta: free unlimited hosting, instrument everything (room counts, night completion rate, share-card taps, per-card 🔥 telemetry).
5. Flip Workers Paid + pricing on at launch.
_Killed by this architecture: on-device llama.cpp + 1.7GB GGUFs (Play-blocker), kiosk/device-admin machinery, per-device premium unlock, Heimdall submodule, LOL/MEH/TRASH modal, Thompson sampling on templates._

toolUseResult.content:
# HELLDECK 2.0 — Gameplay Model Spec v3 (FINAL) — "The Descent"

_v3 = v2 + fixes from round-2 adversarial attacks (pacing/social/technical/business, all HOLDS_WITH_FIXES) and edge re-validations (bar6 6.5→8.0 FUN, birthday13 6.5→7.5 FUN, trio3 fixes folded). Changes from v2 marked ◆._

## 0. Identity constraints (non-negotiable)
- UNHINGED, never WHOLESOME. Dive bar with great lighting; group chat that went too far at 2am; a roast where everyone's in on it.
- The game makes FRIENDS funny about EACH OTHER. Screens carry secrets and votes; mouths carry the comedy. The phone is never the performer.
- Cards use REAL player names.
- ◆ **Spice caps EXPOSURE, never CHAOS.** Low-spice content must still be feral ("Who would bite a raccoon back?" energy). The consent system removes the knife, never the chaos. (Corpus rule, enforced in the content bible.)

## 1. Platform model — ◆ ARCHITECTURE REWORKED
- ◆ **The game brain is a Cloudflare Durable Object (TypeScript) — one per room, server-authoritative.** Every phone (host included) is a dumb renderer of DO state over WebSocket. No phone is ever the authority (phones background, die, and leave for the kitchen).
- ◆ **One game-UI runtime: the web client.** Joiners: any phone browser via QR/room code, no install, no account. Host: same web bundle — either in the browser (any platform, incl. iPhone) or inside the **Android shell app** (a thin WebView shell adding Play Billing, reliable stage audio via foreground service, accelerometer bridge for lift-to-sin, wake-lock). Never build a game screen twice.
- ◆ **Anyone can host from any phone.** Web hosts pay via web checkout (Stripe ~3%); Android shell hosts may pay via Play Billing. iOS shell = later, only if data demands it.
- Sync: NTP-style clock offset per client (median of 5 pings, refreshed); server schedules reveals ~1s ahead at server-time T; countdowns derive from one deadline, never per-tick messages; reveals are idempotent state (late/rejoining phones render the current truth). Target skew ≤150ms p95.
- Reaction taps animate locally, coalesce client-side (1 msg/500ms with count), DO broadcasts heat buckets ≤4 Hz.
- Rejoin is AUTOMATIC: room code in URL, player token in localStorage; page reload lands back in the seat with zero typing. Epoch counter discards stale timer callbacks on tab-thaw; on visible → "RECONNECTING" freeze → render DO truth.
- ◆ Launch precondition: Workers Paid ($5/mo) — free-tier duration math caps at ~43 room-nights/day and FAILS mid-party past caps. (Playtests run free.)
- Every sync moment is VISUAL-FIRST on all phones (full-screen flash/pulse); host-phone audio is garnish, never load-bearing.

## 2. The Stage (host phone mid-table) — optional, adaptive
- Default ON at N≥5, OFF below. Stage face renders PUBLIC-ONLY content: timers, tally counts, reveal spectacle, transitions, audio.
- **Lift-to-sin** (short ballots ONLY, ≤10s inputs): stage shows "NICK — PICK UP TO SIN"; lifting flips to the host's private ballot; laying flat restores the public face. ◆ Host's window runs CONCURRENT with everyone's input window (first 6s), never before it. ◆ Orientation from low-passed gravity vector, baseline calibrated at stage-engage (bar tables aren't level); private face at >50° sustained 250ms; hide INSTANTLY below ~40° (asymmetric hysteresis). ◆ Lift shows an "EYES OFF" shield; thumb-hold-to-reveal shows the ballot. Fallback: manual tap toggle.
- ◆ Long-form host inputs (writing, receipt fetch, clue-giving): stage auto-suspends to a public "THE HOST IS SINNING" banner; restores on re-lay-flat + confirm. Host private long inputs NEVER render on the stage face.
- Consensus/scribe entries always go to a named NON-SUBJECT player's phone, never "the stage."

## 3. Session structure — "The Descent"
- Lobby: group picks depth — **Quick Dip (5 circles ≈ 17-22 min), Standard Descent (7 ≈ 24-30 min), Full Damnation (9 ≈ 28-36 min)** (◆ honest arithmetic, gameplay only, excludes ~3 min lobby). ◆ Single-performer circles run 2 loops with 2 DIFFERENT performers (2 confessors, 2 texts, 2 subjects…) — keeps every circle 3-4 min AND doubles spotlight spread.
- ◆ **Depth gating by pool arithmetic**: circles ≤ unique legal games + sanctioned repeat slots at this N. At N=3 the lobby caps at Standard with an honest on-brand warning ("9 circles for 3 sinners means the wheel comes back around — bring more bodies for Full Damnation").
- **Private consent — ◆ reworked (per-target ceilings, nothing rendered):**
  - During join, every player privately picks their **heat ceiling** (1-5) on their own phone.
  - ◆ Cards NAMING subject X never exceed X's own ceiling. (The lone shy player is fully protected; the feral majority still gets a feral night about each other.)
  - ◆ Generic (no-subject) cards obey the room generic ceiling: second-lowest pick (lowest at N≤4).
  - ◆ Vote-emergent-victim games (Roast Consensus — anyone can become the subject) obey the LOWEST pick above spice 3.
  - ◆ **No ceiling number is EVER rendered anywhere** — the ceiling is a silent server-side pool filter. Nobody can tell "capped at 3" from "the deck dealt that way." No witch hunts.
- **Vibe check** sets the STARTING rung (SOBER/WARMED UP/FERAL). ◆ Precedence: ceiling always wins; starting rung = min(vibe rung, generic ceiling − 2), floor 1 — at least two escalation steps every night. Escalation = the 36-questions ramp.
- ◆ **Arc grammar** (anti roast-fatigue): after circle 4, never two subject-targeting circles back-to-back; at least one aim-OUTWARD circle (Scatterblast, Taboo, Poison Pitch, Red Flag, Title Fight) per third of the night. ◆ Title Fight returns to the shuffler as the second physical spike.
- **Finale (×3)**: all-players-score games at the night's max legal spice. ◆ At N≤4 prefer Reality Check or Over/Under (proven small-N climaxes). ◆ In finale Fill-In, last place/Bargain-holder is never dealt Reader, and the Reader earns performance points (🔥 during the read converts) — nobody is excluded from the ×3 climax.
- THE JUDGMENT: winner + telemetry superlatives + screenshot-shaped share card. ◆ The share card lands on EVERY joiner's phone carrying a "host your own descent" link (the conversion seed — see §9).
- Devil's Bargain (last place, hidden ×2) attaches only to all-players-score circles; revealed with drama at Judgment.
- No elimination. Repeats at small N allowed for proven repeaters (Roast, Scatterblast, Over/Under) with fresh content; never repeat single-performer games.

## 4. Games — ◆ LAUNCH ROSTER = 8 deep, not 12 thin
_(Business finding: solo corpus of 12×100 writer-room-grade cards isn't credible; 8 games with the 4 repeaters 150+ deep is. The other 4 ship as the first free content drop when the corpus supports them.)_

**LAUNCH 8:**
1. **Roast Consensus** (minN 3) — 3 prompts/circle; secret victim vote; synced reveal, name in lights; reveal HOLDS for the table roast. Room Heat: unanimity-minus-victim at N≤5, 60% at N≥8. Plurality tie = "THE ROOM IS TORN — DOUBLE ROAST." ◆ N=3-4 attributed mode ("FACE YOUR ACCUSERS") capped at spice ≤3, same voter→victim edge never printed twice a night.
2. **Fill-In Finisher** (minN 3) — perform-then-vote. Writers write (panic button = 2 curated fallbacks, half points); answers go ONLY to the Reader's teleprompter; ◆ during the write the Reader picks the read-tone ("read these as: a eulogy / a 911 call / a hostage tape") — a job + a comedy layer; Reader performs each aloud (phones show "LISTEN UP"); ◆ vote grid then shows answer TEXTS as memory aids, randomized order per phone (+15s budgeted); secret vote, no self-votes; winner + author reveal. ◆ N≥7: TWO Readers, one per setup, alternating; per setup Reader performs ≤4 answers in one run → one vote picks the top answer → single cross-setup grand face-off (caps reads at ~8, votes at 3). Imp bracket contributes 1 curated answer shown as an on-screen cameo, never performed. ◆ N=3: no Reader; reads assigned by random derangement (reader ≠ author, authorship stays 50/50 ambiguous); all 3 vote among the other two.
3. **Over/Under** (◆ minN 3) — 2 subjects/circle; line debated ALOUD; secret bets; subject enters truth (receipt fetch = blocking input with pause + shame countdown). Phone-receipt stats preferred (screen time, camera roll, unread emails) — verifiable, works for strangers and online-first crews.
4. **Confession or Cap** (◆ minN 3) — confessor dealt 3, picks 1 (consent + strategy); locks TRUE/FALSE; tells it aloud; jury votes; tie = confessor wins ("HUNG JURY — THE LIAR WALKS").
5. **Scatterblast** (minN 3) — stage/volunteer phone is the bomb; category+letter on all phones; boom → "WHO DIED?" majority tap (5s).
6. **Poison Pitch** (◆ minN 3) — private assignments; pitches aloud; secret vote; tie = 10s sudden-death rebuttal + revote, second tie = split pot. ◆ N=3 auto-variant: judge rates each pitch 1-5 damage meter (non-zero-sum) + pitchers secretly predict their own score for bonus. Down-weighted at N=4 (2 voters).
7. **Red Flag Rally** (◆ minN 3) — defense aloud; secret SMASH/PASS; tie = SMASH (performer wins ties).
8. **Alibi Drop** (◆ minN 3) — words only to the accused; alibi aloud; jury picks 3 suspected plants from an 8-word lineup; stage-sequenced word-by-word reveal (sting per word).

**CONTENT DROP 1 (post-launch, free):** Text Thread Trap (minN 3, tie = SURVIVED), Reality Check (◆ minN 3; scribe = non-subject; N≤4-finale-preferred), Taboo Timer (minN 4 — 3 roles), Hot Seat Imposter (minN 5; gated to IRL-familiar crews).
**Manual:** Title Fight = shuffler-scheduled physical spike + host-triggered Fight Break. **Cut:** Unifying Theory.

aiTitle: Redesign card game for fun multiplayer gameplay

input.script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'helldeck-card-autopsy',
  description: 'Deep autopsy of all 917 gold cards + content system, plus trio3 re-validation of spec v3',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Deck autopsies', detail: 'one pathologist per game deck, per-card verdicts' },
    { title: 'Cross-analysis', detail: 'voice homogeneity, structure/generativity, spice axes' },
    { title: 'Validate', detail: 'trio3 replays spec v3' },
    { title: 'Synthesize', detail: 'unified failure taxonomy + rebuild commandments' },
  ],
}

const S = '/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-nicholastoledo-Development-active-HELLDECK/4eb51e65-3e38-49e9-b745-6d128c919d2d/scratchpad'
const CORPUS = `${S}/corpus`
const ASSETS = '/Users/nicholastoledo/Development/active/HELLDECK/app/src/main/assets'

const DECK_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    deck_diagnosis: { type: 'string', description: 'max 250 words: why this deck fails/succeeds as a whole' },
    stats: { type: 'object', properties: { kill: { type: 'number' }, rewrite: { type: 'number' }, keep: { type: 'number' } }, required: ['kill', 'rewrite', 'keep'] },
    failure_mode_counts: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'object', properties: { mode: { type: 'string' }, count: { type: 'number' } }, required: ['mode', 'count'] } },
    keep_ids: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    examples: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'object', properties: {
      id: { type: 'string' }, text: { type: 'string' }, verdict: { type: 'string', enum: ['KILL', 'REWRITE', 'KEEP'] },
      mode: { type: 'string' }, why: { type: 'string' } }, required: ['id', 'verdict', 'mode', 'why'] }, description: '8-12 most instructive cards' },
    rebuild_requirements: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: '4-8 concrete writing requirements for this deck rebuild' },
  },
  required: ['deck_diagnosis', 'stats', 'failure_mode_counts', 'keep_ids', 'examples', 'rebuild_requirements'],
}

const ANALYST_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    findings: { type: 'string', description: 'max 700 words, dense, with counted evidence' },
    key_numbers: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    rebuild_rules: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
  },
  required: ['findings', 'key_numbers', 'rebuild_rules'],
}

const PLAYTEST_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    group_verdict: { type: 'string', enum: ['FUN', 'MIXED', 'NOT_FUN'] },
    fun_score_1_10: { type: 'number' },
    fixed_from_v2: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    failures: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'object', properties: {
      where: { type: 'string' }, what: { type: 'string' },
      severity: { type: 'string', enum: ['FATAL', 'MAJOR', 'MINOR'] },
      fix: { type: 'string' } }, required: ['where', 'what', 'severity', 'fix'] } },
    would_someone_pay_to_host: { type: 'boolean' },
    narrative_highlights: { type: 'string', description: 'max 300 words' },
  },
  required: ['group_verdict', 'fun_score_1_10', 'failures', 'would_someone_pay_to_host', 'narrative_highlights'],
}

const TAXONOMY = `FAILURE TAXONOMY (extend if you find new modes, but use these names where they fit):
- GENERIC_INTERNET: could sit in any 2019-2024 Twitter-humor party deck unchanged (feet pics, mugshots, OnlyFans, Spotify Wrapped, therapist, LinkedIn). The Specificity Test: swap this card into a generic deck — does anything break? No = GENERIC_INTERNET.
- PREWRITTEN_JOKE: the card IS the punchline; players are a laugh track. (Jackbox law: prompts set PLAYERS up to be funny. A great card is a setup that needs the room to complete it.)
- NO_3_ANSWERS: for prompts demanding player answers — if you can't think of 3 different funny responses in 10 seconds, it fails (Jackbox's own writing test).
- WRONG_TARGET: about a hypothetical internet person, not exploitable against the actual people in the room (no {NAME} leverage, no vote tension).
- EXPOSURE_CHAOS_CONFLATION: low spice = boring/wholesome instead of feral-but-impersonal; or high spice = merely explicit instead of higher-stakes-personal. Spice must cap EXPOSURE (how personal), never CHAOS (how deranged).
- THIRD_PARTY_HARM: truthful answer names/implicates specific present people (the 'in this room' desire/history class) or can out orientation/fidelity/health. Above spice 3 this is a hard ban.
- WHOLESOME_CORPORATE: icebreaker tier (Starbucks orders, comfort food, Pixar crying). Identity violation — the one forbidden word is WHOLESOME.
- DATED_MEME: decade-old Reddit debates (horse-sized duck, hot dog sandwich) or aging references that stamp an expiry date on the deck.
- MECHANIC_MISMATCH: doesn't set up its game's core verb (a Roast prompt nobody would vote on; a Confession that's not lie-worthy; a Poison Pitch with one obviously-better option; an Over/Under stat with no debatable line).
- TOO_LONG: not glanceable/speakable in one breath; reveals must land in <2s.
- DUPLICATE_SHAPE: same skeleton as N other cards in the deck (deck decays fast when players recognize the template).
- DARK_NOT_FUNNY: violation without the benign frame (literal abuse description played straight).`

const CRAFT = `CRAFT STANDARDS (from research — apply ruthlessly):
- Benign Violation Theory: funny = norm violation + safety, simultaneously. Distance/fictional framing makes darkness playable; targeting people in the room raises stakes and NEEDS the strongest frame.
- Jackbox prompt rules: seed inherently funny words; mine real embarrassing memories; avoid over-specificity that locks out answers; vary structure across the deck.
- CAH decay lesson: finite prewritten jokes get memorized; player-generative structures don't decay.
- Taste hierarchy: GENERIC (any deck) < SAFE-GOOD (competent, predictable) < CONTEXTUAL (this game only) < DISTINCTIVE (could ONLY be HELLDECK). Minimum bar for KEEP = CONTEXTUAL.
- HELLDECK voice: a dive bar at 2am, affectionate violence, group-chat-that-went-too-far. NEVER corporate, never a listicle, never 'quirky'.`

const GAMES = [
  { key: 'roast_consensus', name: 'ROAST CONSENSUS', mech: "Everyone secretly votes which PRESENT player best fits the prompt; synced reveal puts the victim's name in lights; the table then roasts. Cards must make the VOTE deliciously contested (2-3 plausible victims in any group) and give the roast ammunition. Cards use {NAME}-free 'Who would...' form. Launch game, needs 150+ cards." },
  { key: 'fill_in_finisher', name: 'FILL-IN FINISHER', mech: "Setup with a blank; everyone writes a punchline; a Reader performs all answers ALOUD (with a chosen read-tone like 'read as a eulogy'); room votes. Cards are SETUPS — they must be springboards where 8 different players can all land different funny answers (the 3-answers test is life or death here). Launch game, needs 150+." },
  { key: 'over_under', name: 'OVER/UNDER', mech: "A stat about a named subject; room debates the line ALOUD then secretly bets over/under; subject reveals the truth (phone receipts preferred: screen time, camera roll count). Cards must produce a DEBATABLE line + a gasp-or-vindication reveal. Above spice 3, truthful answers must not implicate present third parties (no 'people in this room' class). Launch game, needs 150+." },
  { key: 'confession_or_cap', name: 'CONFESSION OR CAP', mech: "Confessor is dealt 3 confessions, picks 1, locks TRUE/FALSE, tells the story aloud; jury votes believe/cap. Cards must be lie-worthy: plausible enough that either answer is believable, specific enough to perform, embarrassing enough to matter. First-person 'I once...' form. Launch game, needs 150+." },
  { key: 'scatterblast', name: 'SCATTERBLAST', mech: "Category + letter, hidden-fuse bomb passes around the circle by voice, holder at boom loses. Cards = category+letter pairs; the category must be shoutable under pressure with many valid answers, and funnier when answers get desperate. Launch game." },
  { key: 'poison_pitch', name: 'POISON PITCH', mech: "Two players are assigned opposite sides of a horrifying would-you-rather and pitch AGAINST each other; room votes best pitch (not best option). Both options must be EQUALLY terrible (balance is the mechanic) and pitchable — rich with angles a lawyer could argue. Launch game." },
  { key: 'red_flag_rally', name: 'RED FLAG RALLY', mech: "Defender gets PERK + RED FLAG and must sell the date; room votes SMASH/PASS; tie = SMASH. The perk must be genuinely tempting and the flag genuinely disqualifying — the gap IS the comedy; defense needs argument-room. Launch game." },
  { key: 'alibi_drop', name: 'ALIBI DROP', mech: "Accused gets an accusation + 3 mandatory words to smuggle into a 30s improvised alibi; jury hunts the planted words from an 8-word lineup. Accusations must be absurd-but-answerable; word triplets must be smuggleable-but-suspicious (the comedy is the contortion). Launch game." },
  { key: 'text_thread_trap', name: 'TEXT THREAD TRAP', mech: "Performer reads an incoming text aloud and improvises the reply in a mandatory tone; room votes SURVIVED/BLOCKED. The text must be a live grenade (demands a reply, high stakes); tones do half the comedy work. Content Drop 1." },
  { key: 'reality_check', name: 'REALITY CHECK', mech: "Subject secretly self-rates 1-10 on a dimension; the room debates their REAL number out loud IN FRONT of them; synced ego-gap reveal. Dimensions must be publicly debatable (the room has evidence) and ego-relevant. Content Drop 1." },
  { key: 'taboo_timer', name: 'TABOO TIMER', mech: "Clue-giver describes a word avoiding 5 forbidden words; room shouts guesses; Buzzer phone watches for violations. Words must be culturally alive and the forbidden list must remove all the easy routes. Content Drop 1." },
  { key: 'hot_seat_imposter', name: 'HOT SEAT IMPOSTER', mech: "Imposter answers personal questions AS a silent target player; room votes REAL/FAKE. Questions must be answerable-by-observation for close friends but revealing under improv pressure. Gated to IRL-familiar crews. Content Drop 1." },
  { key: 'title_fight', name: 'TITLE FIGHT', mech: "Instant head-to-head physical/verbal duel between two players. Now a scheduled physical spike / host-triggered Fight Break. Cards must be executable in a living room in 30s with a clear crowd-judgeable winner." },
  { key: 'the_unifying_theory', name: 'THE UNIFYING THEORY', mech: "CUT from the game (highest cognitive load, dead-air risk). Autopsy for SALVAGE only: which trios could convert into material for other games (e.g. Poison Pitch angles, Scatterblast categories)?" },
]

const deckPrompt = (g) => `You are a card-deck pathologist for HELLDECK, a party game being rebuilt. The owner's verdict: 'the cards suck.' Your job: prove exactly HOW, card by card, so the rebuild can't repeat the mistakes.
Read the full deck: ${CORPUS}/${g.key}.md (every card, with spice ratings).
THE GAME THIS DECK SERVES (new mechanics): ${g.mech}
${TAXONOMY}
${CRAFT}
Give EVERY card a verdict (KILL / REWRITE / KEEP) — judge each against the game's core verb, the taxonomy, and the taste hierarchy. Be harsh: the corpus average should NOT survive. KEEP means you'd ship it unchanged in the rebuilt game (CONTEXTUAL tier minimum). Tally exact stats (kill+rewrite+keep must equal the deck size). Identify this deck's dominant failure modes with counts, list the keep_ids, pick the 8-12 most INSTRUCTIVE examples (worst offenders + the rare gems + the borderline teaching cases), and write concrete rebuild requirements for this deck.`

const analysts = [
  { label: 'analyze:voice', prompt: `You are a corpus voice analyst for HELLDECK (917 party-game cards being rebuilt because 'the cards suck'). Read ALL 14 deck files in ${CORPUS}/ (roast_consensus.md, fill_in_finisher.md, over_under.md, confession_or_cap.md, scatterblast.md, poison_pitch.md, red_flag_rally.md, alibi_drop.md, text_thread_trap.md, reality_check.md, taboo_timer.md, hot_seat_imposter.md, title_fight.md, the_unifying_theory.md).
Quantify the HOMOGENEITY problem: (1) count occurrences across the whole corpus of the recycled reference set (ex/exes, therapist/therapy, group chat, OnlyFans, feet pics, mugshot, LinkedIn, Spotify Wrapped, dating app/Tinder/situationship, true crime, cult, 3am, search/browser history, Venmo, podcast, screenshots, red flag) — exact counts per term; (2) estimate what % of cards are written in the same 'relatable millennial-Twitter observational' register vs any OTHER comedic register (absurdist, deadpan, menacing, unhinged-specific, surreal, petty-domestic, physical); (3) find the 10 most-duplicated card SKELETONS across decks (same structure, swapped nouns); (4) name what registers/reference-worlds are MISSING entirely. Output rebuild_rules: a voice-diversity quota system the rebuild must follow.`, schema: ANALYST_SCHEMA },
  { label: 'analyze:structure', prompt: `You are a game-content structure analyst. HELLDECK's 917 cards are being rebuilt. Core question: per deck, does the card SET PLAYERS UP to be funny (generative — Jackbox's law, decay-proof) or DELIVER a prewritten joke (static — CAH's decay curve)? Read all 14 deck files in ${CORPUS}/ and score each deck 0-10 on player-generativity (what fraction of the comedy is produced BY the room vs printed on the card), with 3 example cards each way per deck.
Also: read ${ASSETS}/templates_v3/roast_consensus_enhanced.json, ${ASSETS}/templates_v3/fill_in_finisher.json, ${ASSETS}/lexicons_v2/sexual_innuendo.json and ${ASSETS}/lexicons_v2/red_flag_traits.json — verdict on whether ANY madlib slot-filling system can produce ship-grade cards for this game, or whether the rebuild should be 100% hand-written corpus + {NAME} insertion only (evidence: grammar breakage, semantic pairing failures, the 'random slot combos are not jokes' audit). Output rebuild_rules: per-game structural requirements (what the card must leave for the room to complete) and the template-system verdict.`, schema: ANALYST_SCHEMA },
  { label: 'analyze:spice', prompt: `You are a spice-calibration analyst. HELLDECK cards carry spice 2-5 (53% at spice 3; no spice 1 exists). The design now REQUIRES two decoupled axes: EXPOSURE (how personal/targeted — what consent ceilings cap) and CHAOS (how deranged — never capped). Read all 14 deck files in ${CORPUS}/.
Audit: (1) reclassify a representative sample (~15 cards per spice tier, across decks) onto the two axes — how often does the current single number conflate them?; (2) find the 'corporate icebreaker band' (low spice AND low chaos — the WHOLESOME violation) with counts per deck; (3) find the 'dark not funny band' (high exposure, no benign frame) with counts; (4) find every card violating the new hard ban: above exposure 3, the truthful answer names/implicates present third parties or can out orientation/fidelity/health — list their IDs; (5) define the rebuilt 2-axis rating rubric with 3 calibration example cards per exposure tier 1-5 showing chaos held HIGH at every tier ('feral-but-impersonal' must exist at exposure 1). Output rebuild_rules: the rating rubric + per-tier quotas.`, schema: ANALYST_SCHEMA },
]

const trioPrompt = `Read the game design spec at ${S}/spec_v3.md (the Read tool) — v3 FINAL of the HELLDECK redesign (every player on own phone, no TV, browser-based, DO-authoritative). Supporting rules detail: ${S}/report_read_rules-interactions.md. You are re-running the trio3 playtest: 3 best friends since high school, quiet apartment, maximum trust, want maximum depth. Their v2 run was MIXED 6/10 with a FATAL: minN=4 gates deleted Over/Under, Confession or Cap, Alibi Drop, Reality Check from their night, starving the pool. v3 claims fixes: minN=3 restored on those games (+ Red Flag, Poison Pitch auto-variant), Fill-In N=3 derangement reads (reader ≠ author, authorship ambiguous), depth gated by pool arithmetic (N=3 caps at Standard Descent with honest warning), FACE YOUR ACCUSERS capped at spice ≤3 with no repeated voter→victim edges, per-target heat ceilings, deal ceremonies, PLEAD THE FIFTH, I REST MY CASE. Simulate the full night minute-by-minute in your head (Standard Descent, 7 circles, all three pick ceiling 5, FERAL). Assume good card content. Verify each fix, hunt regressions, score honestly. Would the verdict flip to FUN, and would one of them now pay $9.99 to host?`

phase('Deck autopsies')
const deckResults = await parallel(GAMES.map(g => () => agent(deckPrompt(g), { label: `deck:${g.key}`, phase: 'Deck autopsies', schema: DECK_SCHEMA })))

phase('Cross-analysis')
const analystResults = await parallel([
  ...analysts.map(a => () => agent(a.prompt, { label: a.label, phase: 'Cross-analysis', schema: a.schema })),
  () => agent(trioPrompt, { label: 'replay:trio3-v3', phase: 'Validate', schema: PLAYTEST_SCHEMA }),
])

const deckDigest = GAMES.map((g, i) => {
  const r = deckResults[i]
  if (!r) return { game: g.name, error: 'agent died' }
  return { game: g.name, stats: r.stats, modes: r.failure_mode_counts, diagnosis: r.deck_diagnosis, requirements: r.rebuild_requirements }
})

phase('Synthesize')
const synthesis = await agent(`You are synthesizing the card autopsy for HELLDECK's full content rebuild. Here are the per-deck pathology digests (stats = per-card KILL/REWRITE/KEEP counts):
${JSON.stringify(deckDigest, null, 1)}

And the three cross-cutting analyst reports:
VOICE: ${JSON.stringify(analystResults[0], null, 1)}
STRUCTURE: ${JSON.stringify(analystResults[1], null, 1)}
SPICE AXES: ${JSON.stringify(analystResults[2], null, 1)}

Produce THE AUTOPSY VERDICT as a single dense report (max 1200 words): (1) corpus-wide kill/rewrite/keep totals and what that means; (2) the unified failure taxonomy ranked by how much fun each mode destroyed, with the mechanism of harm for each (why exactly does this mode kill laughs at a real table); (3) the 5 deepest root causes (not symptoms — e.g. 'cards were written to be read silently by one person, not performed to a room'); (4) THE TEN COMMANDMENTS: the ten binding writing laws for the rebuild, each one sentence, each traceable to evidence; (5) per-deck one-line rebuild directives with target deck sizes for the LAUNCH 8 (Roast Consensus, Fill-In Finisher, Over/Under, Confession or Cap, Scatterblast, Poison Pitch, Red Flag Rally, Alibi Drop at 150+/150+/150+/150+/80/100/100/80) and Content Drop 1.`,
  { label: 'synthesize:autopsy', phase: 'Synthesize', schema: { type: 'object', properties: { report: { type: 'string' } }, required: ['report'] } })

return { decks: deckResults, analysts: { voice: analystResults[0], structure: analystResults[1], spice: analystResults[2] }, trio3_v3: analystResults[3], synthesis }
