input.content:
# HDRealRules2.md — THE DESCENT

## The canonical rules for HELLDECK 2.0

_Successor to HDRealRules.md. That document described a pass-the-phone game; this one describes a possession._

_Every player on their own phone. Same room. No TV. One person pays; everyone else walks in through a browser. Screens carry secrets and votes — mouths carry the comedy._

**Design provenance:** this ruleset survived 9 simulated playtests (6 group archetypes + 3 re-validations) and 4 adversarial audits (pacing, social dynamics, technical reality, business). Final round: bar-table group 8/10 FUN, 13-player chaos party 7.5/10 FUN. Every rule below that looks weirdly specific exists because a simulation broke without it.

---

# PART I — THE NIGHT

## 1. Joining

- The host opens HELLDECK (web or Android app), starts a **Night**, gets a 4-letter room code + QR.
- Everyone else scans the QR or types the code into their phone browser. Name + avatar. No install. No account. Joiners are always free.
- The host plays too. Anyone can host from any phone (the game brain lives server-side).
- 3–12 players. Latecomers and overflow become **Imps** (see §9).

## 2. The Descent

A Night is a descent through **Circles**. One circle = one mini-game round, 3–4 minutes.

| Depth | Circles | Runtime (gameplay) |
|---|---|---|
| **Quick Dip** | 5 | ~17–22 min |
| **Standard Descent** | 7 | ~24–30 min |
| **Full Damnation** | 9 | ~28–36 min |

- Depth options are gated by pool arithmetic: at 3 players the lobby caps at Standard, with the warning: _"9 circles for 3 sinners means the wheel comes back around — bring more bodies for Full Damnation."_
- **The deeper you go, the hotter it gets.** Content heat ramps circle by circle (the 36-questions escalation curve). The finale is the most personal content the room's consent allows.
- Single-performer games run **2 loops with 2 different performers** per circle — keeps circles 3–4 min and doubles spotlight spread.

## 3. Consent (invisible, unhinged-preserving)

Two axes, decoupled — this is load-bearing for the whole content system:

> **Spice caps EXPOSURE (how personal), never CHAOS (how deranged).**
> A consent-capped night is still feral. The cap removes the knife, never the chaos.

- During join, every player privately picks their **heat ceiling** (1–5) on their own phone.
- A card **naming subject X never exceeds X's own ceiling.** The one shy player is fully protected; the feral majority still gets a feral night about each other.
- Generic (no-subject) cards obey the room ceiling: second-lowest pick (lowest at N≤4).
- Vote-emergent-victim games (Roast Consensus) obey the lowest pick above exposure 3.
- **No ceiling number is ever rendered anywhere.** The ceiling is a silent server-side deck filter. Nobody can tell "capped" from "that's what the deck dealt." No witch hunts.
- **Vibe check** (host, at lobby): SOBER / WARMED UP / FERAL sets the starting rung. Precedence: ceiling always wins; start = min(vibe rung, ceiling − 2), floor 1 — every night gets at least two escalation steps.
- 18+: the join page carries a one-tap 18+ attestation; the host attests the room at Night creation; depth 4–5 requires it.

## 4. The arc

The shuffler builds each Night's game sequence under these laws:

- Open with simultaneous-input games (everyone acts at once, no spotlight pressure).
- Physical spike mid-descent (Scatterblast; Title Fight is the second spike).
- Spotlight games only after the room is warm.
- **Anti-roast-fatigue grammar:** after circle 4, never two subject-targeting circles back-to-back; at least one aim-OUTWARD circle (Scatterblast, Taboo, Poison Pitch, Red Flag, Title Fight) per third of the night. A palate cleanser isn't wholesome — it's the room uncoiling so the next hit lands.
- N-aware: every game carries minN/maxN; the shuffler never deals a mathematically broken game.
- Repeats allowed at small N for proven repeaters (Roast, Scatterblast, Over/Under) with fresh content. Never repeat single-performer games.

## 5. The finale and THE JUDGMENT

- Final circle = **×3 points**, restricted to all-players-score games (Roast Consensus, Fill-In Finisher, Over/Under; at N≤4 prefer Reality Check or Over/Under). In finale Fill-In, last place is never dealt Reader, and the Reader earns performance points (🔥 during the read converts) — nobody is excluded from the climax.
- **Devil's Bargain:** last place secretly carries ×2 on a late all-players-score circle. Revealed with drama at Judgment ("SAM SOLD THEIR SOUL AT CIRCLE 6"), never silently applied.
- **THE JUDGMENT:** winner crowned + superlatives generated from the night's real telemetry (Most Roasted, The Liar, Fastest Fingers, Biggest Ego Gap…). A screenshot-shaped **share card** lands on every phone — each joiner's copy carries a "host your own descent" link.
- No elimination, ever. Option to descend again.

---

# PART II — HOW ROUNDS RUN

## 6. Pacing laws (server-enforced)

1. One task per phone per moment. All input is simultaneous. No sequential turns anywhere.
2. **SKIPPABLE inputs** (votes, punchlines): timer fires → auto-abstain / auto-panic-answer. The game never waits for stragglers.
3. **BLOCKING inputs** (truth-locks, subject numbers, role acks): timer pauses with a public shame countdown ("WAITING ON SAM'S RECEIPTS…"). Every blocking input has terminal states:
   - 30–45s → the room votes **DRAG THEM BACK or FEED THEM TO THE PIT** (60% = round voided, mocking sting, re-deal a no-subject game), and the host gets **VOID ROUND**.
   - Seat expiry (90s disconnected) auto-voids any blocking input it owns.
   - **PLEAD THE FIFTH** is always available to the subject: forfeit round points, outward sting ("THE WITNESS TAKES THE FIFTH. COWARDICE NOTED."), costs nothing. Never fabricate ground truth; never trap a human.
4. **Reveals hold for the roast.** Reveals render simultaneously on every phone (visual-first: flash/pulse — audio is garnish, bars are loud). Then the reveal HOLDS while the table riffs; any player can DESCEND after the 45s soft cap, and reaction-decay (no 🔥 for 8s past 20s) auto-advances. Server owns inputs; humans own aftermath.
5. **I REST MY CASE:** performers can end their own timer. Bold is rewarded; dead air is not.
6. **SKIP-'EM re-deals the CARD, never the human:** if 60% of the room piles on during a stalled performance, same performer gets new ammo — sting blames the card ("THAT CARD WAS TRASH — NEW WEAPON"). Once per circle. Disabled after I REST MY CASE and in the final 10s.
7. **Deal ceremony:** every deal runs a fixed 5–6s ritual on all phones ("THE DECK IS CHOOSING ITS VICTIM…"). It absorbs exactly one Brimstone burn with the backup card pre-fetched — burned and clean deals are timing-identical.
8. Explainers: 15s the first time a game appears for this group; 5s title card after.
9. Reveal grammar: any reveal payload must be glanceable in <2 seconds (a name, a number, a verdict) or be stage-sequenced into beats.

## 7. Scoring

- **Points go to the bold:** winning punchlines, survived pitches, believed lies, planted words. Voters earn a flat +1 for voting — never for aligning with the majority (no predictable-vote metagame; a player who never risks anything must not win the night).
- Exception: Roast Consensus keeps majority-alignment scoring + **Room Heat** (consensus IS that game): unanimity-minus-victim at N≤5, 60% at N≥8.
- Finale ×3. Devil's Bargain ×2. Descent Rank ladder shows 5s between circles.
- Feedback is invisible: 🔥 reaction spam during reveal-holds doubles as card telemetry. There is no feedback phase.

## 8. Safety valves (the license for exposure 4–5)

- **Brimstone tokens** — 2 per player per night, use-or-lose (no hoarding reward): burn to veto a card or dodge a spotlight.
- **Subject pre-view:** any card naming a subject renders on the subject's phone **≥10 seconds** before the room (inside the deal ceremony — never make a safety valve faster than a sip of beer). Silent BURN → backup ships with zero timing delta. No animation, no trace, no counter.
- **Spotlight burn window:** performer assignments arrive privately 10s before the room learns who was picked; the room sees a generic "DEALING…" face.
- UGC hook: the Reader/host can strike any written punchline at the teleprompter ("BURNED — NEW LINE").
- Writing-level bans (deck-side, not runtime): no card targets appearance, family death, or protected traits; above exposure 3, no card whose truthful answer names or implicates specific present third parties (the "in this room" desire/history class is dead), and none whose answer can out orientation, fidelity, or health.

## 9. Imps (latecomers & overflow)

Imps are citizens, not ghosts:
- Their names enter the card pool (roastable = included).
- They vote at 0.5 weight and fuel Room Heat.
- At N≥7 Fill-In they contribute 1 curated answer, shown as an on-screen cameo.
- Conversion to full player at circle boundaries, in join order, up to the 12-cap — announced as a role ("ZOE IS TONIGHT'S IMP — feed her souls"). Residual Imps get a guaranteed Judgment superlative and first-in-line conversion.

---

# PART III — THE GAMES

## LAUNCH 8

### 1. Roast Consensus 🎯 (minN 3 · launch · 150+ cards)
Three prompts per circle. Everyone secretly votes which PRESENT player fits ("Who would…"). Synced reveal: victim's name in lights; reveal holds for the roast.
- Plurality tie → **"THE ROOM IS TORN — DOUBLE ROAST"** (both names in lights; either vote scores).
- N=3–4 → attributed mode **"FACE YOUR ACCUSERS"** (votes shown with names — the negotiation is the comedy). Capped at exposure ≤3; the same voter→victim edge never prints twice a night.
- Cards must make the vote contested (2–3 plausible victims in any group) and hand the room roast ammunition.

### 2. Fill-In Finisher ✍️ (minN 3 · launch · 150+ cards)
A setup with a blank. Everyone but the Reader writes a punchline (panic button = 2 curated fallbacks at half points). Answers go ONLY to the Reader's teleprompter. **Perform, then vote:** while others write, the Reader picks the read-tone ("read these as: a eulogy / a 911 call / a hostage tape"); then performs every answer aloud — first exposure is ALWAYS out loud to the room. Vote grid (answer texts, randomized order) unlocks after the last read; no self-votes; winner + author reveal.
- N≥7: **two Readers**, one per setup, alternating. Per setup: ≤4 answers in one run → one vote picks the top → single cross-setup grand face-off.
- N=3: no Reader — reads assigned by random derangement (never your own; authorship stays ambiguous); all three vote among the other two.

### 3. Over/Under 📉 (minN 3 · launch · 150+ cards)
Two subjects per circle. A stat about the subject appears; the room debates the line OUT LOUD (that argument is the game); everyone secretly bets OVER/UNDER; the subject fetches the truth (phone receipts preferred: screen time, camera roll count, unread emails). Receipt fetch is a BLOCKING input (pause + shame countdown + terminals). Synced reveal: gasp or vindication.

### 4. Confession or Cap 🤥 (minN 3 · launch · 150+ cards)
Confessor is dealt **3 confessions, privately picks 1** (consent and strategy in one move), locks TRUE/FALSE, performs the story aloud. Jury secretly votes believe/cap. Tie → confessor wins ("HUNG JURY — THE LIAR WALKS").

### 5. Scatterblast 💣 (minN 3 · launch · 80 cards)
Category + letter on all phones; the stage phone (or any volunteered phone) is the bomb with a hidden fuse; the turn passes around the circle by voice. BOOM → every phone flashes **"WHO DIED?"** — majority tap in 5s assigns the loser ("THE TABLE HAS SPOKEN").

### 6. Poison Pitch 💀 (minN 3 · launch · 100 cards)
Two players privately assigned opposite sides of a horrifying would-you-rather; 30s pitches each (both options must be EQUALLY terrible — balance is the mechanic); secret vote for best pitch. Tie → 10s sudden-death rebuttals + revote; second tie → split pot. Down-weighted at N=4.
- N=3 auto-variant: the judge rates each pitch 1–5 on a damage meter (non-zero-sum) and pitchers secretly predict their own score for bonus.

### 7. Red Flag Rally 🚩 (minN 3 · launch · 100 cards)
Defender gets PERK + RED FLAG, 45s to sell the date; secret SMASH/PASS; synced verdict. Tie → SMASH (performers win ties). The perk must genuinely tempt, the flag must genuinely disqualify — the gap is the comedy.

### 8. Alibi Drop 🕵️ (minN 3 · launch · 80 cards)
The accused gets the accusation + 3 mandatory words on their phone only; 30s improvised alibi aloud; the jury privately picks 3 suspected plants from an 8-word lineup. Reveal is stage-sequenced word-by-word with a sting per planted word.

## CONTENT DROP 1 (free, post-launch)

### 9. Text Thread Trap 📱 (minN 3)
Incoming text + mandatory tone delivered privately; performer reads the text aloud and improvises the reply IN TONE; SURVIVED/BLOCKED vote; tie → SURVIVED.

### 10. Reality Check 🪞 (minN 3)
Subject privately self-rates 1–10. The room debates their REAL number OUT LOUD in front of them (do not privatize the debate — it IS the game); a named non-subject scribe enters the consensus; synced ego-gap reveal. Preferred N≤4 finale.

### 11. Taboo Timer ⏱️ (minN 4)
Clue-giver sees word + forbidden list; a designated Buzzer phone also sees the forbidden list (the role finally works); guessers' phones show only the timer.

### 12. Hot Seat Imposter 🎭 (minN 5)
Private roles (Spyfall pattern); askers get suggested questions; performance aloud; secret REAL/FAKE vote. Gated to IRL-familiar crews (the lobby asks once per crew).

## SPIKES & CUTS
- **Title Fight 🥊** — scheduled as the arc's second physical spike + host-triggered "Fight Break." Living-room-executable duels with a crowd-judgeable winner.
- **The Unifying Theory** — CUT (highest cognitive load, worst dead-air risk). Salvage its best trios as Poison Pitch angles and Scatterblast categories.

---

# PART IV — SYSTEMS

## 10. Personalization & crew memory
- {NAME} slots use real lobby names; a spotlight-fairness counter spreads targeting.
- **Fresh Meat protocol:** first-night names are excluded from knowledge-dependent subject roles (Hot Seat, Reality Check, personal-history Over/Under) and draw first-impression variants ("Who's most likely to have already judged everyone's shoes?").
- **IRL-familiarity gate** (once per crew): online-first crews get persona/phone-legible pools; Hot Seat & Reality Check gated out.
- **Crew memory stores ONLY:** scores, win records, superlative titles, night count. **Never:** card text, confession truth values, who-voted-whom, any answers. Memory lives host-side; server room state auto-expires after ~30 days idle.
- Cloud AI is a content-refresh pipeline (draft → human curation) only. Never a runtime dependency. Never the joke author. Never a judge.

## 11. Platform & architecture
- **The game brain is server-side** (one Cloudflare Durable Object per room, TypeScript, authoritative). Every phone — host included — is a renderer over WebSocket. Phones background, die, and go to the kitchen; the room survives all of it.
- **One UI runtime: the web client.** The Android app is a thin shell (Play Billing, stage audio foreground service, lift-to-sin accelerometer bridge, wake-lock) around the same web bundle. No game screen is ever built twice.
- Synced reveals via clock-offset scheduling (≤150ms p95 skew); countdowns derive from one deadline; reveals are idempotent state (rejoiners render current truth). Reaction taps animate locally and coalesce.
- Rejoin is automatic (room code in URL + player token in localStorage). Blocking-input seats auto-void at 90s. Host controls fail over to the next player in join order.
- **The Stage** (optional, N≥5): host phone face-up mid-table renders PUBLIC-ONLY faces (timers, tallies, reveals, audio). **Lift-to-sin** for short ballots: "NICK — PICK UP TO SIN" → lift flips to a shielded private ballot (thumb-hold to reveal), lay flat restores the stage. Long host inputs suspend the stage to a "THE HOST IS SINNING" banner. No phase may assume the stage phone is stationary.

## 12. Monetization
- Joiners: free, always, forever. No ads, ever. No subscription.
- New host's first full Night: free.
- **$9.99 one-time "Host the Descent"** — the paywall sits on the SECOND Night's "BEGIN THE DESCENT" button, lobby already assembled behind it (buy in under 30 seconds at the moment of blocked intent). The Judgment share card seeds the loop: every joiner leaves with a "host your own descent" link.
- Web checkout for browser hosts; Play Billing in the Android shell. Content packs ($2.99–4.99) later. Rating: Mature 17+.

## 13. Dead by design (v1 systems this ruleset kills)
On-device LLM + 1.7GB bundled models (never fired in production; blocked Play publishing) · pass-the-phone voting · "Seat N" anonymized cards · LOL/MEH/TRASH feedback modal · Thompson sampling over bypassed templates · madlib runtime card assembly · per-device premium unlock · kiosk/device-admin mode · infinite session with buried End Game.

# HDRealRules2.md — THE DESCENT

## The canonical rules for HELLDECK 2.0

_Successor to HDRealRules.md. That document described a pass-the-phone game; this one describes a possession._

_Every player on their own phone. Same room. No TV. One person pays; everyone else walks in through a browser. Screens carry secrets and votes — mouths carry the comedy._

**Design provenance:** this ruleset survived 9 simulated playtests (6 group archetypes + 3 re-validations) and 4 adversarial audits (pacing, social dynamics, technical reality, business). Final round: bar-table group 8/10 FUN, 13-player chaos party 7.5/10 FUN. Every rule below that looks weirdly specific exists because a simulation broke without it.

---

# PART I — THE NIGHT

## 1. Joining

- The host opens HELLDECK (web or Android app), starts a **Night**, gets a 4-letter room code + QR.
- Everyone else scans the QR or types the code into their phone browser. Name + avatar. No install. No account. Joiners are always free.
- The host plays too. Anyone can host from any phone (the game brain lives server-side).
- 3–12 players. Latecomers and overflow become **Imps** (see §9).

## 2. The Descent

A Night is a descent through **Circles**. One circle = one mini-game round, 3–4 minutes.

| Depth | Circles | Runtime (gameplay) |
|---|---|---|
| **Quick Dip** | 5 | ~17–22 min |
| **Standard Descent** | 7 | ~24–30 min |
| **Full Damnation** | 9 | ~28–36 min |

- Depth options are gated by pool arithmetic: at 3 players the lobby caps at Standard, with the warning: _"9 circles for 3 sinners means the wheel comes back around — bring more bodies for Full Damnation."_
- **The deeper you go, the hotter it gets.** Content heat ramps circle by circle (the 36-questions escalation curve). The finale is the most personal content the room's consent allows.
- Single-performer games run **2 loops with 2 different performers** per circle — keeps circles 3–4 min and doubles spotlight spread.

## 3. Consent (invisible, unhinged-preserving)

Two axes, decoupled — this is load-bearing for the whole content system:

> **Spice caps EXPOSURE (how personal), never CHAOS (how deranged).**
> A consent-capped night is still feral. The cap removes the knife, never the chaos.

- During join, every player privately picks their **heat ceiling** (1–5) on their own phone.
- A card **naming subject X never exceeds X's own ceiling.** The one shy player is fully protected; the feral majority still gets a feral night about each other.
- Generic (no-subject) cards obey the room ceiling: second-lowest pick (lowest at N≤4).
- Vote-emergent-victim games (Roast Consensus) obey the lowest pick above exposure 3.
- **No ceiling number is ever rendered anywhere.** The ceiling is a silent server-side deck filter. Nobody can tell "capped" from "that's what the deck dealt." No witch hunts.
- **Vibe check** (host, at lobby): SOBER / WARMED UP / FERAL sets the starting rung. Precedence: ceiling always wins; start = min(vibe rung, ceiling − 2), floor 1 — every night gets at least two escalation steps.
- 18+: the join page carries a one-tap 18+ attestation; the host attests the room at Night creation; depth 4–5 requires it.

## 4. The arc

The shuffler builds each Night's game sequence under these laws:

- Open with simultaneous-input games (everyone acts at once, no spotlight pressure).
- Physical spike mid-descent (Scatterblast; Title Fight is the second spike).
- Spotlight games only after the room is warm.
- **Anti-roast-fatigue grammar:** after circle 4, never two subject-targeting circles back-to-back; at least one aim-OUTWARD circle (Scatterblast, Taboo, Poison Pitch, Red Flag, Title Fight) per third of the night. A palate cleanser isn't wholesome — it's the room uncoiling so the next hit lands.
- N-aware: every game carries minN/maxN; the shuffler never deals a mathematically broken game.
- Repeats allowed at small N for proven repeaters (Roast, Scatterblast, Over/Under) with fresh content. Never repeat single-performer games.

## 5. The finale and THE JUDGMENT

- Final circle = **×3 points**, restricted to all-players-score games (Roast Consensus, Fill-In Finisher, Over/Under; at N≤4 prefer Reality Check or Over/Under). In finale Fill-In, last place is never dealt Reader, and the Reader earns performance points (🔥 during the read converts) — nobody is excluded from the climax.
- **Devil's Bargain:** last place secretly carries ×2 on a late all-players-score circle. Revealed with drama at Judgment ("SAM SOLD THEIR SOUL AT CIRCLE 6"), never silently applied.
- **THE JUDGMENT:** winner crowned + superlatives generated from the night's real telemetry (Most Roasted, The Liar, Fastest Fingers, Biggest Ego Gap…). A screenshot-shaped **share card** lands on every phone — each joiner's copy carries a "host your own descent" link.
- No elimination, ever. Option to descend again.

---

# PART II — HOW ROUNDS RUN

## 6. Pacing laws (server-enforced)

1. One task per phone per moment. All input is simultaneous. No sequential turns anywhere.
2. **SKIPPABLE inputs** (votes, punchlines): timer fires → auto-abstain / auto-panic-answer. The game never waits for stragglers.
3. **BLOCKING inputs** (truth-locks, subject numbers, role acks): timer pauses with a public shame countdown ("WAITING ON SAM'S RECEIPTS…"). Every blocking input has terminal states:
   - 30–45s → the room votes **DRAG THEM BACK or FEED THEM TO THE PIT** (60% = round voided, mocking sting, re-deal a no-subject game), and the host gets **VOID ROUND**.
   - Seat expiry (90s disconnected) auto-voids any blocking input it owns.
   - **PLEAD THE FIFTH** is always available to the subject: forfeit round points, outward sting ("THE WITNESS TAKES THE FIFTH. COWARDICE NOTED."), costs nothing. Never fabricate ground truth; never trap a human.
4. **Reveals hold for the roast.** Reveals render simultaneously on every phone (visual-first: flash/pulse — audio is garnish, bars are loud). Then the reveal HOLDS while the table riffs; any player can DESCEND after the 45s soft cap, and reaction-decay (no 🔥 for 8s past 20s) auto-advances. Server owns inputs; humans own aftermath.
5. **I REST MY CASE:** performers can end their own timer. Bold is rewarded; dead air is not.
6. **SKIP-'EM re-deals the CARD, never the human:** if 60% of the room piles on during a stalled performance, same performer gets new ammo — sting blames the card ("THAT CARD WAS TRASH — NEW WEAPON"). Once per circle. Disabled after I REST MY CASE and in the final 10s.
7. **Deal ceremony:** every deal runs a fixed 5–6s ritual on all phones ("THE DECK IS CHOOSING ITS VICTIM…"). It absorbs exactly one Brimstone burn with the backup card pre-fetched — burned and clean deals are timing-identical.
8. Explainers: 15s the first time a game appears for this group; 5s title card after.
9. Reveal grammar: any reveal payload must be glanceable in <2 seconds (a name, a number, a verdict) or be stage-sequenced into beats.

## 7. Scoring

- **Points go to the bold:** winning punchlines, survived pitches, believed lies, planted words. Voters earn a flat +1 for voting — never for aligning with the majority (no predictable-vote metagame; a player who never risks anything must not win the night).
- Exception: Roast Consensus keeps majority-alignment scoring + **Room Heat** (consensus IS that game): unanimity-minus-victim at N≤5, 60% at N≥8.
- Finale ×3. Devil's Bargain ×2. Descent Rank ladder shows 5s between circles.
- Feedback is invisible: 🔥 reaction spam during reveal-holds doubles as card telemetry. There is no feedback phase.

## 8. Safety valves (the license for exposure 4–5)

- **Brimstone tokens** — 2 per player per night, use-or-lose (no hoarding reward): burn to veto a card or dodge a spotlight.
- **Subject pre-view:** any card naming a subject renders on the subject's phone **≥10 seconds** before the room (inside the deal ceremony — never make a safety valve faster than a sip of beer). Silent BURN → backup ships with zero timing delta. No animation, no trace, no counter.
- **Spotlight burn window:** performer assignments arrive privately 10s before the room learns who was picked; the room sees a generic "DEALING…" face.
- UGC hook: the Reader/host can strike any written punchline at the teleprompter ("BURNED — NEW LINE").
- Writing-level bans (deck-side, not runtime): no card targets appearance, family death, or protected traits; above exposure 3, no card whose truthful answer names or implicates specific present third parties (the "in this room" desire/history class is dead), and none whose answer can out orientation, fidelity, or health.

## 9. Imps (latecomers & overflow)

Imps are citizens, not ghosts:
- Their names enter the card pool (roastable = included).
- They vote at 0.5 weight and fuel Room Heat.
- At N≥7 Fill-In they contribute 1 curated answer, shown as an on-screen cameo.
- Conversion to full player at circle boundaries, in join order, up to the 12-cap — announced as a role ("ZOE IS TONIGHT'S IMP — feed her souls"). Residual Imps get a guaranteed Judgment superlative and first-in-line conversion.

---

# PART III — THE GAMES

## LAUNCH 8

### 1. Roast Consensus 🎯 (minN 3 · launch · 150+ cards)
Three prompts per circle. Everyone secretly votes which PRESENT player fits ("Who would…"). Synced reveal: victim's name in lights; reveal holds for the roast.
- Plurality tie → **"THE ROOM IS TORN — DOUBLE ROAST"** (both names in lights; either vote scores).
- N=3–4 → attributed mode **"FACE YOUR ACCUSERS"** (votes shown with names — the negotiation is the comedy). Capped at exposure ≤3; the same voter→victim edge never prints twice a night.
- Cards must make the vote contested (2–3 plausible victims in any group) and hand the room roast ammunition.

### 2. Fill-In Finisher ✍️ (minN 3 · launch · 150+ cards)
A setup with a blank. Everyone but the Reader writes a punchline (panic button = 2 curated fallbacks at half points). Answers go ONLY to the Reader's teleprompter. **Perform, then vote:** while others write, the Reader picks the read-tone ("read these as: a eulogy / a 911 call / a hostage tape"); then performs every answer aloud — first exposure is ALWAYS out loud to the room. Vote grid (answer texts, randomized order) unlocks after the last read; no self-votes; winner + author reveal.
- N≥7: **two Readers**, one per setup, alternating. Per setup: ≤4 answers in one run → one vote picks the top → single cross-setup grand face-off.
- N=3: no Reader — reads assigned by random derangement (never your own; authorship stays ambiguous); all three vote among the other two.

### 3. Over/Under 📉 (minN 3 · launch · 150+ cards)
Two subjects per circle. A stat about the subject appears; the room debates the line OUT LOUD (that argument is the game); everyone secretly bets OVER/UNDER; the subject fetches the truth (phone receipts preferred: screen time, camera roll count, unread emails). Receipt fetch is a BLOCKING input (pause + shame countdown + terminals). Synced reveal: gasp or vindication.

### 4. Confession or Cap 🤥 (minN 3 · launch · 150+ cards)
Confessor is dealt **3 confessions, privately picks 1** (consent and strategy in one move), locks TRUE/FALSE, performs the story aloud. Jury secretly votes believe/cap. Tie → confessor wins ("HUNG JURY — THE LIAR WALKS").

### 5. Scatterblast 💣 (minN 3 · launch · 80 cards)

## SPIKES & CUTS

- **The Unifying Theory** — CUT (highest cognitive load, worst dead-air risk). Salvage its best trios as Poison Pitch angles and Scatterblast categories.

---

python3 << 'EOF'
import json
with open('/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-nicholastoledo-Development-active-HELLDECK/4eb51e65-3e38-49e9-b745-6d128c919d2d/tasks/ww3s6xlp4.output') as f:
    data = json.load(f)
res = data['result']
outdir = '/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-nicholastoledo-Development-active-HELLDECK/4eb51e65-3e38-49e9-b745-6d128c919d2d/scratchpad'
games = ['roast_consensus','fill_in_finisher','over_under','confession_or_cap','scatterblast','poison_pitch','red_flag_rally','alibi_drop','text_thread_trap','reality_check','taboo_timer','hot_seat_imposter','title_fight','the_unifying_theory']
tk=tr=tp=0
for i, d in enumerate(res.get('decks', [])):
    if d:
        with open(f'{outdir}/autopsy_deck_{games[i]}.json','w') as f: json.dump(d, f, indent=1)
        s = d['stats']; tk+=s['kill']; tr+=s['rewrite']; tp+=s['keep']
        top = sorted(d['failure_mode_counts'], key=lambda m:-m['count'])[:3]
        print(f"{games[i]}: K{s['kill']}/R{s['rewrite']}/KEEP{s['keep']}  top: {', '.join(m['mode']+':'+str(m['count']) for m in top)}")
    else:
        print(f"{games[i]}: NULL")
print(f"\nTOTALS (13 decks): kill={tk} rewrite={tr} keep={tp}  (n={tk+tr+tp}) keep rate={tp/(tk+tr+tp)*100:.0f}%")
an = res.get('analysts') or {}
for k in ['voice','structure','spice']:
    v = an.get(k)
    if v:
        with open(f'{outdir}/autopsy_analyst_{k}.json','w') as f: json.dump(v, f, indent=1)
        print(f"analyst:{k}: saved ({len(v.get('findings',''))} chars, {len(v.get('rebuild_rules',[]))} rules)")
t = res.get('trio3_v3')
if t:
    with open(f'{outdir}/r3_trio3_v3.json','w') as f: json.dump(t, f, indent=1)
    print(f"\ntrio3-v3: {t['group_verdict']} {t['fun_score_1_10']}/10 pay={t['would_someone_pay_to_host']} majors={sum(1 for x in t['failures'] if x['severity'] in ('FATAL','MAJOR'))}")
EOF

content.content:
================================================================================
ANALYST: voice
================================================================================
CORPUS: 917 cards / 14 decks confirmed. The homogeneity problem is real and measurable on three axes.

(1) RECYCLED REFERENCES (cards containing term / total occurrences / decks present): ex-exes 59/69/12-of-14-decks — the single biggest crutch, 6.4% of the entire corpus, incl. 16 of text_thread_trap's 50 cards; therapist-therapy 15/22/8 decks; dating-app umbrella (dating app/profile 16, Tinder 5, Hinge 2, situationship 6, +swipe/match) = 33 cards; search-browser-internet history 10/14/8 decks; LinkedIn 10/14/7 decks; 3am 10 (+2am 5 = 15 "unholy hour" punchlines); group chat 7; body count 7 across 6 decks; screenshots 5; OnlyFans 5; podcast 5; Venmo 4; mugshot 3; cult 3; Spotify Wrapped 2 (Spotify any 4); feet pics/foot fetish 2; true crime 2; red flag 56 (50 are red_flag_rally's format; 6 elsewhere). Supporting monoculture: mom 18, hookup 13, Uber 9, boss 9, Yelp 7, TikTok 7. Umbrella stat: 38% of ALL 917 cards (348) literally name a phone/app/online/dating/therapy artifact; text_thread_trap 48/50, the_unifying_theory 32/53, over_under 33/62, taboo_timer 26/50.

(2) REGISTER SPLIT (card-level classification): ~84% (≈769 cards) are one voice — relatable millennial-Twitter observational recognition humor ("haha so true": ex-stalking, screen time, therapy-speak, brunch, Target breakdown). Remainder: absurdist/escalation ~10% (concentrated in alibi_drop, the only deck with a distinct voice — emotional support ham, trebuchet, pigeon negotiation — plus scattered poison_pitch/red_flag_rally), petty-physical ~1.3% (wet sock family), dark/menacing ~1.6% (shovel-bleach-tarp, shrine in trunk), recycled Reddit-canon debates ~2.2% (title_fight's duck-sized horses/Batman/pineapple — plagiarized internet, not a register), deadpan/surreal <1%. Four decks are 100% register-uniform: over_under (62), reality_check (45), hot_seat_imposter (53), text_thread_trap (50).

(3) TOP 10 DUPLICATED SKELETONS (same structure, swapped nouns): S1 "private digital artifact EXPOSED at public venue" — 17+ cards, 5 decks (browser history at wedding / Spotify Wrapped at interview / group chat at funeral / Hinge on billboard / drunk texts in newspaper / body count at family dinner / kinks on business cards / screen-time tattoo); S2 ex-surveillance (stalk/old-photo-like/Google Alert/go back to ex) — 59 cards, 12 decks; S3 "banned/kicked out/restraining order from wholesome venue" — 19 cards, 6 decks; S4 therapist-overwhelmed-by-you — 15 cards, 8 decks; S5 timestamp-as-punchline (2am/3am) — 18 cards, 7 decks; S6 "[bad behavior] + euphemism in quotes ('research'/'self-care'/'healing')" — 13 cards; S7 big-box-store emotional breakdown (Wendy's×3, Costco×3, IKEA×3, Target×2, Walmart×2, Chuck E. Cheese×2) — 13 cards; S8 "most unhinged thing in [Notes app/search history/camera roll]" — 11 cards, 3 decks; S9 search-history-as-evidence — 10 cards, 8 decks; S10 body-count-lie — 7 near-identical cards, 6 decks. WORSE: 15 literal cross-deck duplicate JOKES (~34 cards): Build-A-Bear stuffing (roast_53=confession_1), aggressive yoga (roast_32=confession_15), cried-at-interview-still-hired (roast_45=confession_19), accidental cult+robes (roast_54=confession_3), liked ex's 2017/2019 photo (roast_11=confession_21), rotisserie chicken (roast_7=confession_34), self-checkout fight (roast_38=confession_65), talking-stage-longer-than (×3), burner-account self-hype, faked-own-death (×3), fake-phone-call (×3), plus VERBATIM within-deck dupes: fill_in_finisher_20 = fill_in_finisher_56 ("worst fortune cookie"), poison_pitch_12 ≈ 66 (phone dies at 50%), poison_pitch_5 ≈ 76 (Morgan Freeman).

(4) MISSING ENTIRELY: registers — deadpan understatement, sustained menace, surreal/dream-logic, gross-out/body (earwax bust is the lone card), petty-domestic (roommate/thermostat/dishes ~3 cards), high-register parody voices (legalese, nature-doc narration, noir, corporate-memo — nature-doc appears once), physical/party-physical. Reference worlds — food/culinary beyond chains, animal kingdom beyond cameo pigeons, history/myth, small-town/rural (corpus is 100% urban-millennial), trades/blue-collar work (work = office/HR/LinkedIn only), sports, gaming (NPC/side-quest as slang only), music scene beyond Spotify, books/art/academia, cars, medicine beyond therapy, nature/outdoors, anything non-US. The one deck that escapes the monoculture (alibi_drop, ~55% absurdist) is the proof the format supports other voices. Corpus files: /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-nicholastoledo-Development-active-HELLDECK/4eb51e65-3e38-49e9-b745-6d128c919d2d/scratchpad/corpus/*.md.

KEY NUMBERS:
  - 917 cards / 14 decks total
  - ex/exes: 59 cards (6.4% of corpus), 69 occurrences, in 12/14 decks; 16/50 of text_thread_trap
  - 38% of all cards (348/917) name a phone/app/online/dating/therapy artifact
  - ~84% of cards in one register (relatable millennial-observational); absurdist ~10%; menacing ~1.6%; petty-physical ~1.3%; deadpan/surreal <1%
  - therapist/therapy: 15 cards / 22 occurrences / 8 decks
  - dating-app umbrella 33 cards (dating app/profile 16, Tinder 5, Hinge 2, situationship 6)
  - search/browser/internet history: 10 cards across 8 decks; LinkedIn: 10 cards across 7 decks
  - 3am: 10 cards + 2am: 5 = 15 unholy-hour punchlines across 7 decks

================================================================================
ANALYST: structure
================================================================================
Corpus confirmed: 917 cards, 14 decks (73+80+80+53+62+85+45+50+140+53+50+50+53+43). Decks split cleanly into ROOM-ENGINES (card is a rig; friends produce the comedy; decay-proof) and JOKE-DELIVERY (punchline printed; CAH decay curve).

Generativity scores (fraction of comedy produced by the room), with 3 generative / 3 static examples per deck by card id:
- HOT_SEAT_IMPOSTER 10 — pure probe + real-friend ground truth. Gen: 25 (honest Tinder bio), 29 (drunk alter ego), 45 (autobiography title). Weak: 6, 11, 13 — trivia (karaoke/Starbucks/rewatch) with no reveal delta.
- TABOO_TIMER 10 — word+forbidden list, clue-giver improvises everything. Gen: 22 BODY COUNT, 5 SITUATIONSHIP, 42 EDGING. Weak: 44 SLAY, 47 ERA, 38 SIDE QUEST (guessable in 2s; slang will date).
- SCATTERBLAST 9.5 — category+letter+10s, all output player-made. Gen: 51 (serial killer's search history/H), 10 (reasons your ex is your ex/L), 22 (worst things at a funeral/C). Weak: 25 (DATA BUG: text letter S, field letter "C", truncated category), 46, 47 (dated meme).
- ALIBI_DROP 9 — accusation + 3 mandatory words; player improvises the story. Gen: 71 (shovel/bleach/tarp), 1 (ex's apartment 3am), 22 (Walmart 4am bathrobe). Static-leaning: word triads that ARE the punchline — 5 ("Potassium deficiency"), 24 ("It started it"), 53 ("Main character energy").
- FILL_IN_FINISHER 9 — setup+blank, punchline 100% player-written. Gen: 14 (AirDrop at family dinner), 66 (Step 1/Step 2/Prison), 55 (body-count Yelp review). Weak: 56 (exact dup of 20), 40 (anecdote-fetch, not construction), 3 (double blank, unanchored).
- OVER_UNDER 9 — neutral stat, comedy = friend's real number vs room's model. Gen: 60 (hookup thoughts in this room), 13 (ex-stalking this week), 19 (panic-deletes). Weak: 15, 34, 23 — inventory stats, zero self-exposure.
- THE_UNIFYING_THEORY 8.5 — player writes the theory. Gen: 3 (bathroom/first date/job interview), 24 (dorm/prison/NYC apartment), 41 (casino/dating app/vending machine). Static: 14, 17, 28 — connection pre-solved on the card (all fake promises / all gaslighting / all hollow validation).
- REALITY_CHECK 8 — self-rating vs room-rating gap. Gen: 14 (talked behind backs in this room), 44 (actual-vs-perceived attractiveness), 4 (sell out the group). Broken frame: 8, 33, 37 — counts jammed into a 1-10 scale (~8 cards).
- TITLE_FIGHT 7.5 — arguments are the comedy but prompts are stock. Gen: 43 (drunk you vs hungry you), 16 (mom's vs dad's search history), 42 (ex writes eulogy vs dating profile). Generic: 3 (duck-sized horses), 7 (pineapple pizza), 4 (Batman/Superman) — Reddit chestnuts.
- TEXT_THREAD_TRAP 6.5 — reply is improvised, but ~15/50 setups land their own punchline. Gen: 2 ("We found something in your old room. Call me."), 30 ("The walls are thinner than you think."), 3 (boss, 11pm). Static: 41 ("...oh. Nevermind. That's on brand for you."), 42 ("JK they don't know about you."), 50 (sassy GPS) — sender steals the joke.
- ROAST_CONSENSUS 6 — vote at a real friend is the payoff, but the scenario is an authored one-liner. Gen: 55, 78, 43 ("in this room" stakes). Static: 77 ("struck oil"), 109 ("meeting could have been an email"), 53 (Build-A-Bear — recycled from confession_1).
- POISON_PITCH 5 — both horns fully authored; room adds only the pick+defense. Debate-fuel: 22, 28, 41. Coin-flip novelty: 2 (maple syrup/glitter), 8 (goose honk), 79 — joke entirely printed.
- CONFESSION_OR_CAP 3.5 — prewritten anecdotes with finished punchlines; ~60/80 too specific for anyone to claim, which breaks the bluff mechanic. Claimable: 5, 18, 74. Auto-cap: 78 (DoorDash punchline), 42 ("They knew."), 44. Dup: 13≈51.
- RED_FLAG_RALLY 3 — perk+flag both printed; player votes. Best (engineered irony): 25, 38, 39. Broken: 2, 5, 13 — 19/50 cards carry a DIFFERENT joke in extra fields than in display text.

TEMPLATE VERDICT: no madlib slot system can ship. roast_taboo_1 ("Most likely to get caught {innuendo} at {context}"): 75/75 sexual_innuendo entries are bare infinitives → 100% ungrammatical renders ("get caught handle a big load... of laundry at a work happy hour"); 73/75 embed their own "…" punchline → double-punchline collision ("finger... paint with the kids" + random context is a liability, not a joke). 10/20 roast_consensus_enhanced templates are one "{plan} — because {evidence}" skeleton; random cross-products are non-sequiturs — the funny in the corpus's best cards (red_flag 25/38/39) is the engineered dependency between halves, exactly what sampling can't make. needs_article="none" on 150/150 lexicon entries — grammar machinery never used. ~15/75 red_flag_traits are literal abuse descriptions (stalking, blackmail, isolation), unusable as comedy. The one working "template" file (fill_in_finisher.json) isn't slot-filling: its 10 blueprints are hand-written prompts, 5 duplicating corpus cards. Rebuild: 100% hand-written corpus + {NAME} insertion only (proper nouns are the only grammatically/semantically inert substitution), plus authored per-deck structured fields.

KEY NUMBERS:
  - 917 cards / 14 decks confirmed (73+80+80+53+62+85+45+50+140+53+50+50+53+43)
  - Generativity: hot_seat_imposter 10, taboo_timer 10, scatterblast 9.5, alibi_drop 9, fill_in_finisher 9, over_under 9, unifying_theory 8.5, reality_check 8, title_fight 7.5, text_thread_trap 6.5, roast_consensus 6, poison_pitch 5, confession_or_cap 3.5, red_flag_rally 3
  - 75/75 sexual_innuendo entries grammar-break the 'get caught ___' template frame (bare infinitive vs required gerund)
  - 73/75 sexual_innuendo entries carry an embedded '...' punchline → double-punchline collision with any template context
  - 19/50 red_flag_rally cards: display text and extra-field perk/red_flag are two different jokes
  - 10/20 roast_consensus_enhanced templates are the same '{plan} because {evidence}' skeleton reworded
  - 5/10 fill_in_finisher.json blueprints duplicate hand-written corpus cards verbatim
  - 150/150 lexicon entries (both files): needs_article='none', pluralizable=false — agreement metadata never exercised

================================================================================
ANALYST: spice
================================================================================
Corpus: 917 cards, 14 decks. Spice: 2=115 (12.5%), 3=488 (53.2%), 4=205 (22.4%), 5=109 (11.9%); no spice 1.

(1) CONFLATION. Resampled 60 cards (15/tier) onto Exposure (E) and Chaos (C). 36/60 (60%) cannot be encoded by one number (axis gap >=2, or spice tracks neither axis). Conflation grows with tier: spice2 3/15 (20%), spice3 8/15 (53%), spice4 12/15 (80%), spice5 13/15 (87%). Mechanism: spice currently encodes max(taboo-vocabulary, personalness), so opposite cards get equal numbers — taboo_timer_42 EDGING is spice 5 at E1/C4 (it's Pictionary; nobody discloses anything) while hot_seat_imposter_53 body count is spice 5 at E5/C2 (pure interrogation, zero comedy). Worst absurdities: title_fight_12 cats-vs-dogs = spice 4 at E1/C1; hot_seat_imposter_40 "hidden talent" and confession_or_cap_26 "pretended to know NFTs" = spice 4 icebreakers; alibi_drop_71 shovel/bleach/tarp = spice 5 at E1/C5 (perfect feral-impersonal, wrongly gated behind max-spice consent).

(2) CORPORATE ICEBREAKER BAND (E<=2-ish core, C<=2; would run unchanged at an HR team-building session): ~272/917 cards = 29.7% of the corpus violates WHOLESOME-never. Per deck: title_fight 36/43 (84% — toilet paper over/under, pineapple pizza, Batman/Superman), taboo_timer 37/50 (74% — Gen-Z vocab quiz: RIZZ, SLAY, NPC, ERA), over_under 34/62 (55% — alarms, browser tabs, streaming services), hot_seat_imposter 28/53 (53% — Starbucks order, karaoke song, dream vacation), roast_consensus 40/140 (29%), poison_pitch 28/85 (33% — damp socks tier), reality_check 17/45, scatterblast 16/53, the_unifying_theory 11/53, fill_in_finisher 10/80, confession_or_cap 7/80, alibi_drop 4/73, text_thread_trap 4/50, red_flag_rally 0/50 (SMASH/PASS mechanic immunizes, but ~14 have icebreaker cores). Spice inflation hides this: many band cards carry spice 3-4.

(3) DARK-NOT-FUNNY BAND (E>=4, C<=2, no benign frame — interrogation without a joke): 20 core + 7 borderline. Per deck: over_under 8 (ids 2, 13, 20, 42, 48, 60, 61, 62 — kiss count, ex-stalking, things broken in anger), hot_seat_imposter 4 (24 meanest thing said, 34 biggest regret, 52 worst secret act, 53 body count), reality_check 4 (14, 27 people who cried because of you, 31 trauma-responses, 44 attractiveness-gap), roast_consensus 2 (60, 78), fill_in_finisher 2 (42 partner's phone, 48 what ended last relationship). Borderline: reality_check_7/20, hot_seat_28, over_under_55, fill_in_43/52, text_thread_trap_36 (health-scare premise). These are therapy/interrogation prompts wearing a party-game costume.

(4) HARD-BAN VIOLATIONS (E>3 AND truthful answer names/implicates present third parties or can out orientation/fidelity/health) — 8 clear: over_under_60 (people in this room they'd hook up with — present parties + orientation), over_under_61 (lied about body count), roast_consensus_78 (hooked up with someone in this room), roast_consensus_60 (vote pins body-count lying on present player), reality_check_14 (people in this room talked behind backs about), hot_seat_imposter_53 (body count + lying, answered publicly about a present target), confession_or_cap_58 (was 'the other person' — fidelity confession), fill_in_finisher_42 (disturbing thing on partner's phone — implicates possibly-present partner, fidelity reveal). Borderline/fence: fill_in_finisher_43, _48, _52, reality_check_20, _27, hot_seat_imposter_52, plus orientation-outing vector at E3 in hot_seat_imposter_9 and scatterblast_20 (celebrity crushes "you'd never admit" force a closeted player to lie or out themselves).

(5) The corpus proves feral-but-impersonal already exists (alibi_drop_71, scatterblast_51, red_flag_rally_41 — all E1/C5) but the single spice number buries them at tier 5 behind the same consent gate as body-count interrogations, while cats-vs-dogs ships at spice 4. Decoupling fixes both failure modes at once: consent ceilings cap E only; C floor kills the wholesome band. Full rubric, calibration cards, and quotas in rebuild_rules.

KEY NUMBERS:
  - 917 cards across 14 decks; spice 2/3/4/5 = 115/488/205/109 (12.5%/53.2%/22.4%/11.9%); no spice 1
  - 60-card two-axis resample: 36/60 (60%) conflated; by tier: 20% (s2), 53% (s3), 80% (s4), 87% (s5)
  - Corporate icebreaker band (WHOLESOME violation): ~272/917 = 29.7% of corpus
  - Worst wholesome decks: title_fight 36/43 (84%), taboo_timer 37/50 (74%), over_under 34/62 (55%), hot_seat_imposter 28/53 (53%)
  - Dark-not-funny band (E>=4, C<=2, no benign frame): 20 core + 7 borderline; over_under worst at 8
  - Hard-ban violations: 8 clear IDs + 8 borderline/fence
  - Same spice 5, opposite axes: taboo_timer_42 (E1/C4) vs hot_seat_imposter_53 (E5/C2)
  - Spice-4 icebreakers proving conflation: title_fight_12 (E1/C1), hot_seat_imposter_40, confession_or_cap_26

================================================================================
TRIO3-V3 remaining majors:
================================================================================
[MAJOR] §4.1 Roast Consensus — FACE YOUR ACCUSERS, 'same voter→victim edge never printed twice a night': At N=3 only 6 voter→victim edges exist, but one Roast circle consumes up to 9 (3 prompts × 3 voters). By prompt 2 the rule forces predetermined votes if enforced as a ballot constraint (prompt 3 has ZERO legal votes — a hang/crash if implemented that way), or attribution silently vanishes if enforce
  FIX: Scope the edge dedup per-CIRCLE not per-night at N≤4, or cap Roast at 2 prompts at N=3, or attribute only the plurality edge. Explicitly specify display-suppression (never ballot constraint) so prompt

[MAJOR] §4.2 Fill-In Finisher N=3 derangement — 'authorship stays 50/50 ambiguous': The claim is mathematically false. With reader ≠ author guaranteed and 3 players, both derangements are 3-cycles: the moment a player hears their own line read, elimination resolves ALL authorship for everyone (I know mine; the third player can't be reading their own; therefore the one I read is the
  FIX: Drop the ambiguity claim and lean into attribution ('you're performing Sam's filth — sell it'), or collect votes BEFORE the reads (vote on text, then reads as victory laps).

================================================================================
ANALYST: structure
================================================================================
Corpus confirmed: 917 cards, 14 decks (73+80+80+53+62+85+45+50+140+53+50+50+53+43). Decks split cleanly into ROOM-ENGINES (card is a rig; friends produce the comedy; decay-proof) and JOKE-DELIVERY (punchline printed; CAH decay curve).

Generativity scores (fraction of comedy produced by the room), with 3 generative / 3 static examples per deck by card id:
- HOT_SEAT_IMPOSTER 10 — pure probe + real-friend ground truth. Gen: 25 (honest Tinder bio), 29 (drunk alter ego), 45 (autobiography title). Weak: 6, 11, 13 — trivia (karaoke/Starbucks/rewatch) with no reveal delta.
- TABOO_TIMER 10 — word+forbidden list, clue-giver improvises everything. Gen: 22 BODY COUNT, 5 SITUATIONSHIP, 42 EDGING. Weak: 44 SLAY, 47 ERA, 38 SIDE QUEST (guessable in 2s; slang will date).
- SCATTERBLAST 9.5 — category+letter+10s, all output player-made. Gen: 51 (serial killer's search history/H), 10 (reasons your ex is your ex/L), 22 (worst things at a funeral/C). Weak: 25 (DATA BUG: text letter S, field letter "C", truncated category), 46, 47 (dated meme).
- ALIBI_DROP 9 — accusation + 3 mandatory words; player improvises the story. Gen: 71 (shovel/bleach/tarp), 1 (ex's apartment 3am), 22 (Walmart 4am bathrobe). Static-leaning: word triads that ARE the punchline — 5 ("Potassium deficiency"), 24 ("It started it"), 53 ("Main character energy").
- FILL_IN_FINISHER 9 — setup+blank, punchline 100% player-written. Gen: 14 (AirDrop at family dinner), 66 (Step 1/Step 2/Prison), 55 (body-count Yelp review). Weak: 56 (exact dup of 20), 40 (anecdote-fetch, not construction), 3 (double blank, unanchored).
- OVER_UNDER 9 — neutral stat, comedy = friend's real number vs room's model. Gen: 60 (hookup thoughts in this room), 13 (ex-stalking this week), 19 (panic-deletes). Weak: 15, 34, 23 — inventory stats, zero self-exposure.
- THE_UNIFYING_THEORY 8.5 — player writes the theory. Gen: 3 (bathroom/first date/job interview), 24 (dorm/prison/NYC apartment), 41 (casino/dating app/vending machine). Static: 14, 17, 28 — connection pre-solved on the card (all fake promises / all gaslighting / all hollow validation).
- REALITY_CHECK 8 — self-rating vs room-rating gap. Gen: 14 (talked behind backs in this room), 44 (actual-vs-perceived attractiveness), 4 (sell out the group). Broken frame: 8, 33, 37 — counts jammed into a 1-10 scale (~8 cards).
- TITLE_FIGHT 7.5 — arguments are the comedy but prompts are stock. Gen: 43 (drunk you vs hungry you), 16 (mom's vs dad's search history), 42 (ex writes eulogy vs dating profile). Generic: 3 (duck-sized horses), 7 (pineapple pizza), 4 (Batman/Superman) — Reddit chestnuts.
- TEXT_THREAD_TRAP 6.5 — reply is improvised, but ~15/50 setups land their own punchline. Gen: 2 ("We found something in your old room. Call me."), 30 ("The walls are thinner than you think."), 3 (boss, 11pm). Static: 41 ("...oh. Nevermind. That's on brand for you."), 42 ("JK they don't know about you."), 50 (sassy GPS) — sender steals the joke.
- ROAST_CONSENSUS 6 — vote at a real friend is the payoff, but the scenario is an authored one-liner. Gen: 55, 78, 43 ("in this room" stakes). Static: 77 ("struck oil"), 109 ("meeting could have been an email"), 53 (Build-A-Bear — recycled from confession_1).
- POISON_PITCH 5 — both horns fully authored; room adds only the pick+defense. Debate-fuel: 22, 28, 41. Coin-flip novelty: 2 (maple syrup/glitter), 8 (goose honk), 79 — joke entirely printed.
- CONFESSION_OR_CAP 3.5 — prewritten anecdotes with finished punchlines; ~60/80 too specific for anyone to claim, which breaks the bluff mechanic. Claimable: 5, 18, 74. Auto-cap: 78 (DoorDash punchline), 42 ("They knew."), 44. Dup: 13≈51.
- RED_FLAG_RALLY 3 — perk+flag both printed; player votes. Best (engineered irony): 25, 38, 39. Broken: 2, 5, 13 — 19/50 cards carry a DIFFERENT joke in extra fields than in display text.

TEMPLATE VERDICT: no madlib slot system can ship. roast_taboo_1 ("Most likely to get caught {innuendo} at {context}"): 75/75 sexual_innuendo entries are bare infinitives → 100% ungrammatical renders ("get caught handle a big load... of laundry at a work happy hour"); 73/75 embed their own "…" punchline → double-punchline collision ("finger... paint with the kids" + random context is a liability, not a joke). 10/20 roast_consensus_enhanced templates are one "{plan} — because {evidence}" skeleton; random cross-products are non-sequiturs — the funny in the corpus's best cards (red_flag 25/38/39) is the engineered dependency between halves, exactly what sampling can't make. needs_article="none" on 150/150 lexicon entries — grammar machinery never used. ~15/75 red_flag_traits are literal abuse descriptions (stalking, blackmail, isolation), unusable as comedy. The one working "template" file (fill_in_finisher.json) isn't slot-filling: its 10 blueprints are hand-written prompts, 5 duplicating corpus cards. Rebuild: 100% hand-written corpus + {NAME} insertion only (proper nouns are the only grammatically/semantically inert substitution), plus authored per-deck structured fields.

KEY NUMBERS:
  - 917 cards / 14 decks confirmed (73+80+80+53+62+85+45+50+140+53+50+50+53+43)
  - Generativity: hot_seat_imposter 10, taboo_timer 10, scatterblast 9.5, alibi_drop 9, fill_in_finisher 9, over_under 9, unifying_theory 8.5, reality_check 8, title_fight 7.5, text_thread_trap 6.5, roast_consensus 6, poison_pitch 5, confession_or_cap 3.5, red_flag_rally 3
  - 75/75 sexual_innuendo entries grammar-break the 'get caught ___' template frame (bare infinitive vs required gerund)
  - 73/75 sexual_innuendo entries carry an embedded '...' punchline → double-punchline collision with any template context
  - 19/50 red_flag_rally cards: display text and extra-field perk/red_flag are two different jokes
  - 10/20 roast_consensus_enhanced templates are the same '{plan} because {evidence}' skeleton reworded
  - 5/10 fill_in_finisher.json blueprints duplicate hand-written corpus cards verbatim
  - 150/150 lexicon entries (both files): needs_article='none', pluralizable=false — agreement metadata never exercised

================================================================================
ANALYST: spice
================================================================================
Corpus: 917 cards, 14 decks. Spice: 2=115 (12.5%), 3=488 (53.2%), 4=205 (22.4%), 5=109 (11.9%); no spice 1.

(1) CONFLATION. Resampled 60 cards (15/tier) onto Exposure (E) and Chaos (C). 36/60 (60%) cannot be encoded by one number (axis gap >=2, or spice tracks neither axis). Conflation grows with tier: spice2 3/15 (20%), spice3 8/15 (53%), spice4 12/15 (80%), spice5 13/15 (87%). Mechanism: spice currently encodes max(taboo-vocabulary, personalness), so opposite cards get equal numbers — taboo_timer_42 EDGING is spice 5 at E1/C4 (it's Pictionary; nobody discloses anything) while hot_seat_imposter_53 body count is spice 5 at E5/C2 (pure interrogation, zero comedy). Worst absurdities: title_fight_12 cats-vs-dogs = spice 4 at E1/C1; hot_seat_imposter_40 "hidden talent" and confession_or_cap_26 "pretended to know NFTs" = spice 4 icebreakers; alibi_drop_71 shovel/bleach/tarp = spice 5 at E1/C5 (perfect feral-impersonal, wrongly gated behind max-spice consent).

(2) CORPORATE ICEBREAKER BAND (E<=2-ish core, C<=2; would run unchanged at an HR team-building session): ~272/917 cards = 29.7% of the corpus violates WHOLESOME-never. Per deck: title_fight 36/43 (84% — toilet paper over/under, pineapple pizza, Batman/Superman), taboo_timer 37/50 (74% — Gen-Z vocab quiz: RIZZ, SLAY, NPC, ERA), over_under 34/62 (55% — alarms, browser tabs, streaming services), hot_seat_imposter 28/53 (53% — Starbucks order, karaoke song, dream vacation), roast_consensus 40/140 (29%), poison_pitch 28/85 (33% — damp socks tier), reality_check 17/45, scatterblast 16/53, the_unifying_theory 11/53, fill_in_finisher 10/80, confession_or_cap 7/80, alibi_drop 4/73, text_thread_trap 4/50, red_flag_rally 0/50 (SMASH/PASS mechanic immunizes, but ~14 have icebreaker cores). Spice inflation hides this: many band cards carry spice 3-4.

(3) DARK-NOT-FUNNY BAND (E>=4, C<=2, no benign frame — interrogation without a joke): 20 core + 7 borderline. Per deck: over_under 8 (ids 2, 13, 20, 42, 48, 60, 61, 62 — kiss count, ex-stalking, things broken in anger), hot_seat_imposter 4 (24 meanest thing said, 34 biggest regret, 52 worst secret act, 53 body count), reality_check 4 (14, 27 people who cried because of you, 31 trauma-responses, 44 attractiveness-gap), roast_consensus 2 (60, 78), fill_in_finisher 2 (42 partner's phone, 48 what ended last relationship). Borderline: reality_check_7/20, hot_seat_28, over_under_55, fill_in_43/52, text_thread_trap_36 (health-scare premise). These are therapy/interrogation prompts wearing a party-game costume.

(4) HARD-BAN VIOLATIONS (E>3 AND truthful answer names/implicates present third parties or can out orientation/fidelity/health) — 8 clear: over_under_60 (people in this room they'd hook up with — present parties + orientation), over_under_61 (lied about body count), roast_consensus_78 (hooked up with someone in this room), roast_consensus_60 (vote pins body-count lying on present player), reality_check_14 (people in this room talked behind backs about), hot_seat_imposter_53 (body count + lying, answered publicly about a present target), confession_or_cap_58 (was 'the other person' — fidelity confession), fill_in_finisher_42 (disturbing thing on partner's phone — implicates possibly-present partner, fidelity reveal). Borderline/fence: fill_in_finisher_43, _48, _52, reality_check_20, _27, hot_seat_imposter_52, plus orientation-outing vector at E3 in hot_seat_imposter_9 and scatterblast_20 (celebrity crushes "you'd never admit" force a closeted player to lie or out themselves).

(5) The corpus proves feral-but-impersonal already exists (alibi_drop_71, scatterblast_51, red_flag_rally_41 — all E1/C5) but the single spice number buries them at tier 5 behind the same consent gate as body-count interrogations, while cats-vs-dogs ships at spice 4. Decoupling fixes both failure modes at once: consent ceilings cap E only; C floor kills the wholesome band. Full rubric, calibration cards, and quotas in rebuild_rules.

KEY NUMBERS:
  - 917 cards across 14 decks; spice 2/3/4/5 = 115/488/205/109 (12.5%/53.2%/22.4%/11.9%); no spice 1
  - 60-card two-axis resample: 36/60 (60%) conflated; by tier: 20% (s2), 53% (s3), 80% (s4), 87% (s5)
  - Corporate icebreaker band (WHOLESOME violation): ~272/917 = 29.7% of corpus
  - Worst wholesome decks: title_fight 36/43 (84%), taboo_timer 37/50 (74%), over_under 34/62 (55%), hot_seat_imposter 28/53 (53%)
  - Dark-not-funny band (E>=4, C<=2, no benign frame): 20 core + 7 borderline; over_under worst at 8
  - Hard-ban violations: 8 clear IDs + 8 borderline/fence
  - Same spice 5, opposite axes: taboo_timer_42 (E1/C4) vs hot_seat_imposter_53 (E5/C2)
  - Spice-4 icebreakers proving conflation: title_fight_12 (E1/C1), hot_seat_imposter_40, confession_or_cap_26

By prompt 2 the rule forces predetermined votes if enforced as a ballot constraint (prompt 3 has ZERO legal votes — a hang/crash if implemented that way), or attribution silently vanishes if enforce
  FIX: Scope the edge dedup per-CIRCLE not per-night at N≤4, or cap Roast at 2 prompts at N=3, or attribute only the plurality edge. Explicitly specify display-suppression (never ballot constraint) so prompt

With reader ≠ author guaranteed and 3 players, both derangements are 3-cycles: the moment a player hears their own line read, elimination resolves ALL authorship for everyone (I know mine; the third player can't be reading their own; therefore the one I read is the
  FIX: Drop the ambiguity claim and lean into attribution ('you're performing Sam's filth — sell it'), or collect votes BEFORE the reads (vote on text, then reads as victory laps).

lines: ### 1. Roast Consensus 🎯 (minN 3 · launch · 150+ cards)
Three prompts per circle. Everyone secretly votes which PRESENT player fits ("Who would…"). Synced reveal: victim's name in lights; reveal holds for the roast.
- Plurality tie → **"THE ROOM IS TORN — DOUBLE ROAST"** (both names in lights; either vote scores).
-- N=3–4 → attributed mode **"FACE YOUR ACCUSERS"** (votes shown with names — the negotiation is the comedy). Capped at exposure ≤3; the same voter→victim edge never prints twice a night.

- Cards must make the vote contested (2–3 plausible victims in any group) and hand the room roast ammunition.
### 2. Fill-In Finisher ✍️ (minN 3 · launch · 150+ cards)

lines: ### 2. Fill-In Finisher ✍️ (minN 3 · launch · 150+ cards)

A setup with a blank. Everyone but the Reader writes a punchline (panic button = 2 curated fallbacks at half points). Answers go ONLY to the Reader's teleprompter. **Perform, then vote:** while others write, the Reader picks the read-tone ("read these as: a eulogy / a 911 call / a hostage tape"); then performs every answer aloud — first exposure is ALWAYS out loud to the room. Vote grid (answer texts, randomized order) unlocks after the last read; no self-votes; winner + author reveal.
- N≥7: **two Readers**, one per setup, alternating. Per setup: ≤4 answers in one run → one vote picks the top → single cross-setup grand face-off.
-- N=3: no Reader — reads assigned by random derangement (never your own; authorship stays ambiguous); all three vote among the other two.

+- N=3: no Reader — reads assigned by random derangement (never your own). Authorship is deducible at N=3 (both derangements are 3-cycles), so the game leans INTO attribution: the read prompt says "you're performing Sam's filth — sell it," and the vote is on the text, not the mystery.
### 3. Over/Under 📉 (minN 3 · launch · 150+ cards)

Two subjects per circle. A stat about the subject appears; the room debates the line OUT LOUD (that argument is the game); everyone secretly bets OVER/UNDER; the subject fetches the truth (phone receipts preferred: screen time, camera roll count, unread emails). Receipt fetch is a BLOCKING input (pause + shame countdown + terminals). Synced reveal: gasp or vindication.

content.content:
### alibi_drop (2 keeps)
  REQ: Words are contraband, not answers: each of the 3 words must be a 1-2 word common noun/concept that could plausibly surface in ANY 30-second story but is orthogonal to THIS accusation. Hard test per word: if it could be the accused's actual explanation ('lost k
  REQ: Lineup-compatible or dead: every triplet entry must sit indistinguishably in an 8-word lineup beside 5 decoys. No clauses ('It started it'), no 3+ word bits ('a nap that went too far'), no numbers-as-quirk ('409 bouncy balls'), no phrases that can only be reci
  REQ: Accusation formula: absurd-but-answerable, one breath (≤14 words), built on forensic evidence with one specific detail (a time, an object, a witness — '3am', 'your trunk', 'the FBI'). The accused must be able to spin 3+ different alibis; if the premise already
  REQ: Spice caps EXPOSURE, never chaos: spice 1-2 = impersonal feral absurdity (crimes against mannequins), 3-4 = the accused's habits and relationships as evidence, 5 = real-ex / boss / 'in this room' territory with the fictional-accusation frame doing the safety w
  REQ: Break the skeletons: max 2 cards per opener pattern per 20-card run ('What happened at…', 'Why were you banned…', 'Why were you seen…'). Vary the interrogator's voice across the deck — cop, ex reading texts aloud, HOA letter, TSA agent, the group chat — so the
  REQ: Zero shelf-life vocabulary: no brands-as-punchlines (LinkedIn, John Wick, Wendy's, Chuck E. Cheese), no 2016-2022 Twitter phrases (main character energy, social experiment, emotional support X, mercury retrograde, aux cord), no memorized bits (shovel/bleach/ta
  REQ: No word repeats deck-wide, and ban universal-solvent alibi words entirely ('a dare' ×6, 'a misunderstanding', 'lost', karaoke ×5, coupons ×3 in the current corpus). Every repeated word teaches the jury the deck instead of the player.
  REQ: Add a room-leverage lane: ~20% of accusations implicate shared group history or recruit the jury as witnesses ('the camping trip WE don't speak of', 'Why does {NAME} flinch when you mention the lake house?') — accusation from inside the room, alibi performed t

### confession_or_cap (19 keeps)
  REQ: PLAUSIBILITY WINDOW TEST (the core lie-worthiness gate): before a card ships, imagine six friends voting on a stranger telling it — if you cannot argue BOTH believe and cap, kill it. Ban universal behaviors (everyone's done it = auto-believe: #20, #60) and car
  REQ: SETUP, NEVER PUNCHLINE: the card must end where the story begins. No quoted one-liners ('inappropriate stuffing behavior'), no twist endings (DoorDash), no trailing tags ('They knew.' 'I was 23.' 'It was not happy tears.'). Test: if the card gets a laugh read 
  REQ: ONE ANCHOR + TWO OPEN SLOTS per card: exactly one concrete interrogable detail (place, object, number, artifact) the jury can cross-examine, plus at least two unfilled slots (who / where / what happened next) the confessor must supply. Clone the keeper fingerp
  REQ: SPICE = EXPOSURE, NEVER EXPLICITNESS: 3 = feral-but-impersonal public chaos; 4 = social/romantic self-owns; 5 = high-exposure personal (shame, desire, betrayal you were party to — #58, #79). A hickey excuse is not a 5 (#50); a mental-breakdown one-way flight i
  REQ: SKELETON BUDGET: maximum 2 cards per template verb across the whole 150 ('I cried in [place]', 'I ate an entire [food]', 'I pretended [X] to avoid [Y]', 'I blamed [scapegoat]', fake-phone-call). Run a dedupe pass on verb+shape before ship; a 150-card deck need
  REQ: ZERO LOAD-BEARING BRANDS OR MEMES: no card where the joke dies with the app or the news cycle (NFTs, Crumbl, LinkedIn-as-punchline, Venmo-request-bit, finsta, situationship discourse). Brands may appear only as neutral setting (a Costco can host a story; 'cryi
  REQ: POV-UNIVERSAL AND UNCHECKABLE: every card must be lockable TRUE by anyone at the table regardless of gender, orientation, or relationship status ('told a date', never 'told a girl' — see #69). And phrase claims as past events, never present possessions — 'save
  REQ: SANE LOGIC OR IT'S AUTO-CAP: the premise must survive five seconds of jury scrutiny. 'Told the locksmith I was breaking into my ex's place so he'd hurry' (#61) and 'pretended to SPEAK Spanish to dodge jury duty' (#63) fall apart on contact, which forces a cap 

### fill_in_finisher (15 keeps)
  REQ: Skeleton quota: no sentence template ('The worst ___', 'The most ___ I've...') may exceed 6 cards per 80 (~8%). This deck spent 41% on two skeletons. Require 10+ distinct structures: reverse-punchline (Step 3: Prison), fixed-tag beats ('...and honestly, fair')
  REQ: Performance-first: at least 40% of cards must force the Reader's mouth — speech acts (whisper, moan, eulogize, voicemail, intercom, last words, toast) whose comedy only exists ALOUD. QA test: read 3 candidate answers in a read-tone; if it's equally funny silen
  REQ: Hard ban list, enforced by grep before gold status: therapist, LinkedIn, Notes app, search history, screen time, FBI agent, intrusive thoughts, villain origin story, sleep-paralysis demon, Venmo, body count, 'the ick', Yelp-review-of-X, Shark Tank — plus the S
  REQ: Write-verb only: prompts must invite INVENTED punchlines, never true-history recall. 'The most ___ I've done/seen/found' is banned as a fill-in setup — recall prompts punish players without a story and pull toward flat truth. Real-memory territory gets a ficti
  REQ: Spice = EXPOSURE only, never chaos: impersonal/hypothetical filth (worst thing to moan) caps at spice 3; spice 4-5 is reserved for self-implication. Any card whose truthful answer implicates another present person (partner's phone, what ended my relationship, 
  REQ: Room leverage: at least 25% of the deck carries {NAME} slots or answer-about-the-room hooks (this deck has ZERO in 80). Votes should sometimes hurt — a punchline aimed at the person across the table is worth three aimed at a hypothetical stranger.
  REQ: Every setup must SEED at least one concrete comic element — a place, an object, a relationship, a fixed beat (family dinner + AirDrop; hug + whisper; Step 3: Prison). Pure mad-lib frames ('The worst part about ___ is ___', 'I thought ___ was a good idea until 
  REQ: QA gates before 'gold': (1) 3-answers test — three DIFFERENT funny answers in 10 seconds or kill; (2) exact + near-duplicate scan (this deck shipped a verbatim dupe and five near-dupe pairs); (3) one human reads the entire deck end to end, aloud, in order.

### hot_seat_imposter (5 keeps)
  REQ: Observation Test (gate every card): at least two people in the room must plausibly hold evidence — witnessed it, received the text, has seen drunk-them. Ban the constructions 'nobody knows,' 'secretly,' 'when nobody's watching,' 'hidden': they select for exact
  REQ: Resolvable ground truth: the silent target must be able to confirm/deny in under 10 seconds with a story or an on-the-spot phone receipt. Ban unverifiable hypotheticals (lottery plans, baby names, autobiography titles, dream vacations) — no ground truth means 
  REQ: Performance over trivia: the answer must be a story, impression, or recreation — never a single token (emoji, song title, number, coffee order). Rule of thumb: if the REAL answer is one word, the round is a memory quiz, not improv under pressure.
  REQ: Spice = EXPOSURE of the target only, never explicitness and never third parties: 1-2 feral-but-impersonal, 3 embarrassing-and-witnessed, 4 reputation-denting self-material, 5 self-incriminating-but-benign. Hard ban above spice 3 on anything routing through fid
  REQ: Break the template: max 40% of cards may use the 'What's their [superlative] ___?' skeleton (currently 50/53). Require structural variety — impressions ('Answer as drunk-them'), receipts challenges ('The target will show the screen after the vote'), scenario f
  REQ: Max 2 phone-contents cards per deck (current deck has 5: Googled / lock screen / camera roll / Notes app / search history) — keep the receipts mechanic, kill the redundancy.
  REQ: Voice pass on every card: kill icebreaker vocabulary ('go-to,' 'favorite,' 'dream,' 'comfort,' 'guilty pleasure') and worn Twitter-meme framing ('toxic trait,' 'lowkey,' 'unhinged Notes app'). Double swap test: the card must break if dropped into a corporate i
  REQ: Design for the lie, not the confession: the best rounds happen when the imposter's FAKE answer is funnier and more plausible than the truth. Write questions where a wrong-but-believable answer roasts the target — the game is the room prosecuting what it has wi

### over_under (12 keeps)
  REQ: Receipt-first quota: at least 70% of cards must resolve via a number the subject's phone can display in under 10 seconds (screen time, unread badge, Screenshots/People album counts, blocked list, order history, call log). Name the receipt surface in the card's
  REQ: Non-receipt counts must be chaos/pride-valenced (a high number is legend status: things broken in anger, times returned to an ex) and bounded to memorable events — never shame-tallies of invisible habits, which subjects sandbag until unders always win and the
