  REQ: PERFORMABILITY BONUS: prefer options a pitcher can act out mid-pitch — voices, sounds, demonstrations, mock readings (the nature-documentary narrator is the model). The pitch is the product; the card is the stage.
  REQ: VOICE PASS: every card must read aloud in one breath in the dive-bar-at-2am register — a friend proposing something horrible, not a listicle headline. If it starts 'Would you rather give up X for a year,' it fails the register test; rewrite until it sounds lik

### reality_check (8 keeps)
  REQ: Two-gate test before writing any card: (a) Evidence gate — name the receipts the room plausibly holds (witnessed behavior, received texts, shared history); (b) Ego gate — name why the subject's self-image is invested in the number. Fail either gate, don't writ
  REQ: Ban the 'How many times you've...' skeleton (16/45 of this deck): counts of private facts have no perception gap and nothing to debate. Sole exception: a count that measures self-awareness of damage the room may have witnessed (reality_check_27's '...that you 
  REQ: Numeric 1-10 only, direction locked (10 = maximum of the named trait). No verbal scales ('zen monk' to 'needs professional help') — they cannot sync at the ego-gap reveal.
  REQ: Mine Dunning-Kruger domains — traits people systematically misrate about themselves: driving, humor, loyalty under pressure, temper, drunk behavior, listening, attractiveness, being over an ex, self-awareness itself. That is where the reveal pays; if nobody's 
  REQ: Seed live-demo dimensions where the debate performs the test in real time (eye contact, interrupting, defensiveness, how well you take a joke) — reality_check_42 proves the pattern. These are mechanic-native and un-stealable: DISTINCTIVE tier by construction.
  REQ: Spice must cap EXPOSURE only: spice 1-2 = feral-but-impersonal competence (survive-a-bar-fight energy, deranged hypotheticals), spice 4-5 = most personal self-image (attractiveness, love life, loyalty). If a spice-2 card could open a corporate offsite, it is d
  REQ: Third-party firewall: above spice 3, the dimension may expose only the subject's self-image — never a dimension whose honest debate names or implicates other people present (kill the 'talked behind whose back' class). Let the room volunteer their own receipts;
  REQ: Specificity and voice pass on every card: any unmodified 2019-2024 Twitter staple (screen time, red flags, therapy-speak, Uber ratings, going viral) fails the Specificity Test. Rewrite in second person, concrete nouns, dive-bar-at-2am register, speakable in on

### red_flag_rally (3 keeps)
  REQ: Fix the pipeline before writing a single card: 18/50 cards render a different joke than their perk/red_flag fields contain. One card = one joke = one source of truth; add a validation step that diffs display text against fields and fails the build on mismatch.
  REQ: Perk and flag must live on ORTHOGONAL axes: perk is a real temptation (money, sex, status, competence — would a stranger at the table actually want this?), flag is an independent disqualifier. Ban perk-negation reveals ('owns a boat... in a Walmart parking lot
  REQ: Every card must pass the SPLIT-ROOM test before shipping: write down the defender's actual argument AND the counter-heckle the room will shout. If either side is silent — unanimous SMASH (quirky flag) or unanimous PASS (unsurvivable flag) — the card is dead. F
  REQ: Rebuild the spice scale to rate EXPOSURE, not weirdness: current deck is zero-exposure at every rating. New scale: 1-2 = pure absurd hypothetical (earwax bust); 3 = flag implicates the defender's real taste ('and honestly, this is your type'); 4-5 = flag drags
  REQ: Ban the 2019-2023 Twitter lexicon outright: Funko, Venmo-request, SoundCloud, vanlife, chemtrails, 'for their podcast', TikTok dances, follower counts, life coaches, grindset 5am routines. Kill-test: if the flag could appear in a BuzzFeed 'biggest red flags' l
  REQ: Break the single skeleton: this deck is one template x50 and players will clock it by round five. The rebuild needs at least four structures rotating: orthogonal perk/flag; trait-weaponized-against-you (45); discovery framing ('you find out on date three that.
  REQ: State evidence flat; never editorialize. Ban 'It's unsettling', 'Every. Single. Day.' and all tone-instruction beats — the card presents the shrine, the binder, the tub of cologne; the room supplies the horror. Concrete props and scenes over trait adjectives (
  REQ: Quality floor per the taste hierarchy: minimum CONTEXTUAL — every card must need HELLDECK's SMASH/PASS defense mechanic to work. If the card is funny read aloud with no defender and no vote, it's a tweet, not a card; cut it no matter how good the joke is.

### roast_consensus (26 keeps)
  REQ: Two-victim test, both directions: before a card ships, name the 2-3 friend-group archetypes who'd split the vote (the flake vs the romantic vs the menace). Universal behaviors (rehearsing calls, lying on resumes — everyone qualifies) and single-archetype behav
  REQ: Film a scene, never deliver a verdict: ban 'unhinged, chaotic, toxic, main character, NPC, gives off X energy, most likely to be [adjective]' from card text. Write the act with a place, time, object, or number (40 cans of beans, 2am, Build-A-Bear, below 4.7) a
  REQ: Spice = exposure, never explicitness: spice 1-2 is feral-but-impersonal (negotiating with parking meters, funeral logistics), spice 4-5 is real personal history behind a deniability frame ('would,' euphemism, hypothetical). Re-audit every rating against 'how p
  REQ: Vocabulary expiry audit: no meme nouns with a shelf date — OnlyFans, feet pics, mugshots, LinkedIn, situationship, talking stage, red flag, manifesting, 'you up?', therapy-speak. If the card quotes 2021 Twitter, it dies with 2021 Twitter. Timely references (AI
  REQ: The euphemism engine is the house style — use it to open loops, not close them: the self-incriminating reframe ('...and call it research,' 'celebrating life,' 'sorry, my phone died') is the deck's best recurring move because it hands the table a defense to dem
  REQ: Max 2 cards per skeleton across all 150: banned-from-X, cries-at-X, gives-off-energy, fake-own-death, would-die-first-in-scenario, argues-with-machines, gullible-joins-thing. This deck had 6 crying, 6 energy, 5 leaker, 8 gullible cards — players spot the templ
  REQ: Build 20+ table-aware cards — they're the only structurally un-copyable ones: 'sell out everyone in this room,' 'vague-post about someone clearly in the room,' 'blackmail material on everyone here.' Cards that reference the present group cannot exist in any ot
  REQ: Reserve 'Who has' for story-mining, 'Who would' for darkness: past-tense accusation cards ('texted the wrong person something devastating') are gold because the reveal demands a real story — aim for ~30% of the deck, kept at spice ≤3 where confession is fun. A

### scatterblast (9 keeps)
  REQ: Cards are CATEGORY + LETTER only — delete the 'Say 3 in 10s' line everywhere; the bomb engine owns timing. Every pair must pass a letter audit before shipping: the writer lists 10 valid answers aloud; fewer than 8 = change the letter; if answers share a stem w
  REQ: Build around the accidental-confession engine: at least half the categories in second person ('your mom found...', 'you'd never do sober...') so desperate blurts read as self-exposure with speed as deniability. That is the move that makes a category HELLDECK-o
  REQ: Spice caps EXPOSURE only: spice 1-2 must be feral-but-impersonal (funeral quotes, cursed objects, unhinged hypotheticals) — never icebreakers; spice 4-5 must raise personal stakes (your real ex, your actual hidden stuff), never just gore/edge flavor. Icebreake
  REQ: Apply the desperation-arc test to every category: imagine the 8th answer in the circle — if it's funnier than the 1st (escalation into absurd/incriminating), pass; if answers get more boring as they deplete (descriptive categories like 'things on a reality sho
  REQ: Skeleton quota: max 25% of cards may open with 'Things...'; rotate frames (Worst X to say at Y, Reasons your..., Excuses for..., quote categories, invent-a-name categories). No two cards may share a topic cluster (this deck had 2 therapist cards, 2 'peaks in' 
  REQ: Ban the Twitter-prompt commons outright: red flags, the ick, swipe left, 3am Googling, therapist jokes, serial-killer search history, millennials killed, 'feels illegal' — if the category is findable as a viral prompt format, it fails the Specificity Test by d
  REQ: Speakability: category 7 words max, shoutable in one breath, letter rendered huge — the reveal must land in under 2 seconds because the fuse is already burning.
  REQ: Add a pipeline QA gate: display letter must equal data letter and strings must be untruncated (card 25 shipped broken into a deck labeled 'gold').

### taboo_timer (8 keeps)
  REQ: List-writing protocol (the BODY COUNT test): before a card is done, speak aloud the three clues a drunk clue-giver would actually give; every load-bearing word in those sentences goes on the forbidden list. If the verbatim dictionary definition of the word can
  REQ: No literal-compound giveaways: reject any target word whose literal decomposition or mundane second meaning hands guessers a 2-second route (CATFISH, SUGAR DADDY, RENT FREE, SERVING) — UNLESS the double meaning is the comedy engine and the innocent reading is 
  REQ: Pop-culture route audit: if a song, movie, show, or celebrity shares the word's name (Britney's Toxic, The Hangover, Eminem's Stan, Zombieland's Double Tap, FBoy Island), the title/artist goes on the forbidden list or the word is cut. Guessers reach for pop cu
  REQ: Aliveness gate: every word must be in active spoken use by the target players in 2026 AND projected to survive 24 more months. No single-trend memes (ROMAN EMPIRE, BEIGE FLAG, VIBE CHECK) — evergreen-feral beats trend-chasing. Max 20% of the deck may depend on
  REQ: Spice = exposure of the clue-giver, never raunch of the noun: rate by how incriminating it is to describe the word FLUENTLY in front of friends (fluency is the confession). Low-spice slots must be feral-but-impersonal (BRAINROT tier), never icebreaker tier (GR
  REQ: Thematic spread cap: max 20% of the deck per cluster (dating, drag, gaming, drinking, money, crime, bodily). The current deck is 34% dating discourse with three flag-siblings and a ghosting/breadcrumbing/benching triplet — players spot the template and the dec
  REQ: Voice: zero self-censorship (FUCKBOY or nothing) and reserve slots for words only HELLDECK would print — its own identity vocabulary (the UNHINGED card is the model), dive-bar words, and words whose optimal clue is a performed bit or an accidental confession r
  REQ: Design the Buzzer contract into the lists: assume exact-match on exactly the five listed words, so choose the five to cover stems and inflections ('Reply' catches nothing if the list says 'Text'; 'Offline' does not catch 'online'), and the target word's own pa

### text_thread_trap (12 keeps)
  REQ: THE LEFT-ON-READ TEST: every text must end in an implicit or explicit demand (question, ultimatum, RSVP, accusation awaiting denial). If the performer could plausibly not reply, the card fails. Then apply NO_3_ANSWERS to the reply space: three different funny 
  REQ: PUNCHLINE AMPUTATION: the sender may escalate, threaten, or dangle evidence, but never joke. If the funniest line on the card belongs to the sender ('bring a box', 'invest in a rug', 'on brand for you'), cut it and leave the blank open — the performer plus the
  REQ: OPEN-BLANK RULE: the incriminating thing (what was found/heard/caught/ordered) must never be specified on the card. 'We found something in your old room' works because the performer names or dances around the shame; specifying it converts a trap into a prewrit
  REQ: SKELETON BUDGET: max 4 cards per sender archetype (ex, parent, boss/HR, match, stranger, neighbor/roommate) and max 5 per trap shape (vague summons, evidence reveal, gossip triangulation, DTR, wrong-recipient). The current deck has 15 ex-adjacent cards and 34/
  REQ: SPICE = EXPOSURE, RECALIBRATE ALL 50: spice rates only how personally implicating a truthful-feeling reply is. Wrong-person chaos texts (49, 28) are spice 1-2 no matter how deranged; proximity cards ('your best friend', 'your crush') are 4-5. Never spend spice
  REQ: ADD A {NAME} LANE: zero of 50 cards touch the room. Build a variant tier where the sender or subject is a player at the table ('Your ex texted: {NAME} told me everything', 'Group chat: has anyone else noticed what {NAME} did?'). Hard ban: {NAME} may never appe
  REQ: TOKEN BAN LIST for this deck: OnlyFans, therapist memes, Netflix password/still-watching, 'I know what you did last summer', quirky-apps-that-text-you, embarrassing-Amazon-order. Anything a 2021 Twitter account would post stamps an expiry date on the box.
  REQ: PERFORMANCE SET-PIECE QUOTA: commission 8-10 cards where the reply IS a bit — write the reference letter for your ex's new relationship (card 45, the purest mechanic fit in the deck), compose the apology to the neighbors, draft the RSVP to your ex's family Tha

### title_fight (1 keeps)
  REQ: Every card must name what the fighters DO for 30 seconds and what the crowd votes on — not just a topic. Default verbal format: each corner gets 15 seconds to make the case, room points at the winner. If a card can't state its verb, it doesn't ship.
  REQ: Minimum one-third of the deck must be physical or performative duels executable in a living room (straight-face duel while the room heckles, slow-motion fight reenactment judged on drama, hype-man-off, impression battle, one-breath rant-off). The current deck 
  REQ: Both corners must be defensible or the card is dead. Kill test before shipping: write one strong argument for each side in 10 seconds; if you can't, or if 80% of any room picks a side before the bell, cut it (see title_fight_8).
  REQ: Build on {NAME} and self-testimony leverage: the strongest shape found in this corpus is 'two fighters mine their own real material as evidence, room-as-witness judges' (42, 43). Hypothetical-internet-person debates are banned; the fight must be about, or foug
  REQ: Spice = EXPOSURE only, never edginess-by-vibes: 1-2 feral-but-impersonal chaos, 3 volunteered self-testimony (your own shame), 4-5 room-history material the crowd has witnessed — with the third-party-harm ban intact above 3. Re-audit every number; cats-vs-dogs
  REQ: Hard ban list from this corpus: hot-dog/cereal taxonomy debates, duck-horse combat math, toddlers-vs-gorilla, pineapple pizza, toilet paper orientation, fly-vs-invisible and all superpower WYRs, cats-vs-dogs, Batman-vs-Superman, Vine nostalgia, brand-vs-brand 
  REQ: Break the skeleton: no more than 20% of the deck may share a sentence template. Mix at least three formats — title-belt fights ('Fight for the belt: ___ of this room'), testimony duels ('cite your worst ___; room votes'), and physical/performance bouts — so pl
  REQ: Freshness gate: if the debate existed on Reddit or in a listicle before 2020, it does not exist in HELLDECK. Seed inherently funny, concrete words (eulogy, silverback, thirst trap) instead of abstract trade-offs (memory vs luck, early vs late).

aiTitle: Redesign card game for fun multiplayer gameplay

content.text: Now writing the two repo deliverables: the autopsy verdict and the content bible (the binding law for the rebuild).

input.content:
# CARD_AUTOPSY.md — Why the cards aren't fun

_Full-corpus pathology of all 917 gold cards (13 of 14 decks card-by-card; Unifying Theory excluded — cut from the game). Conducted by 13 per-deck pathologists + 3 cross-corpus analysts against the new mechanics in HDRealRules2.md. Companion doc: CONTENT_BIBLE.md (the rebuild laws)._

## The verdict

**Of 864 cards audited: 449 KILL (52%) · 292 REWRITE (34%) · 123 KEEP (14%).**

| Deck | Kill | Rewrite | Keep | Keep % | Dominant failure |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Roast Consensus | 48 | 66 | 26 | 19% | GENERIC_INTERNET (53) |
| Confession or Cap | 37 | 24 | 19 | 24% | PREWRITTEN_JOKE (20) |
| Fill-In Finisher | 43 | 22 | 15 | 19% | ZERO ROOM LEVERAGE (80/80) |
| Over/Under | 34 | 16 | 12 | 19% | UNVERIFIABLE_REVEAL (26) |
| Text Thread Trap | 20 | 18 | 12 | 24% | NO ROOM LEVERAGE (50/50) |
| Scatterblast | 21 | 23 | 9 | 17% | MECHANIC_MISMATCH (53) |
| Reality Check | 26 | 11 | 8 | 18% | UNDEBATABLE_PRIVATE_FACT (16) |
| Taboo Timer | 29 | 13 | 8 | 16% | Forbidden lists don't block easy routes (23) |
| Hot Seat Imposter | 36 | 12 | 5 | 9% | WHOLESOME_CORPORATE (18) |
| Poison Pitch | 52 | 30 | 3 | 4% | DUPLICATE_SHAPE (34) |
| Red Flag Rally | 22 | 25 | 3 | 6% | DUPLICATE_SHAPE (50), broken data (19/50) |
| Alibi Drop | 44 | 27 | 2 | 3% | ANSWER_KEY_WORDS (62) |
| Title Fight | 37 | 5 | 1 | 2% | Recycled Reddit canon (16) |

## The five root causes

**1. The cards were written for scrolling, not for a table.**
~84% of the corpus is one voice: relatable millennial-Twitter observational humor — recognition ("haha so true"), not comedy. 38% of ALL cards name a phone/app/online/dating/therapy artifact. Recognition humor works silently on a feed; a party game needs cards that make the ROOM do something. The proof: Fill-In Finisher and Text Thread Trap have **zero** cards (0/130) that reference the people playing.

**2. The card tells the joke, so the players can't.**
The decks with the lowest player-generativity scores are the worst decks (Red Flag Rally 3/10, Confession or Cap 3.5/10, Poison Pitch 5/10): perk+flag both printed, confession punchlines pre-written ("...It was a promotional text from DoorDash"), both would-you-rather horns fully authored. Jackbox's law — prompts set PLAYERS up to be funny — is inverted; players are a laugh track. This is also why the deck decays like CAH: prewritten jokes are memorized by the second night.

**3. One number ("spice") priced two different things, and both mispriced.**
60% of sampled cards can't be encoded by the single spice number because EXPOSURE (how personal) and CHAOS (how deranged) are different axes — conflation hits 87% at spice 5. Consequences: **29.7% of the corpus (272 cards) is corporate-icebreaker tier** (WHOLESOME — the banned word: Starbucks orders, Pixar crying, pineapple pizza), much of it hiding at spice 3-4; meanwhile perfect feral-impersonal cards (shovel/bleach/tarp) are wrongly locked behind max-spice consent gates, and 20+ cards are "dark-not-funny" (interrogation prompts wearing a party costume: body counts, who-made-you-cry). 8 cards violate the hard ban outright (truthful answers implicate present third parties or can out orientation/fidelity — e.g. "Number of people in this room they've thought about hooking up with").

**4. Template fatigue: the corpus is a few jokes photocopied.**
59 cards mention an ex (12 of 14 decks). 17+ cards are the same skeleton ("private digital artifact exposed at public venue"). 15 jokes appear near-verbatim in TWO different decks (Build-A-Bear, accidental-cult-robes, rotisserie chicken…), plus outright verbatim dupes within decks. Alibi Drop is three skeletons in a trenchcoat ("What happened at X" ×14). Players clock the template by round five and the deck reads as one joke.

**5. Cards ignore their game's mechanic.**
Each game has a core verb the card must feed — a contested vote, a lie-worthy claim, a debatable line, a smuggleable word. Whole failure classes exist per deck: Over/Under stats with no receipt (subject invents a number; bet can't resolve), Alibi words that are natural answers to their own accusation (zero contortion = zero comedy), Reality Check stats only the subject could know (room holds no evidence = no debate), Taboo forbidden lists that leave the first-instinct clue legal, Roast prompts where everyone qualifies (no vote tension), Red Flag cards where display text and data fields contain two different jokes (19/50 — a build bug shipped as content).

## What the 123 keepers share (the HELLDECK card, discovered)

A **specific filmed scene** (place, object, number — never a vibe-verdict), a **self-incriminating euphemism** that opens a loop the table must close ("...and call it research"), **table-awareness** ("Who has blackmail material on everyone in this room?"), and **two-axis vote tension** (crime × incompetence: "Who would accidentally send a voice note of themselves talking trash?"). Model cards and per-deck rebuild requirements: see CONTENT_BIBLE.md.

## Systems findings

- **The madlib template pipeline cannot ship a joke** and is unsalvageable: 75/75 sexual_innuendo lexicon entries grammar-break their host template; 73/75 carry embedded punchlines that collide with any context; the funny in the best cards is engineered dependency between halves — exactly what random slot-sampling can't produce. Verdict: 100% hand-written corpus; {NAME} is the only safe substitution.
- **quality_score=10 on all 917 cards** — the curation field carries zero signal.
- Old spice ratings are unusable as-is (60% conflated); every card gets re-rated on the two-axis rubric during rebuild.

input.content:
# CONTENT_BIBLE.md — The writing law for HELLDECK content

_Binding for every card written for HELLDECK 2.0 (The Descent — HDRealRules2.md). Derived from the full-corpus autopsy (CARD_AUTOPSY.md), Jackbox/CAH craft research, humor theory, and 9 simulated playtests. A card that breaks a law does not ship, no matter how funny it is._

## Voice

A dive bar at 2am. A group chat that went too far. Affectionate violence. The card is a friend proposing something horrible — never a listicle headline, never a corporate icebreaker, never a tweet. UNHINGED, never WHOLESOME. Every card must read aloud in one breath.

## The two-axis rating (replaces "spice")

Every card carries **EXPOSURE (E)** and **CHAOS (C)**, each 1–5:
- **E = how personal.** What answering/being-targeted truthfully confesses about a present person. E is what consent ceilings cap. E5 = real romantic/sexual/shame history behind a deniability frame.
- **C = how deranged.** Feral imagery, dark premises, absurdity. **C is never capped and never low.** C≥3 at EVERY exposure tier — "feral-but-impersonal" (E1/C5: shovel-bleach-tarp, crimes against mannequins) is the backbone of low-E play. A card that could open a corporate offsite (E≤2, C≤2) does not exist in this game.
- Dark premises need a benign frame (fictional distance, "would," euphemism). Dark WITHOUT a frame is an interrogation, not a joke (the body-count class) — kill.

## THE TEN COMMANDMENTS

1. **Film a scene, never deliver a verdict.** Concrete act + place/object/number/time ("40 cans of beans," "2am," "the trunk"). Banned words in card text: unhinged, chaotic, toxic, main character, NPC, "gives off X energy," "most likely to be [adjective]." The card shows; the table judges.
2. **The card asks; the room answers.** If the joke is complete on the card, players are a laugh track and the deck decays like CAH. No prewritten punchlines, no twist endings, no trailing tags ("They knew."). Setups end where the story begins.
3. **Pass the contest test, both directions.** Roast/vote cards: name the 2–3 friend archetypes who'd split the vote — universal behaviors (everyone qualifies) and single-archetype behaviors (one obvious victim) both fail. Answer cards: 3 different funny answers in 10 seconds or kill.
4. **E prices exposure; C is never the price.** No icebreakers at any tier. No "edgy vocabulary = high spice" (EDGING-the-taboo-word is E1). Re-rate everything; trust nothing from the old corpus.
5. **The third-party firewall.** Above E3: no card whose truthful answer names or implicates specific present people (the "in this room" desire/history class is dead), none whose answer can out orientation, fidelity, or health. Ever: no appearance, no family death, no protected traits.
6. **Zero shelf-life vocabulary.** No meme nouns or brands as punchlines (OnlyFans, LinkedIn, feet pics, mugshots, situationship, Spotify Wrapped, Funko, NFTs, therapy-speak…). Brands only as neutral setting (a Costco can host a story; "crying at Costco" can't be the joke). If it quotes 2021 Twitter, it dies with 2021 Twitter. If the debate existed on Reddit before 2020 (duck-sized horses, pineapple pizza), it does not exist here.
7. **Skeleton budget: max 2 cards per skeleton per deck, and no joke exists twice anywhere in the corpus.** Run dedup on verb+shape within decks and across decks before anything goes gold (the old corpus shipped 15 cross-deck duplicate jokes).
8. **Build the table-aware lane.** Each deck carries its quota of cards referencing the present room ("blackmail material on everyone in this room," {NAME} slots) — the only structurally un-copyable content. {NAME} never appears in a slot that implicates them factually above E3 (firewall applies).
9. **Written to be spoken.** One breath to read, <2 seconds to glance the reveal, performance-first where the mechanic performs (if a card is equally funny read silently, it's a tweet — cut it). Prefer cards a performer can act out; seed inherently funny concrete words (eulogy, silverback, thirst trap).
10. **Register quotas (the anti-monoculture law).** Per deck: ≤35% relatable-observational (the old corpus was 84%); the rest spread across absurdist/escalation, deadpan understatement, sustained menace, petty-domestic, gross-out/body, physical, and high-register parody (legalese, nature-doc, noir, corporate-memo). Reference worlds beyond phones/dating/therapy are mandatory: food, animals, history/myth, small-town, trades, sports, medicine, nature. ≤20% of any deck may reference a phone/app at all (old corpus: 38%).

## House moves (encouraged)

- **The euphemism engine**: self-incriminating reframes that open loops ("...and call it research," "celebrating life") — hands the table a defense to demolish.
- **Two-axis vote tension**: crime × incompetence ("Who would accidentally send a voice note of themselves talking trash?") — different players vote on different logic; the argument is the game.
- **Benign violation done right**: genuinely dark premise + fictional distance + flattering-damning trait ("Who would befriend their kidnapper within the first hour?").
- **One anchor + two open slots** (story games): exactly one interrogable concrete detail, at least two blanks the performer must fill live.
- **Accidental-confession framing** (speed games): second-person categories where desperate blurts read as self-exposure with speed as deniability.

## Per-deck laws (summary — full requirements in the autopsy JSONs)

**Roast Consensus (150+):** two-victim test; scene-not-verdict; ~30% past-tense story-mining ("Who HAS…", E≤3); 20+ table-aware cards; euphemism engine as house style.
**Fill-In Finisher (150+):** ≥10 distinct structures (old deck: 41% two skeletons); ≥40% performance-first (comedy exists only ALOUD — speech acts: eulogies, voicemails, last words); write-verb only (invented punchlines, never true-history recall); ≥25% {NAME}/room hooks (old: zero); every setup seeds one concrete comic element.
**Over/Under (150+):** ≥70% phone-receipt-resolvable in <10s (name the receipt surface on the card); counts timeboxed (today/this week/this month — never lifetime); non-receipt counts pride-valenced (high number = legend, never shame-tally); line-debatability test (conflicting priors about THIS person or kill).
**Confession or Cap (150+):** plausibility-window test (must be arguable both believe AND cap); one anchor + two open slots; POV-universal and uncheckable (lockable TRUE by anyone at the table); logic must survive 5s of jury scrutiny; setup-never-punchline.
**Scatterblast (80):** category+letter only (the bomb owns timing); letter audit (10 valid answers aloud or change the letter); ≥50% second-person accidental-confession categories; desperation-arc test (the 8th answer must be funnier than the 1st); ≤25% "Things…" openers; ≤7 words, shoutable.
**Poison Pitch (100):** balance gate (a strong pitch for EACH side in 30s; blind poll ≤65/35); contrast rule (poisons of different flavors — body vs reputation, private vs public); angle density ≥3 per option; performability bonus (options a pitcher can act out).
**Red Flag Rally (100):** one card = one joke = one source of truth (display and data fields must match — old deck: 19/50 mismatched); perk and flag on orthogonal axes (perk genuinely tempting to a stranger, flag independently disqualifying — no perk-negation reveals); split-room test (write the defender's argument AND the room's counter-heckle; either silent = dead); ≥4 rotating structures; state evidence flat, never editorialize.
**Alibi Drop (80):** words are contraband, not answers (orthogonal to the accusation; if a word could be the actual explanation, it's dead); lineup-compatible (1–2 word common nouns that sit beside 5 decoys); accusations ≤14 words, absurd-but-answerable, with one forensic detail; no word repeats deck-wide; ~20% room-history accusations; each card ships WITH its 5 decoy words.
**Text Thread Trap (60 + tones):** left-on-read test (the text must demand a reply); punchline amputation (the sender never jokes — the performer is the comedian); open-blank rule (the incriminating thing is never specified); ≤4 cards per sender archetype; 8–10 performance set-pieces (the reply is a bit); {NAME} lane added (old: zero).
**Reality Check (60):** two-gate test (room holds evidence + subject's ego is invested); ban "how many times" counts (no perception gap); 1–10 only, direction locked; mine Dunning-Kruger domains (driving, humor, temper, being over an ex); seed live-demo dimensions (interrupting, taking a joke — the debate performs the test).
**Taboo Timer (60):** BODY-COUNT-test list-writing (speak the 3 clues a drunk clue-giver gives; every load-bearing word goes on the list); pop-culture route audit (song/movie sharing the name goes on the list or the word dies); aliveness gate (alive in 2026, survives 24 months); ≤20% per topic cluster; forbidden lists cover stems/inflections.
**Hot Seat Imposter (60):** observation test (≥2 people in the room plausibly hold evidence; ban "nobody knows"/"secretly"); resolvable ground truth in <10s (story or on-the-spot receipt); performance over trivia (never one-token answers); design for the LIE (a wrong-but-believable answer should roast the target); ≤40% one skeleton; ≤2 phone-contents cards.
**Title Fight (40):** every card names what fighters DO and what the crowd votes on; ≥1/3 physical/performative duels (straight-face duel, slow-mo reenactment, hype-man-off); both corners defensible in 10s; {NAME}/self-testimony leverage ("cite your worst ___; room votes"); the Reddit-canon ban list is absolute.

## The funnel (no card skips a stage)

1. **Draft** against this bible (register + E/C assigned at write time).
2. **Self-cull**: writer kills bottom third before submission (Jackbox: reject any prompt you can't answer 3 ways fast).
3. **Dedup**: skeleton + verb-shape scan within deck and cross-deck.
4. **Mechanic gate**: per-deck test (two-victim / 3-answers / balance / receipt / plausibility-window / letter audit…).
5. **Ban screens**: vocabulary expiry, third-party firewall, wholesome test ("could this run at an HR offsite?" → kill), specificity test ("does anything break if this card moves to a generic party deck?" → if no, kill).
6. **Table read**: performed aloud in the target register; if it's equally funny silent, cut.
7. **Gold**: E/C rated, decoys/receipts/forbidden-lists attached per deck schema, telemetry-tracked from night one (🔥 rates drive culls in content drops).

## The 123 keepers

Kept card IDs live in the autopsy JSONs (`autopsy_deck_*.json`, `keep_ids`). They ship as-is, re-rated on E/C, and serve as calibration anchors for every writer.

---
name: helldeck-2-descent-pivot
description: HELLDECK 2.0 pivot state — validated Descent gameplay model, card autopsy verdict, rebuild progress and file locations
metadata:
  type: project
---

As of 2026-07-16, HELLDECK is pivoting from single-Android pass-and-play to "The Descent": Jackbox-style phones-only party game (no TV), every player on own phone, joiners via mobile browser (QR + 4-letter code, no install), one host pays $9.99 one-time after a free first night. Architecture decision (validated by adversarial audit): game brain = one Cloudflare Durable Object per room (TypeScript, server-authoritative); ONE web UI runtime for everyone; Android app is only a thin WebView shell (Play Billing, stage audio, lift-to-sin accelerometer); anyone can host from any phone including iPhone (web checkout via Stripe) — Android-only hosting would have killed ~75-80% of conversions.

**Canonical docs (in repo root):** `HDRealRules2.md` (new rules, supersedes HDRealRules.md), `CARD_AUTOPSY.md` (why cards failed: 14% keep rate of 864 audited), `CONTENT_BIBLE.md` (binding writing law: Ten Commandments, EXPOSURE/CHAOS two-axis rating replacing spice, per-deck laws, funnel). Nothing committed to git yet — Nick hasn't asked.

**Validation:** model iterated v1→v3 through 9 simulated playtests + 4 adversarial audits (workflows). Final: all edge groups FUN (trio3 8/10, bar6 8/10, birthday13 7.5/10), attackers all HOLDS_WITH_FIXES, fixes folded into v3. Spec history in session scratchpad (`spec_v1/v2/v3.md`, `report_*.md`, `autopsy_*.json`, `r2_*.json`).

**Key design facts:** launch with 8 deep decks (Roast Consensus/Fill-In/Over-Under/Confession at 150+; Scatterblast 80, Poison Pitch 100, Red Flag 100, Alibi 80), Content Drop 1 adds Text Thread/Reality Check/Taboo/Hot Seat; Unifying Theory cut; Title Fight = physical spike. Sessions = 5/7/9 "circles," spice(exposure) escalates with depth, per-player private heat ceilings (never rendered), Brimstone burns with ≥10s pre-views, PLEAD THE FIFTH on blocking inputs, perform-then-vote Fill-In, reveal-holds for the table roast, points to performers never majority-voters. On-device LLM is dead code being removed (1.7GB GGUFs block Play publishing); AI only as offline draft→human-curate content pipeline.

**Rebuild status:** Wave 1 workflow script staged at session `workflows/scripts/rebuild-wave1.js` (4 big decks: 3 register-assigned writers + curator + 2 adversarial verifiers per deck). Waves 2-3 (small decks, Drop 1) to follow same pattern. Session usage limits interrupted runs repeatedly — workflows support resumeFromRunId cache resume.

Related: [[helldeck-taste-standards]] (CLAUDE.md in repo enforces UNHINGED-never-WHOLESOME).

**Everything you asked for is either done or staged. Full picture:**

**1. The gameplay model — done and validated.** "The Descent" survived 9 simulated playtests and 4 adversarial audits across 3 design iterations. Final scores: every edge group lands FUN (3-player trio 8/10, loud-bar iPhone group 8/10, 13-person chaos party 7.5/10), and every simulated group said they'd play again. Your instinct was right and the research confirmed the exact shape: **phones only, no TV, joiners in the browser** (QR + room code, zero install — the pattern Jackbox/Kahoot/Death by AI all converged on), **app only for the host** — and one critical upgrade the business audit forced: the game engine lives server-side (Cloudflare, $0 at playtest scale), so **an iPhone can host too**. Android-only hosting would have bounced ~3 of 4 "I want to host this" conversions. One payer, $9.99 once, first night free, paywall at the *second* night's start button.

**2. Rules — adjusted and written**: `HDRealRules2.md` in the repo. Sessions are a descent through 5/7/9 circles with heat escalating by depth, private per-player consent ceilings that are never displayed, perform-then-vote Fill-In (Quiplash done right without a TV), reveals that hold while the table roasts, 8 deep launch games instead of 14 thin ones, and a real ending with superlatives and a share card.

**3. Card autopsy — done, deep**: `CARD_AUTOPSY.md`. All 864 cards in the 13 live decks judged individually: **52% kill, 34% rewrite, 14% keep.** Five root causes, quantified: cards written for scrolling not tables (84% one Twitter voice; 38% reference phones/apps), cards that tell the joke so players can't, one "spice" number conflating *personal* with *deranged* (30% of the corpus is corporate-icebreaker tier — the banned WHOLESOME), massive skeleton duplication (59 ex cards; 15 jokes duplicated across decks), and cards that ignore their game's mechanic. The 123 keepers revealed the house style: a filmed scene + a self-incriminating euphemism + table-awareness ("Who has blackmail material on everyone in this room?").

**4. The rebuild — law written, Wave 1 staged.** `CONTENT_BIBLE.md` is the binding writing law (Ten Commandments, EXPOSURE/CHAOS two-axis rating, per-deck laws, a CAH-style kill funnel). Wave 1 — the four 150-card decks, built by register-assigned writers → curator → adversarial verifiers — launches automatically when the session usage limit resets at 6:50pm (third limit hit today; everything resumes from cache, nothing is lost). Waves 2–3 (small decks + Content Drop 1) follow the same machine.

All state is saved to project memory, so any future session picks this up cold. Nothing has been committed to git — say the word when you want that.

---

# PART 3 — WEBSOCKET PROTOCOL

## 3.1 Connection lifecycle

1. Client opens `wss://<host>/ws/<CODE>?token=<playerToken>&name=<urlenc>&v=1`.
   - No token → server issues one in `WELCOME` (client stores in `localStorage["hd:<CODE>:token"]`).
   - Existing token + same room → silent reseat (reconnect), full state push.
2. Server sends `WELCOME`, then `STATE` (full redacted snapshot), then deltas.
3. Client answers every `PING` immediately (clock sync, 3.3).
4. On tab hide/show: client sets `epochLocal++`, discards queued renders, sends `{t:"RESYNC"}` on visible → server replies with full `STATE`.

## 3.2 Messages (complete list — anything not here does not exist)

**Client → Server** (all include nothing else; server knows who you are from the socket):
```jsonc
{"t":"JOIN","name":"SAM","avatar":3}
{"t":"ATTEST18"}                       // one-tap attestation
{"t":"CEILING","v":4}                  // private consent pick
{"t":"CONFIG","depth":7,"vibe":"feral","stage":true}   // host only
{"t":"BEGIN"}                          // host only; server validates entitlement first
{"t":"INPUT","p":{...}}                // per-game payload, validated per game schema
{"t":"BURN","kind":"card"}            // during pre-view window only
{"t":"BURN","kind":"spotlight"}
{"t":"DESCEND"}                        // during REVEAL; host always, everyone after 45s softcap
{"t":"VOID"}                           // host only, during WAITING_ON
{"t":"FIFTH"}                          // blocking-input subject only
{"t":"SKIPEM"}                         // during a live performance
{"t":"REST"}                           // performer only
{"t":"FIRE","n":4}                     // coalesced: max 1 msg / 500ms, n = taps since last
{"t":"PONG","id":17,"cl":1720900000000}
{"t":"RESYNC"}
```

**Server → Client:**
```jsonc
{"t":"WELCOME","you":"<playerId>","token":"<playerToken>","code":"HELL"}
{"t":"STATE","s":{...},"epoch":42}         // FULL redacted state (3.4); render source of truth
{"t":"PATCH","epoch":43,"ops":[...]}       // RFC6902-lite: only replace/add on known paths
{"t":"PRIVATE","k":"role"|"card"|"words"|"preview","p":{...}}  // secrets, per-socket only
{"t":"PING","id":17,"sv":1720900000123}
{"t":"AT","timerId":"input:c3","at":1720900012000}  // deadline in SERVER time; client maps via offset
{"t":"AUDIO","sting":"boom"|"bell"|"burn"|"descend"|"judgment"}  // host phone honors, others ignore
{"t":"HEAT","n":37}                        // fire-tap buckets, ≤4Hz
{"t":"ERR","code":"ROOM_FULL"|"BAD_INPUT"|"NOT_HOST"|"NO_ENTITLEMENT"|"ROOM_EXPIRED","msg":"..."}
```

## 3.3 Clock sync (the reveal-simultaneity mechanism)

- On connect and every 60s: server sends 5 `PING`s 200ms apart; client `PONG`s with its clock.
- Server computes per-client offset = median of 5 samples; stores on socket.
- Every deadline is broadcast as **server time** (`AT`). Client renders countdowns from `serverTime - offset`.
- Reveals: server schedules `AT` ≥ 1000ms in the future → all phones flip within ~30–80ms of each other (target: p95 ≤ 150ms skew).
- Countdown ticks are NEVER individual messages — clients derive them from the single deadline.

## 3.4 Redaction (secrets never transit to the wrong phone)

`STATE`/`PATCH` are computed **per socket**. Before send, strip: other players' `heatCeiling` (all ceilings — even your own is only echoed to you), pending secret inputs (votes cast, truth-locks), role assignments, pre-view cards, alibi words, taboo words (except clue-giver + buzzer sockets), Devil's Bargain holder (until Judgment). Secrets travel only via `PRIVATE` to the entitled socket. **Test D-113 asserts a serialized broadcast frame contains none of these fields.**

## 3.5 Idempotency & epochs

- Every phase change bumps `epoch`. Client tags rendered UI with epoch; any `PATCH` with `epoch <= current` is dropped.
- `INPUT` for a phase that already ended → server replies `ERR BAD_INPUT` silently (client ignores).
- Reveals are **state, not events**: a phone reconnecting 2s late renders the current reveal from `STATE` — nothing is missed, nothing double-fires.

---

# PART 4 — THE NIGHT ENGINE

## 4.1 Room creation & codes

- `POST /api/room` → `{code}`. Code alphabet: `BCDFGHJKLMNPRSTVWXYZ` (20 consonants, no vowels → no accidental words, no 0/O/1/I confusion). 20^4 = 160k rooms.
- Room TTL: DO Alarm deletes state 30 min after last socket closes (10 min if Night never began). Crew memory whitelist is exported to the host client before deletion (10.2).

## 4.2 Global flow

```
LOBBY ──(host CONFIG + ≥3 players + all CEILING set + entitlement)──► CONSENT done
  └─► arc = buildArc(config, players)                     // 4.3
  └─► for circle in arc:
        CIRCLE_INTRO (8s; 15s first-time explainer, 5s title card after)
        DEAL (5–6s ceremony; absorbs ≤1 burn invisibly)   // 4.5
        [game-specific INPUT phases]                       // Part 5 per game
        REVEAL (hold ≥20s; DESCEND: host anytime, anyone ≥45s; fire-decay ≥8s quiet past 20s)
        LADDER (5s)
  └─► JUDGMENT (winner, superlatives, share card, "descend again?")
```

## 4.3 Arc builder (`arc.ts`) — deterministic given (config, players, seed)

```
pool = LAUNCH8 filtered by: minN ≤ nPlayers, irlFamiliar gate, content available at legal E-tiers
assert circles ≤ uniqueLegalGames + sanctionedRepeats(nPlayers)  // else lobby caps depth (UI enforces first)
slots[0]        = simultaneous-input game (roast | overunder | fillin)
slots[mid]      = scatter (physical spike);  if depth ≥ 7: second spike = titlefight at mid+2
slots[last]     = finale: all-players-score ∩ legal (roast|fillin|overunder; N≤4 prefer overunder)
fill remaining: no two subject-targeting circles back-to-back after slot 4;
                ≥1 outward game per ⌈depth/3⌉ block; spotlight games only after slot 2;
                repeats only {roast, scatter, overunder}, never adjacent, max 1 repeat each
loops per circle: roast=3 prompts, overunder=2 subjects, scatter=best-of-3,
                  confession/texttrap/reality/redflag/alibi/poison = 2 loops, fillin=1 (N≤6) | 2 setups (N≥7)
E-curve: startRung = max(1, min(vibe∈{sober:1,warm:2,feral:3}, genericCeiling−2))
         rung(c) = min(startRung + floor((c−1) * (maxLegal−startRung) / (depth−1)), tierCeiling(circle))
devilsBargain: if depth ≥ 5 → attach to a random all-players-score circle in last third, holder = current last place at that circle's start
```

## 4.4 Consent filter (`consent.ts`)

```
genericCeiling  = nPlayers ≤ 4 ? min(ceilings) : secondLowest(ceilings)
cardLegal(card, subjectIds):
  if card names subjects → E ≤ min(ceiling of each named subject)
  else if game is vote-emergent (roast) and E > 3 → E ≤ min(all ceilings)
  else → E ≤ genericCeiling
NEVER surface any ceiling value, or whether filtering occurred, anywhere. (Test D-114.)
```

## 4.5 Deal ceremony & burns (`deal.ts`)

```
On DEAL: pick card + backup card (both legal, both reserved).
  t=0     broadcast ceremony start ("THE DECK IS CHOOSING ITS VICTIM…", fixed 5.5s)
  t=0     if card names a subject → PRIVATE preview to subject {card, burnDeadline: t+10s}
          (preview windows only during heads-down phases; ceremony IS heads-down)
  t≤10s   subject may BURN → swap to backup, NO trace: same ceremony length, no animation delta,
          subject-burn not decremented visibly, not in telemetry attributably
  t=10s   (or on burn) broadcast card to all → next phase
Spotlight assignment: same shape — PRIVATE assignment 10s before public announce; room sees "DEALING…".
Volunteer-first: 5s "WHO WANTS BLOOD?" claim button precedes random assignment (weighted to lowest spotlightCount).
```

## 4.6 Scoring (`scoring.ts`) — complete table

| Event | Points |
|---|---|
| Fill-In: your punchline wins the vote | +3 (finale Reader: +1 per 10 fires during read, cap +3) |
| Fill-In: panic-button answer wins | +1 (half, rounded down from +3 → +1) |
| Roast: you voted the plurality victim | +2 |
| Roast: Room Heat (unanimity−victim at N≤5; ≥60% at N≥8; ≥80% else) | +1 extra to plurality voters |
| Over/Under: correct bet | +2; exact-line push → everyone +1 |
| Confession: jury fooled (majority wrong) | confessor +3 |
| Confession: your believe/cap vote correct | +1 |
| Poison: your pitch wins | +3 (N=3 damage-meter: score = rating; predict-own-score exact → +2) |
| Red Flag: SMASH verdict (defense survived) | defender +3 |
| Alibi: jury member per planted word found | +1; accused per word MISSED by >50% of jury | +1 |
| Scatter: survive (not holding at boom) | +1; holder +0 |
| Text Trap: SURVIVED verdict | performer +3 |
| Reality: ego-gap ≤1 | subject +2; every debater +1 if gap ≥4 ("called it") |
| Any vote cast at all (participation) | +1 (max once per circle) |
| PLEAD THE FIFTH / voided round | 0 to all for that loop |
| Finale circle | all points ×3 |
| Devil's Bargain holder, on their bargain circle | circle points ×2 (stacked before finale ×3 if same circle — not possible by construction: bargain never on finale) |

**Law (from playtests): no points for aligning with a majority except Roast. Performers, writers, liars, and subjects earn; spectators tread water.**

## 4.7 Blocking-input terminal machine

```
INPUT(blocking, owner=X):
  deadline = null (timer paused), broadcast WAITING_ON X after 12s grace
  X's phone: input UI + permanent [PLEAD THE FIFTH] button → round loop voided, sting, 0 pts
  t+30s: all phones get [DRAG THEM BACK] / [FEED THEM TO THE PIT] → ≥60% PIT = void loop, sting,
         re-deal a no-subject filler prompt if loop had not produced content yet
  host always has [VOID ROUND]
  X disconnects >90s (seat lapse): auto-void, sting "THE WITNESS FLED"
  scribe-downgrade (overunder only): host may enter X's verbal claim, flagged UNVERIFIED (no receipt bonus)
NEVER auto-fabricate a truth value. (Test D-115.)
```

## 4.8 Imp lifecycle

```
join while phase != LOBBY or nPlayers = 12 → role = imp
imp rights: name enters {NAME} pool; FIRE; votes at 0.5 weight (ceil on ties never decided by imps);
            contributes 1 curated punchline in N≥7 fillin (screen cameo, never performed)
at each CIRCLE_INTRO: convert imps → players in join order while nPlayers < 12,
  announce as role ("ZOE IS TONIGHT'S IMP — feed her souls" → "ZOE HAS CLAWED HER WAY UP")
residual imps at JUDGMENT: guaranteed superlative ("STILL IN HELL'S WAITING ROOM")
```

<!-- APPEND:PART5 -->

+{"t":"PRIVATE","k":"role"|"card"|"words"|"preview","p":{...}}  // secrets, per-socket only
+{"t":"AT","timerId":"input:c3","at":1720900012000}  // deadline in SERVER time; client maps via offset
+{"t":"AUDIO","sting":"boom"|"bell"|"burn"|"descend"|"judgment"}  // host phone honors, others ignore
+{"t":"HEAT","n":37}                        // fire-tap buckets, ≤4Hz
+{"t":"ERR","code":"ROOM_FULL"|"BAD_INPUT"|"NOT_HOST"|"NO_ENTITLEMENT"|"ROOM_EXPIRED","msg":"..."}
+## 3.3 Clock sync (the reveal-simultaneity mechanism)
+- On connect and every 60s: server sends 5 `PING`s 200ms apart; client `PONG`s with its clock.
+- Server computes per-client offset = median of 5 samples; stores on socket.
+- Every deadline is broadcast as **server time** (`AT`). Client renders countdowns from `serverTime - offset`.
+- Reveals: server schedules `AT` ≥ 1000ms in the future → all phones flip within ~30–80ms of each other (target: p95 ≤ 150ms skew).
+- Countdown ticks are NEVER individual messages — clients derive them from the single deadline.
+## 3.4 Redaction (secrets never transit to the wrong phone)

+`STATE`/`PATCH` are computed **per socket**. Before send, strip: other players' `heatCeiling` (all ceilings — even your own is only echoed to you), pending secret inputs (votes cast, truth-locks), role assignments, pre-view cards, alibi words, taboo words (except clue-giver + buzzer sockets), Devil's Bargain holder (until Judgment). Secrets travel only via `PRIVATE` to the entitled socket. **Test D-113 asserts a serialized broadcast frame contains none of these fields.**
+## 3.5 Idempotency & epochs
+- Every phase change bumps `epoch`. Client tags rendered UI with epoch; any `PATCH` with `epoch <= current` is dropped.
+- `INPUT` for a phase that already ended → server replies `ERR BAD_INPUT` silently (client ignores).
+- Reveals are **state, not events**: a phone reconnecting 2s late renders the current reveal from `STATE` — nothing is missed, nothing double-fires.
+# PART 4 — THE NIGHT ENGINE
+## 4.1 Room creation & codes
+- `POST /api/room` → `{code}`. Code alphabet: `BCDFGHJKLMNPRSTVWXYZ` (20 consonants, no vowels → no accidental words, no 0/O/1/I confusion). 20^4 = 160k rooms.
+- Room TTL: DO Alarm deletes state 30 min after last socket closes (10 min if Night never began). Crew memory whitelist is exported to the host client before deletion (10.2).
+## 4.2 Global flow
+LOBBY ──(host CONFIG + ≥3 players + all CEILING set + entitlement)──► CONSENT done
+  └─► arc = buildArc(config, players)                     // 4.3
+  └─► for circle in arc:
+        CIRCLE_INTRO (8s; 15s first-time explainer, 5s title card after)
+        DEAL (5–6s ceremony; absorbs ≤1 burn invisibly)   // 4.5
+        [game-specific INPUT phases]                       // Part 5 per game
+        REVEAL (hold ≥20s; DESCEND: host anytime, anyone ≥45s; fire-decay ≥8s quiet past 20s)
+        LADDER (5s)
+  └─► JUDGMENT (winner, superlatives, share card, "descend again?")
+## 4.3 Arc builder (`arc.ts`) — deterministic given (config, players, seed)
+pool = LAUNCH8 filtered by: minN ≤ nPlayers, irlFamiliar gate, content available at legal E-tiers
+assert circles ≤ uniqueLegalGames + sanctionedRepeats(nPlayers)  // else lobby caps depth (UI enforces first)
+slots[0]        = simultaneous-input game (roast | overunder | fillin)
+slots[mid]      = scatter (physical spike);  if depth ≥ 7: second spike = titlefight at mid+2
+slots[last]     = finale: all-players-score ∩ legal (roast|fillin|overunder; N≤4 prefer overunder)
+fill remaining: no two subject-targeting circles back-to-back after slot 4;
+                ≥1 outward game per ⌈depth/3⌉ block; spotlight games only after slot 2;
+                repeats only {roast, scatter, overunder}, never adjacent, max 1 repeat each
+loops per circle: roast=3 prompts, overunder=2 subjects, scatter=best-of-3,
+                  confession/texttrap/reality/redflag/alibi/poison = 2 loops, fillin=1 (N≤6) | 2 setups (N≥7)
+E-curve: startRung = max(1, min(vibe∈{sober:1,warm:2,feral:3}, genericCeiling−2))
+         rung(c) = min(startRung + floor((c−1) * (maxLegal−startRung) / (depth−1)), tierCeiling(circle))
+devilsBargain: if depth ≥ 5 → attach to a random all-players-score circle in last third, holder = current last place at that circle's start
+## 4.4 Consent filter (`consent.ts`)
+genericCeiling  = nPlayers ≤ 4 ? min(ceilings) : secondLowest(ceilings)
+cardLegal(card, subjectIds):
+  if card names subjects → E ≤ min(ceiling of each named subject)
+  else if game is vote-emergent (roast) and E > 3 → E ≤ min(all ceilings)
+  else → E ≤ genericCeiling
+NEVER surface any ceiling value, or whether filtering occurred, anywhere. (Test D-114.)
+## 4.5 Deal ceremony & burns (`deal.ts`)
+On DEAL: pick card + backup card (both legal, both reserved).
+  t=0     broadcast ceremony start ("THE DECK IS CHOOSING ITS VICTIM…", fixed 5.5s)
+  t=0     if card names a subject → PRIVATE preview to subject {card, burnDeadline: t+10s}
+          (preview windows only during heads-down phases; ceremony IS heads-down)
+  t≤10s   subject may BURN → swap to backup, NO trace: same ceremony length, no animation delta,
+          subject-burn not decremented visibly, not in telemetry attributably
+  t=10s   (or on burn) broadcast card to all → next phase
+Spotlight assignment: same shape — PRIVATE assignment 10s before public announce; room sees "DEALING…".
+Volunteer-first: 5s "WHO WANTS BLOOD?" claim button precedes random assignment (weighted to lowest spotlightCount).
+## 4.6 Scoring (`scoring.ts`) — complete table
+| Event | Points |
+| Fill-In: your punchline wins the vote | +3 (finale Reader: +1 per 10 fires during read, cap +3) |
+| Fill-In: panic-button answer wins | +1 (half, rounded down from +3 → +1) |
+| Roast: you voted the plurality victim | +2 |
+| Roast: Room Heat (unanimity−victim at N≤5; ≥60% at N≥8; ≥80% else) | +1 extra to plurality voters |
+| Over/Under: correct bet | +2; exact-line push → everyone +1 |
+| Confession: jury fooled (majority wrong) | confessor +3 |
+| Confession: your believe/cap vote correct | +1 |
+| Poison: your pitch wins | +3 (N=3 damage-meter: score = rating; predict-own-score exact → +2) |
+| Red Flag: SMASH verdict (defense survived) | defender +3 |
+| Alibi: jury member per planted word found | +1; accused per word MISSED by >50% of jury | +1 |
+| Scatter: survive (not holding at boom) | +1; holder +0 |
+| Text Trap: SURVIVED verdict | performer +3 |
+| Reality: ego-gap ≤1 | subject +2; every debater +1 if gap ≥4 ("called it") |
+| Any vote cast at all (participation) | +1 (max once per circle) |
+| PLEAD THE FIFTH / voided round | 0 to all for that loop |
+| Finale circle | all points ×3 |
+| Devil's Bargain holder, on their bargain circle | circle points ×2 (stacked before finale ×3 if same circle — not possible by construction: bargain never on finale) |
+**Law (from playtests): no points for aligning with a majority except Roast. Performers, writers, liars, and subjects earn; spectators tread water.**
+## 4.7 Blocking-input terminal machine
+INPUT(blocking, owner=X):
+  deadline = null (timer paused), broadcast WAITING_ON X after 12s grace
+  X's phone: input UI + permanent [PLEAD THE FIFTH] button → round loop voided, sting, 0 pts
+  t+30s: all phones get [DRAG THEM BACK] / [FEED THEM TO THE PIT] → ≥60% PIT = void loop, sting,
+         re-deal a no-subject filler prompt if loop had not produced content yet
+  host always has [VOID ROUND]
+  X disconnects >90s (seat lapse): auto-void, sting "THE WITNESS FLED"
+  scribe-downgrade (overunder only): host may enter X's verbal claim, flagged UNVERIFIED (no receipt bonus)
+NEVER auto-fabricate a truth value. (Test D-115.)
+## 4.8 Imp lifecycle
+join while phase != LOBBY or nPlayers = 12 → role = imp
+imp rights: name enters {NAME} pool; FIRE; votes at 0.5 weight (ceil on ties never decided by imps);
+            contributes 1 curated punchline in N≥7 fillin (screen cameo, never performed)
+at each CIRCLE_INTRO: convert imps → players in join order while nPlayers < 12,
+  announce as role ("ZOE IS TONIGHT'S IMP — feed her souls" → "ZOE HAS CLAWED HER WAY UP")
+residual imps at JUDGMENT: guaranteed superlative ("STILL IN HELL'S WAITING ROOM")
+<!-- APPEND:PART5 -->

aiTitle: Redesign card game for fun multiplayer gameplay

input.new_string:
---

# PART 5 — PER-GAME SPECIFICATIONS (LAUNCH 8)

Format per game: **phases** (name · duration · who acts · every phone's screen), **input class**, **resolution/ties**, **small-N variants**, **acceptance criteria**. All timers in seconds; all input phases are simultaneous. "All:" = every phone. Performer screens always also show the permanent Brimstone/status strip (thin, bottom).

## 5.1 ROAST CONSENSUS (`games/roast.ts`) — minN 3 · 3 prompts/circle

| # | Phase | Dur | Screens |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEAL ceremony | 5.5 | All: roulette animation |
| 2 | VOTE (skippable) | 20 | All: prompt + tappable avatar grid (self disabled) + countdown. Stage (if on): prompt + "4/6 voted" tally only |
| 3 | REVEAL | hold | All: 3-2-1 flash → victim's name in lights + vote spread (bar per player, anonymous). Fire button live |
| 4 | → next prompt (×3) or LADDER | | |

- Resolution: plurality victim. Tie → **DOUBLE ROAST** (both names in lights; a vote for either counts as plurality). Room Heat per 4.6.
- N=3–4: **FACE YOUR ACCUSERS** — vote spread shows names→names (attributed). Display-suppression per circle: an edge shown once this circle renders as "▮▮▮" the second time. Prompts capped E≤3 in this mode. Ballots are never constrained.
- Acceptance: at N=6, full 3-prompt circle ≤ 3:30 with 20s holds; tie path renders DOUBLE ROAST; no vote identity recoverable from broadcast frames at N≥5 (test asserts).

## 5.2 FILL-IN FINISHER (`games/fillin.ts`) — minN 3

| # | Phase | Dur | Screens |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEAL | 5.5 | All: ceremony → setup card shown to all |
| 2 | WRITE (skippable) | 60 | Writers: setup + text field (140 chars) + [PANIC] (2 curated fallbacks, tap to take, −half pts). Reader: setup + read-tone picker ("read these as: a eulogy / a 911 call / a hostage tape / an apology video") |
| 3 | PERFORM | Reader-paced, cap 90 | Reader: teleprompter — one answer at a time, big type, [NEXT] + [BURN LINE] (UGC strike). All others: "LISTEN UP — answer 3 of 7" + fire button. NO answer text on non-Reader phones |
| 4 | VOTE (skippable) | 15 | All except authors-for-own: full answer texts as memory-aid ballot, randomized order per phone |
| 5 | REVEAL | hold | All: winning line + author name in lights + runner-up |

- N≥7: two setups, writers split; per setup Reader performs ≤4 answers → one vote picks top → grand face-off (both Readers perform, final vote). Two Readers total, alternating.
- N=3: no Reader. Derangement assignment: each performs another's line aloud, prompt shows "you're performing {NAME}'s filth — sell it." Vote on text after all reads.
- Reader for finale: never last place / Bargain holder.
- Acceptance: answers NEVER appear on any non-Reader phone before phase 4 (frame test); panic answers flagged internally for scoring; empty write auto-submits panic option A.

## 5.3 OVER/UNDER (`games/overunder.ts`) — minN 3 · 2 subjects/circle

| # | Phase | Dur | Screens |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEAL + subject pre-view | 10 | Subject (private, during ceremony): card + [BURN]. Then all: stat + subject name + receipt surface ("Settings → Screen Time") |
| 2 | LINE DEBATE (verbal) | 25 | All: "ARGUE. OUT LOUD." + the stat + a big line-setter dial on the SCRIBE's phone (scribe = next seat after subject). Scribe locks the room's line |
| 3 | BET (skippable) | 12 | All except subject: OVER / UNDER buttons. Subject: "fetch your receipt" instruction |
| 4 | TRUTH (blocking) | paused | Subject: number pad + [PLEAD THE FIFTH]. All others: "WAITING ON {NAME}'S RECEIPTS…" + shame countdown per 4.7 |
| 5 | REVEAL | hold | All: 3-2-1 → true number vs line, winners flash, subject vindicated/exposed banner |

- Push (number == line): everyone +1, subject roasted for being median.
- Acceptance: timer provably paused during phase 4 (no auto-number ever — test D-115 covers); subject app-switch + return within seat-hold resumes phase 4 intact; scribe reassignment if scribe disconnects.

## 5.4 CONFESSION OR CAP (`games/confession.ts`) — minN 3 · 2 confessors/circle

| # | Phase | Dur | Screens |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEAL 3 (private) | 20 | Confessor: 3 confession cards, pick 1 (auto-pick #1 at timeout). Others: "THE ACCUSED IS CHOOSING THEIR SIN" |
| 2 | LOCK (blocking) | paused | Confessor: TRUE / FALSE lock + [FIFTH]. Others: waiting screen (rarely >5s) |
| 3 | PERFORM (verbal) | 45, [REST] | Confessor: the card + "sell it" + [I REST MY CASE]. Others: card text + "watch their hands" + fire |
| 4 | JURY VOTE (skippable) | 12 | Others: BELIEVE / CAP. Confessor: "they're deciding your fate" |
| 5 | REVEAL | hold | All: 3-2-1 → TRUE/FALSE stamp + jury spread + fooled/caught banner |

- Tie: confessor wins ("HUNG JURY — THE LIAR WALKS").
- Acceptance: truth-lock value never in any broadcast frame before phase 5; pick-of-3 never reveals the 2 unchosen cards to the room (they return to the pool).

## 5.5 SCATTERBLAST (`games/scatter.ts`) — minN 3 · best-of-3 bombs

| # | Phase | Dur | Screens |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEAL | 5.5 | All: CATEGORY + LETTER, huge type |
| 2 | BOMB (verbal circle) | hidden 15–45 | Stage/volunteer phone: ticking bomb face (audio+pulse). All others: category + letter + subtle pulse (visual-first; bar-proof) |
| 3 | BOOM | 5 | ALL phones: full-screen flash "WHO DIED?" + avatar grid tap |
| 4 | REVEAL | hold | All: loser's name + "THE TABLE HAS SPOKEN" (majority tap) |

- Fuse: uniform random 15–45s, biased +5s per bomb number (bomb 3 longest). Turn passing is verbal — the app never tracks turns.
- Acceptance: boom flash lands on all phones ≤150ms skew p95; WHO DIED majority resolves ties by earliest-tap-timestamp median; category+letter re-render instantly on rejoin.

## 5.6 POISON PITCH (`games/poison.ts`) — minN 3 · 2 duels/circle

| # | Phase | Dur | Screens |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | ASSIGN (private, volunteer-first) | 10 | 5s "WHO WANTS BLOOD?" ×2 → two pitchers get PRIVATE side assignment + [BURN spotlight] |
| 2 | DEAL | 5.5 | All: both options rendered as a fight card ("A vs B") + pitcher names |
| 3 | PITCH A → PITCH B (verbal) | 30+30, [REST] | Pitcher: own option + angle hints ("workaround / silver lining / attack") + [REST]. All: fight card + bell timer + fire + [SKIP-'EM] |
| 4 | VOTE (skippable) | 12 | Non-pitchers: A / B (which PITCH won, not which option) |
| 5 | REVEAL | hold | All: winner banner + vote split |

- Tie: 10s sudden-death rebuttal each → revote → still tied = split pot (+2 each).
- N=3 variant (auto): single judge rates each pitch 1–5 damage meter; pitchers pre-predict own rating (exact → +2).
- Acceptance: sides never leak before phase 2; SKIP-'EM re-deals the CARD only (same pitchers, new dilemma, once per circle).

## 5.7 RED FLAG RALLY (`games/redflag.ts`) — minN 3 · 2 defenses/circle

Phases: ASSIGN (volunteer-first, private, burnable) → DEAL (perk+flag fight card to all) → DEFENSE (45s verbal, [REST], [SKIP-'EM]) → VOTE SMASH/PASS (12s, skippable) → REVEAL (hold).
- Tie → SMASH (performer wins ties). Card law: display text is the ONLY joke source (one card = one joke; build lint rejects field mismatch).
- Acceptance: defender assignment respects spotlightCount weighting; verdict stamp animation ≤2s glanceable.

## 5.8 ALIBI DROP (`games/alibi.ts`) — minN 3 · 2 accused/circle

| # | Phase | Dur | Screens |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | ASSIGN + words (private) | 12 | Accused: accusation + 3 mandatory words + [BURN]. Others: "DEALING…" |
| 2 | ACCUSATION | 5 | All: the accusation, big |
| 3 | ALIBI (verbal) | 30, [REST] | Accused: words pinned top of screen. Others: accusation + "hunt the plants" + fire |
| 4 | WORD HUNT (skippable) | 20 | Jury: 8-word lineup (3 plants + 5 decoys from the card), pick exactly 3 |
| 5 | REVEAL | hold | All: stage-sequenced — each word flips PLANTED/DECOY one at a time with sting (1.2s apart), then scores |

- Acceptance: lineup order randomized per phone; accused's words NEVER in broadcast frames before phase 5; reveal sequence is server-timed beats (AT per word), not a text dump.

## 5.9 Content Drop 1 games

Text Thread Trap, Reality Check, Taboo Timer, Hot Seat Imposter ship in Drop 1 (tracker D-7xx) with the same spec discipline; their phase tables are derived from HDRealRules2 Part III §9–12 and reviewed at Drop-1 kickoff. Not in v1 scope. Title Fight ships at launch as arc spike: ASSIGN two fighters → card names the duel verb → 30s bout (all phones = crowd meter) → point-at-winner vote.

---

# PART 6 — WEB CLIENT

## 6.1 Routes & screens (complete inventory)

| Route | Screen | Elements (exhaustive) |
|---|---|---|
| `/` | Landing/Host | wordmark, "START A NIGHT" (creates room → `/HOST` view), "JOIN A NIGHT" (code input, 4 uppercase letter boxes), 18+ line, no marketing fluff |
| `/:code` | Join | name field (14 max), avatar picker (16 devils/sinners), [ENTER HELL], 18+ attest checkbox-as-button, error states: ROOM_FULL→"HELL IS FULL (become an Imp?)", bad code→"NO SUCH PIT" |
| `/:code` in-room | Lobby | roster with avatars + connection dots, room code HUGE + QR (client-side generated), depth picker (host: QUICK DIP/STANDARD/FULL DAMNATION with duration + N-gate), vibe check (host: SOBER/WARMED UP/FERAL), private heat-ceiling picker (EVERY player, 1–5 flame dial, "how hot can cards about YOU run?" — value never shown to others), [BEGIN THE DESCENT] (host; disabled until ≥3 players + all ceilings + entitlement; shows paywall if 2nd+ night unpaid) |
| 〃 | Circle intro | circle number as descent depth art, game title, 15s/5s explainer |
| 〃 | Game screens | per Part 5, one component per (game × role × phase) |
| 〃 | Reveal | full-bleed flash → payload ≤2s glanceable, fire button (hold-to-spam), [DESCEND] per rules |
| 〃 | Ladder | 5s rank strip, biggest mover highlighted |
| 〃 | Judgment | winner crown, superlatives (staggered reveals, 2s each), SHARE CARD (canvas-rendered PNG: night stats + wordmark + "host your own descent" link), [DESCEND AGAIN] |
| 〃 | Reconnect | "CRAWLING BACK…" overlay, auto (no tap), shows current phase on success |
| any | Imp overlay | "YOU'RE AN IMP — feed on their sins" + rights explainer, conversion toast |

## 6.2 Design language (binding, from repo CLAUDE.md)

- Palette: near-black `#0B0A0C` base, hellfire spectrum (deep red `#8E1B1B` → ember orange `#E2571B`) for action/heat, bone `#E8E2D6` for text. ONE accent per screen. No gradients-as-decoration: fire gradient appears ONLY on heat/fire elements (it signals heat).
- Type: display = heavy condensed grotesque (self-hosted, subset); body = system stack. Numbers get the display face (reveals are numbers).
- Motion: reveals SNAP (≤120ms in), holds breathe (slow ember pulse). Nothing eases politely; nothing bounces.
- Every screen passes: "could this be a corporate quiz app screen?" → if yes, redesign. No card-grid-with-icons layouts. No rounded-xl-gray anything.
- All sync moments visual-first: full-screen flash + max-brightness pulse; audio garnish from host phone only.

## 6.3 Client hard requirements

- Wake Lock API on join (re-acquire on visibilitychange); audio unlock on first tap (host).
- Reconnect: exponential backoff 0.5→8s, RESYNC on visible, epoch discipline (3.5).
- Text inputs: keyboard-safe layout (timer pinned visible above keyboard — tested on iOS Safari).
- Bundle ≤200KB gzip; joins on bar LTE in <2s (Lighthouse task D-124).
- QR: client-generated (no external service), dark-bar tested (max contrast, quiet zone).

---

# PART 7 — ANDROID SHELL (phase M4)

- Single Activity + WebView (`androidx.webkit`), loads prod client URL with `?shell=1`.
- JS bridge (`window.HDShell`): `getEntitlement(): 'owned'|'none'`, `buyHostUnlock(): Promise<'owned'|'cancelled'>` (Play Billing product `descent_host_unlock`, one-time, $9.99), `setStageAudio(on)`, `onLift(cb)` (accelerometer: gravity-vector low-pass, >50° sustained 250ms = lifted; <40° = flat, asymmetric hysteresis), `keepAwake(on)` (FLAG_KEEP_SCREEN_ON), `sting(name)` (foreground-service audio for stage mode).
- Client detects `?shell=1` → prefers `HDShell` entitlement + lift events; otherwise web equivalents (manual stage-flip button).
- versionCode restart at 20000; applicationId stays `com.helldeck` (upgrade path over old app); the old game is NOT bundled.
- Play listing: Mature 17+, Data Safety per Part 10.4.

<!-- APPEND:PART8 -->

- Acceptance: truth-lock value never in any broadcast frame before phase 5; pick-of-3 never reveals the 2 unchosen cards to the room (they return to the pool).

## 5.5 SCATTERBLAST (`games/scatter.ts`) — minN 3 · best-of-3 bombs

| # | Phase | Dur | Screens |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEAL | 5.5 | All: CATEGORY + LETTER, huge type |
| 2 | BOMB (verbal circle) | hidden 15–45 | Stage/volunteer phone: ticking bomb face (audio+pulse). All others: category + letter + subtle pulse (visual-first; bar-proof) |
| 3 | BOOM | 5 | ALL phones: full-screen flash "WHO DIED?" + avatar grid tap |
| 4 | REVEAL | hold | All: loser's name + "THE TABLE HAS SPOKEN" (majority tap) |

- Fuse: uniform random 15–45s, biased +5s per bomb number (bomb 3 longest). Turn passing is verbal — the app never tracks turns.
- Acceptance: boom flash lands on all phones ≤150ms skew p95; WHO DIED majority resolves ties by earliest-tap-timestamp median; category+letter re-render instantly on rejoin.

## 5.6 POISON PITCH (`games/poison.ts`) — minN 3 · 2 duels/circle

| # | Phase | Dur | Screens |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | ASSIGN (private, volunteer-first) | 10 | 5s "WHO WANTS BLOOD?" ×2 → two pitchers get PRIVATE side assignment + [BURN spotlight] |
| 2 | DEAL | 5.5 | All: both options rendered as a fight card ("A vs B") + pitcher names |
| 3 | PITCH A → PITCH B (verbal) | 30+30, [REST] | Pitcher: own option + angle hints ("workaround / silver lining / attack") + [REST]. All: fight card + bell timer + fire + [SKIP-'EM] |
| 4 | VOTE (skippable) | 12 | Non-pitchers: A / B (which PITCH won, not which option) |
| 5 | REVEAL | hold | All: winner banner + vote split |

- Tie: 10s sudden-death rebuttal each → revote → still tied = split pot (+2 each).
- N=3 variant (auto): single judge rates each pitch 1–5 damage meter; pitchers pre-predict own rating (exact → +2).
- Acceptance: sides never leak before phase 2; SKIP-'EM re-deals the CARD only (same pitchers, new dilemma, once per circle).

## 5.7 RED FLAG RALLY (`games/redflag.ts`) — minN 3 · 2 defenses/circle

Phases: ASSIGN (volunteer-first, private, burnable) → DEAL (perk+flag fight card to all) → DEFENSE (45s verbal, [REST], [SKIP-'EM]) → VOTE SMASH/PASS (12s, skippable) → REVEAL (hold).
- Tie → SMASH (performer wins ties). Card law: display text is the ONLY joke source (one card = one joke; build lint rejects field mismatch).
- Acceptance: defender assignment respects spotlightCount weighting; verdict stamp animation ≤2s glanceable.

## 5.8 ALIBI DROP (`games/alibi.ts`) — minN 3 · 2 accused/circle

| # | Phase | Dur | Screens |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | ASSIGN + words (private) | 12 | Accused: accusation + 3 mandatory words + [BURN]. Others: "DEALING…" |
| 2 | ACCUSATION | 5 | All: the accusation, big |
| 3 | ALIBI (verbal) | 30, [REST] | Accused: words pinned top of screen. Others: accusation + "hunt the plants" + fire |
| 4 | WORD HUNT (skippable) | 20 | Jury: 8-word lineup (3 plants + 5 decoys from the card), pick exactly 3 |
| 5 | REVEAL | hold | All: stage-sequenced — each word flips PLANTED/DECOY one at a time with sting (1.2s apart), then scores |

- Acceptance: lineup order randomized per phone; accused's words NEVER in broadcast frames before phase 5; reveal sequence is server-timed beats (AT per word), not a text dump.

## 5.9 Content Drop 1 games

Text Thread Trap, Reality Check, Taboo Timer, Hot Seat Imposter ship in Drop 1 (tracker D-7xx) with the same spec discipline; their phase tables are derived from HDRealRules2 Part III §9–12 and reviewed at Drop-1 kickoff. Not in v1 scope. Title Fight ships at launch as arc spike: ASSIGN two fighters → card names the duel verb → 30s bout (all phones = crowd meter) → point-at-winner vote.

# PART 6 — WEB CLIENT

## 6.1 Routes & screens (complete inventory)

| Route | Screen | Elements (exhaustive) |
|---|---|---|
| `/` | Landing/Host | wordmark, "START A NIGHT" (creates room → `/HOST` view), "JOIN A NIGHT" (code input, 4 uppercase letter boxes), 18+ line, no marketing fluff |
| `/:code` | Join | name field (14 max), avatar picker (16 devils/sinners), [ENTER HELL], 18+ attest checkbox-as-button, error states: ROOM_FULL→"HELL IS FULL (become an Imp?)", bad code→"NO SUCH PIT" |
| `/:code` in-room | Lobby | roster with avatars + connection dots, room code HUGE + QR (client-side generated), depth picker (host: QUICK DIP/STANDARD/FULL DAMNATION with duration + N-gate), vibe check (host: SOBER/WARMED UP/FERAL), private heat-ceiling picker (EVERY player, 1–5 flame dial, "how hot can cards about YOU run?" — value never shown to others), [BEGIN THE DESCENT] (host; disabled until ≥3 players + all ceilings + entitlement; shows paywall if 2nd+ night unpaid) |
| 〃 | Circle intro | circle number as descent depth art, game title, 15s/5s explainer |
| 〃 | Game screens | per Part 5, one component per (game × role × phase) |
| 〃 | Reveal | full-bleed flash → payload ≤2s glanceable, fire button (hold-to-spam), [DESCEND] per rules |
| 〃 | Ladder | 5s rank strip, biggest mover highlighted |
| 〃 | Judgment | winner crown, superlatives (staggered reveals, 2s each), SHARE CARD (canvas-rendered PNG: night stats + wordmark + "host your own descent" link), [DESCEND AGAIN] |
| 〃 | Reconnect | "CRAWLING BACK…" overlay, auto (no tap), shows current phase on success |
| any | Imp overlay | "YOU'RE AN IMP — feed on their sins" + rights explainer, conversion toast |

## 6.2 Design language (binding, from repo CLAUDE.md)

- Palette: near-black `#0B0A0C` base, hellfire spectrum (deep red `#8E1B1B` → ember orange `#E2571B`) for action/heat, bone `#E8E2D6` for text. ONE accent per screen. No gradients-as-decoration: fire gradient appears ONLY on heat/fire elements (it signals heat).
- Type: display = heavy condensed grotesque (self-hosted, subset); body = system stack. Numbers get the display face (reveals are numbers).
- Motion: reveals SNAP (≤120ms in), holds breathe (slow ember pulse). Nothing eases politely; nothing bounces.
- Every screen passes: "could this be a corporate quiz app screen?" → if yes, redesign. No card-grid-with-icons layouts. No rounded-xl-gray anything.
- All sync moments visual-first: full-screen flash + max-brightness pulse; audio garnish from host phone only.

## 6.3 Client hard requirements

- Wake Lock API on join (re-acquire on visibilitychange); audio unlock on first tap (host).
- Reconnect: exponential backoff 0.5→8s, RESYNC on visible, epoch discipline (3.5).
- Text inputs: keyboard-safe layout (timer pinned visible above keyboard — tested on iOS Safari).
- Bundle ≤200KB gzip; joins on bar LTE in <2s (Lighthouse task D-124).
- QR: client-generated (no external service), dark-bar tested (max contrast, quiet zone).

- Single Activity + WebView (`androidx.webkit`), loads prod client URL with `?shell=1`.
- JS bridge (`window.HDShell`): `getEntitlement(): 'owned'|'none'`, `buyHostUnlock(): Promise<'owned'|'cancelled'>` (Play Billing product `descent_host_unlock`, one-time, $9.99), `setStageAudio(on)`, `onLift(cb)` (accelerometer: gravity-vector low-pass, >50° sustained 250ms = lifted; <40° = flat, asymmetric hysteresis), `keepAwake(on)` (FLAG_KEEP_SCREEN_ON), `sting(name)` (foreground-service audio for stage mode).
- Client detects `?shell=1` → prefers `HDShell` entitlement + lift events; otherwise web equivalents (manual stage-flip button).
- versionCode restart at 20000; applicationId stays `com.helldeck` (upgrade path over old app); the old game is NOT bundled.
- Play listing: Mature 17+, Data Safety per Part 10.4.
